quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.A.1 · The original bridge — Liu et al. 2011, Journal of Immunology
quizzes.stem: Liu et al. 2011 proposed that IFIT3 amplifies RIG-I-pathway IFN-β signaling by physically bridging MAVS and TBK1 on the mitochondrial outer membrane. Which experimental result most directly established that the MAVS-binding surface of IFIT3—rather than its broader antiviral effector activity—is required for this amplification?
choices: A. IFIT3 knockdown reduced VSV titers in IFN-α-pretreated cells but had no measurable effect on IRF3 phosphorylation after Sendai virus stimulation, indicating that IFIT3 functions as a downstream antiviral effector rather than at the MAVS–TBK1 signaling step.

B. An IFIT3 point mutant (E164A/E165A) that retained TBK1 binding but lost co-immunoprecipitation with MAVS also failed to enhance IRF3-driven luciferase reporter activity upon overexpression, demonstrating that engagement of the MAVS-binding TPR surface is required for the scaffolding function.

C. IFIT3 co-purified with IFIT1 and IFIT2 to form a heterotrimer that sequestered viral 5′-triphosphate RNA, preventing RIG-I re-engagement and constituting the dominant antiviral mechanism in Sendai-virus-infected HEK293 cells.

D. Overexpression of IFIT3 enhanced IRF3 phosphorylation in MAVS-knockdown cells reconstituted with a cytosolic, C-terminally truncated MAVS variant, demonstrating that IFIT3-mediated TBK1 recruitment does not require MAVS membrane anchoring.
quizzes.explanation: The E164A/E165A separation-of-function mutant is the load-bearing experiment: it decouples MAVS binding from TBK1 binding and shows that losing the MAVS contact alone—while preserving the N-terminal TBK1 contact at K38—is sufficient to abolish IFN-β amplification even at high overexpression, proving that simultaneous engagement of both scaffold partners is mechanistically required. This directly refutes models in which TBK1 recruitment alone, or a non-scaffolding effector activity such as PPP-RNA sequestration, accounts for the amplifier phenotype.
quizzes.citation: Liu et al. J Immunol. 2011;187(6). PMID: 21813773. DOI: 10.4049/jimmunol.1100963.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.A.2 · Structural reinterpretation — Johnson et al. 2018, Immunity
quizzes.stem: Johnson et al. (2018, Immunity) resolved the crystal structure of human IFIT1 in ternary complex with cap-0 RNA and the C-terminal domain of IFIT3. Based on that structural and biochemical analysis, how does IFIT3 mechanistically alter IFIT1 RNA recognition?
choices: A. The IFIT3 C-terminal domain docks onto a surface of IFIT1 distal to the RNA-binding cavity and allosterically reshapes that cavity to favor cap-0 RNA while disfavoring cap-1 and 5′-ppp ligands.

B. The IFIT3 C-terminal domain inserts a positively charged loop directly into IFIT1's cap-binding pocket, sterically excluding 2′-O-methylated cap-1 ligands while leaving m7G-coordinating residues structurally unchanged.

C. The IFIT3 N-terminal MAVS-bridging domain is the primary structural interface with IFIT1; it activates IFIT1 RNA selectivity by recruiting TBK1, which phosphorylates a serine within the IFIT1 cap-binding groove.
D. IFIT3 increases IFIT1 antiviral specificity solely by masking a CRL4-recognized degron on IFIT1, extending its half-life; no allosteric reshaping of the RNA-binding cavity was detected.
quizzes.explanation: The Johnson et al. structure showed that the IFIT3 CTD engages IFIT1 at a face remote from the RNA-binding cavity, yet HDX-MS confirmed this distal contact propagates an allosteric rearrangement that tightens cap-0 recognition and disfavors cap-1 and 5′-ppp RNA — establishing IFIT3 as an allosteric co-factor, not a passive scaffold. The cycloheximide chase data showing extended IFIT1 half-life is a second, separable finding; it does not explain the shift in RNA selectivity.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.A.3 · The original eIF3 inhibitor — Terenzi et al. 2006, Journal o
quizzes.stem: Terenzi et al. 2006 used co-immunoprecipitation of eIF3 subunits and sucrose-gradient 48S assembly assays to compare ISG54/IFIT2 and ISG56/IFIT1 in HEK293 cells and cell-free translation extracts. What was their central mechanistic finding distinguishing the two proteins?
choices: A. ISG54/IFIT2 contacts both eIF3-e and eIF3-c, blocking both ternary-complex delivery to the 40S ribosome and 48S pre-initiation complex assembly, whereas ISG56/IFIT1 contacts only eIF3-e and inhibits only ternary-complex delivery.

B. ISG56/IFIT1 contacts both eIF3-e and eIF3-c, blocking both ternary-complex delivery and 48S pre-initiation complex assembly, whereas ISG54/IFIT2 contacts only eIF3-e and inhibits only ternary-complex delivery.

C. ISG54/IFIT2 requires IFIT3 as an obligate scaffold to stably contact eIF3-e, and in the absence of IFIT3 cannot inhibit 48S complex formation, whereas ISG56/IFIT1 inhibits ternary-complex delivery independently as a monomer.

D. ISG54/IFIT2 and ISG56/IFIT1 contact the same eIF3 subunits but differ kinetically: IFIT2 is induced rapidly and transiently after IFN-β to block translation acutely, while IFIT1 accumulates more slowly to sustain long-term suppression.
quizzes.explanation: Terenzi et al. showed by co-IP that IFIT2 engages both eIF3-e and eIF3-c while IFIT1 contacts only eIF3-e; sucrose-gradient fractionation confirmed that IFIT2 therefore disrupts two mechanistically distinct steps — delivery of the eIF2·GTP·Met-tRNAi ternary complex and assembly of the 48S pre-initiation complex — whereas IFIT1 blocks only the former. This dual eIF3 contact is the direct biochemical precedent underlying Fleith 2018's conclusion that the IFIT1/IFIT2/IFIT3 heterotrimer is a more potent translational repressor than any monomer alone.
quizzes.citation: Terenzi F, Hui DJ, Merrick WC, Sen GC. Distinct induction patterns and functions of two closely related interferon-inducible human genes, ISG54 and ISG56. J Biol Chem. 2006;281(45):34064-34071. PMID: 16973618.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.B.1 · IFIT1 binds 5′-triphosphate RNA and the Ifit1 knockout mouse
quizzes.stem: In Pichlmair et al. 2011, Ifit1−/− C57BL/6 mice were infected with VSV and influenza A to test IFIT1's antiviral function in vivo. Which result most directly demonstrated the biological specificity of IFIT1-mediated defense?
choices: A. Ifit1−/− mice accumulated significantly higher viral titers and more severe pathology after infection with VSV and influenza A, but controlled non-PPP-RNA pathogens as effectively as wild-type animals, directly coupling the in vivo phenotype to IFIT1's PPP-RNA recognition specificity.

B. Ifit2−/− mice, but not Ifit1−/− mice, accumulated elevated VSV titers in the CNS, establishing IFIT2 as the dominant in vivo effector of PPP-RNA sequestration and leaving IFIT1's non-redundant antiviral role uncertain.

C. Ifit1−/− mice showed equivalently elevated titers across all cytosolic RNA viruses tested regardless of 5′-cap status, indicating that IFIT1 provides broad antiviral protection through a mechanism independent of PPP-RNA specificity.

D. Ifit1−/− mice controlled VSV normally but showed elevated replication of 2′-O-methylation-deficient flavivirus mutants, indicating that cap-0 discrimination rather than 5′-triphosphate sequestration is IFIT1's primary in vivo antiviral function.
quizzes.explanation: Pichlmair et al. showed that Ifit1−/− mice accumulated markedly higher titers and developed more severe pathology after VSV and influenza A infection — both PPP-RNA-generating viruses — while controlling non-PPP-RNA pathogens equivalently to wild-type animals. This virus-class-selective vulnerability directly coupled the in vivo phenotype to IFIT1's biochemically defined PPP-RNA-binding activity (nanomolar Kd, R187-dependent C-terminal groove), establishing IFIT1 as a de facto pattern-recognition effector rather than a broadly antiviral or redundant ISG.
quizzes.citation: Pichlmair A, Lassnig C, Eberle CA, et al. IFIT1 is an antiviral protein that recognizes 5′-triphosphate RNA. Nat Immunol. 2011;12(7):624–630. PMID: 21642987. DOI: 10.1038/ni.2048.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.B.2 · 2′-O methylation as host-self discrimination — Daffis et al.
quizzes.stem: In Daffis et al. (2010), a WNV mutant (E218A) lacking viral 2′-O methyltransferase activity produced cap-0 mRNA and was attenuated in wild-type mice. Which experimental result most directly established IFIT1—rather than another innate immune component—as the host effector that discriminates cap-0 viral RNA from host cap-1 mRNA?
choices: A. WNV-E218A was attenuated in wild-type mice but caused lethal encephalitis in Ifit1−/− mice, with brain viral titers matching those of parental cap-1 WNV in wild-type animals.
B. WNV-E218A attenuation was abolished in Mavs−/− mice but retained in Ifit1−/− mice, placing mitochondrial RIG-I/MDA5 signaling upstream of IFIT1 as the principal restriction mechanism.
C. Ifit2−/− mice were as permissive to WNV-E218A as Ifit1−/− mice, establishing IFIT2 rather than IFIT1 as the cap-0-recognition subunit within the IFIT heterotrimer.
D. Wild-type fibroblasts phosphorylated eIF2α in response to WNV-E218A but not parental WNV, linking cap-0 restriction to the PKR-dependent integrated stress response independently of IFIT1.
quizzes.explanation: Rescue of WNV-E218A pathogenicity specifically in Ifit1−/− mice identified IFIT1 as the direct effector that sequesters cap-0 RNA; parental cap-1 WNV caused equivalent lethal disease regardless of Ifit1 status, confirming that 2′-O methylation is the molecular self-tag that protects host mRNA from IFIT1-mediated restriction. The parallel Ifnar1−/− result placed IFIT1 downstream of type I IFN induction, distinguishing it as the terminal cap-0 reader rather than an upstream signaling node.
quizzes.citation: Daffis S, Szretter KJ, Schriewer J, Li J, Youn S, Errett J, Lin TY, Schneller S, Zust R, Dong H, Thiel V, Sen GC, Fensterl V, Klimstra WB, Pierson TC, Buller RM, Gale M Jr, Shi PY, Diamond MS. 2′-O methylation of the viral mRNA cap evades host restriction by IFIT family members. Nature. 2010;468(7322):452–456. PMID: 21085181

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.B.3 · IFIT5 crystal with PPP-RNA — Abbas et al. 2013, Nature
quizzes.stem: Abbas et al. (2013) solved the crystal structure of human IFIT5 bound to 5′-PPP-RNA. Which structural feature best accounts for IFIT5's selectivity for single-stranded PPP-RNA over double-stranded RNA substrates?
choices: A. Nine TPR-like helical hairpins fold into a deep, positively charged central cavity whose narrow geometry sterically excludes dsRNA, while lysine and arginine residues lining the interior directly coordinate the 5′-triphosphate.

B. A helicase-like insertion domain within IFIT5 unwinds short dsRNA duplexes to expose the 5′-triphosphate, enabling recognition of both ssRNA and partially unwound dsRNA in a RIG-I-independent manner.
C. Obligate heterodimerization of IFIT5 with IFIT3 remodels a shallow surface groove into a composite cleft that selectively accommodates dsRNA ends but not single-stranded overhangs.

D. The 5′-triphosphate and cap-0 moieties are coordinated by the same basic-residue pocket, so IFIT5 toggles between PPP-RNA and capped-RNA recognition through minor side-chain rearrangements without cavity remodeling.
quizzes.explanation: Abbas et al. demonstrated that IFIT5's nine TPR-like helical hairpins assemble into a positively charged clamshell cavity whose interior dimensions physically exclude dsRNA while Lys/Arg residues on the cavity floor directly coordinate the 5′-triphosphate; mutagenesis of these cavity residues abolished PPP-RNA binding in EMSA and abrogated antiviral activity in HEK293 cells, confirming that the cavity geometry — not heterotrimerization or a helicase domain — is the structural determinant of RNA selectivity.
quizzes.citation: Abbas et al. (2013) 'Structural basis for viral 5′-PPP-RNA recognition by human IFIT proteins.' Nature 494:60–64. PMID 23334420.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.B.4 · Human IFIT1 with cap-0 RNA — Abbas et al. 2017, PNAS
quizzes.stem: The 1.6 Å co-crystal structure of human IFIT1 bound to cap-0 RNA provided the atomic basis for IFIT1's discrimination between viral cap-0 and host cap-1 transcripts. Which mechanism best explains why 2′-O methylation at the N1 position converts a high-affinity IFIT1 substrate into a non-substrate?
choices: The N1 2′-O-methyl group sterically clashes with the floor of IFIT1's RNA-binding tunnel, physically blocking productive accommodation of cap-1 RNA.
The N1 2′-O-methyl group disrupts water-mediated contacts that allow the m7G cap to adopt multiple conformations within the hydrophobic lobe, preventing stable engagement.
Electrostatic repulsion between the N1 2′-O-methyl group and positively charged residues lining the RNA tunnel reduces cap-1 binding affinity.
The N1 2′-O-methyl group shifts cap-binding equilibrium toward eIF4E, which outcompetes IFIT1 for methylated 5′ ends in host transcripts.
quizzes.explanation: The crystal structure showed that the N1 2′-O-methyl group directly collides with the floor of IFIT1's positively charged RNA tunnel—a geometry absent in the cap-0 complex—providing the first atomic-resolution explanation for the cap-0/cap-1 discrimination originally demonstrated functionally by Daffis et al. (2010). Structure-guided point mutants that disrupted this tunnel region abrogated restriction of coronavirus 2′-O MTase mutants in 293T cells, confirming the structural model in a cellular context.
quizzes.citation: Abbas et al. (2017) Proc Natl Acad Sci USA 114(11):2987–2992. PMID: 28251928. DOI: 10.1073/pnas.1612444114

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.B.5 · IFIT5 as a broad cellular RNA binder — Katibah et al. 2014,
quizzes.stem: Katibah et al. (2014) used TGIRT-seq to profile the endogenous RNA-binding targets of IFIT5 in HEK293 cells. What was the principal unexpected finding from this approach?
choices: IFIT5 co-purified with precursor and processed tRNAs and other RNA polymerase III transcripts, identifying endogenous non-viral ligands that bear 5′-triphosphate ends
IFIT5 bound exclusively to 5′-triphosphate RNAs in cells, mirroring the strict PPP-RNA specificity previously characterized for IFIT1 in structural studies
IFIT5's cellular RNA association required co-expression of IFIT1, with IFIT5-only pulldowns yielding no enriched endogenous transcripts
IFIT5 co-purified with cap-1-modified mRNAs in cell lysates, suggesting that cap-1 exclusion observed in vitro is overcome at physiological protein concentrations
quizzes.explanation: TGIRT-seq of IFIT5 immunoprecipitates revealed association with precursor and mature tRNAs and other pol III transcripts — endogenous RNAs that naturally carry 5′-PPP ends from pol III transcription. This repositions IFIT5 as a broader cellular RNA-binding protein, demonstrating that its antiviral PPP-RNA recognition is one activity among several rather than a strictly virus-sensing function.
quizzes.citation: Katibah GE et al. (2014) Broad and adaptable RNA structure recognition by the human interferon-induced tetratricopeptide repeat protein IFIT5. PNAS 111(33):12025-12030. PMID 25092312.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.C.1 · Ifit2−/− mice and lethal VSV neuropathogenesis — Fensterl et
quizzes.stem: Fensterl et al. 2012 used Ifit2−/− and Ifit1−/− C57BL/6 mice challenged with VSV via intranasal versus intracranial routes. Which experimental result most directly established that IFIT2 restricts VSV at an early, route-dependent step rather than at the level of neuronal replication?
choices: A. Intranasal VSV was uniformly lethal in Ifit2−/− mice with accompanying neurological signs, whereas Ifit1−/− mice were largely protected by either route, and intracranial inoculation abolished the Ifit2−/− mortality phenotype.

B. Ifit1−/− mice showed selective lethality after intranasal VSV challenge while Ifit2−/− mice survived comparably to wild-type, implicating IFIT1's PPP-RNA cap-0 sensing activity in olfactory-route restriction.

C. Ifit2−/− mice exhibited several hundred-fold elevated brain titers of both VSV and EMCV after intranasal challenge, demonstrating that IFIT2 provides broad, non-selective protection against neurotropic RNA viruses.

D. Intracranial VSV produced higher brain titers in Ifit2−/− mice than in wild-type, and viral tropism expanded from neurons into astrocytes, indicating IFIT2 controls both spread efficiency and CNS cell-type selectivity.
quizzes.explanation: The route-comparison design is the load-bearing experiment: intranasal challenge (which requires the virus to spread from the olfactory epithelium through the CNS) was lethal in Ifit2−/− but not Ifit1−/− mice, yet switching to intracranial delivery — bypassing the olfactory entry step — eliminated the survival difference. This dissociation localizes IFIT2's protective function to early olfactory-route spread rather than to replication within neurons. EMCV was not enhanced in Ifit2−/− brains, further demonstrating virus-specific (not generalized neurotropic-virus) restriction.
quizzes.citation: Fensterl V, Wetzel JL, Ramachandran S, Ogino T, Stohlman SA, Bergmann CC, Diamond MS, Virgin HW, Sen GC. Interferon-induced Ifit2/ISG54 protects mice from lethal VSV neuropathogenesis. PLoS Pathog. 2012;8(5):e1002712. PMID: 22615570.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.D.1 · RIG-G / IFIT3 forces G1/S arrest via p21 and p27 in APL — Xi
quizzes.stem: In Xiao et al. 2006, ectopic expression of RIG-G/IFIT3 in U937 myeloid cells produced G1/S accumulation. Co-immunoprecipitation identified which mechanism as the primary driver of p27 stabilization?
choices: A. IFIT3 bound JAB1, blocking JAB1-dependent ubiquitin-proteasome-mediated degradation of p27, thereby elevating p27 protein and arresting cells at G1/S
B. IFIT3 engaged CDK4–cyclin D1 complexes through its TPR repeats, preventing Rb phosphorylation and S-phase entry independent of CKI induction
C. IFIT3 recruited CRL4-DCAF2 to ubiquitinate cyclin E, reducing CDK2 activity and stalling cells at the G1/S transition
D. IFIT3 sequestered eIF4E away from the 5′ cap of cyclin D1 mRNA, suppressing cap-dependent translation and lowering CDK4 activity
quizzes.explanation: Xiao et al. demonstrated by co-IP that RIG-G/IFIT3 physically associates with JAB1, the co-factor required for proteasomal targeting of p27; JAB1 sequestration stabilizes p27 protein, driving G1/S arrest. A secondary arm — IFIT3-mediated c-myc repression leading to p21 induction — was also shown, but the JAB1/p27 axis was the novel mechanistic finding validated by co-immunoprecipitation.
quizzes.citation: Xiao et al., PNAS 2006, PMID 17050680 — RIG-G/IFIT3 forces G1/S arrest via p27 stabilization (JAB1 sequestration) and p21 induction (c-myc repression) in U937 myeloid cells

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.C.3 · The canonical IFIT review — Fensterl & Sen 2015, Journal of
quizzes.stem: Fensterl & Sen (2015, J Virol) synthesize the Ifit knockout mouse literature and identify a key species-level caveat for interpreting those phenotypes. Which statement best captures that caveat?
choices: A. Human IFIT1, IFIT2, and IFIT3 form stable heterocomplexes, whereas not all mouse Ifit paralogs assemble equivalent complexes, so knockout phenotypes in mice may not fully reflect the stoichiometry of IFIT activity in human cells.
B. Mouse Ifit1 preferentially binds m7G-capped viral RNA rather than 5′-triphosphate RNA, explaining why Ifit1-knockout mice show unexpectedly mild susceptibility to negative-sense RNA viruses.

C. Ifit2-knockout mice develop spontaneous type I IFN-driven autoimmunity, establishing Ifit2 as a negative regulator of basal interferon signaling in lymphoid tissue rather than a direct antiviral effector.
D. The primate-specific paralog IFIT1B is expressed from the mouse Ifit locus and fully compensates for Ifit1 loss in knockout models, masking antiviral phenotypes that would otherwise be observed.
quizzes.explanation: Fensterl & Sen explicitly note that human IFIT1/IFIT2/IFIT3 assemble into heterocomplexes but that not all mouse Ifit paralogs form equivalent complexes — a species difference that limits direct extrapolation of mouse knockout phenotypes to human IFIT biology. The other choices invert mouse Ifit1's established PPP-RNA (not cap) binding preference, fabricate an autoimmunity phenotype not described for Ifit2 knockouts, and misplace IFIT1B (a primate-specific gene) into the mouse genome.
quizzes.citation: Fensterl V, Sen GC. Interferon-induced Ifit proteins: their role in viral pathogenesis. J Virol. 2015;89(5):2462–8. PMID 25428874. DOI 10.1128/JVI.02744-14.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.C.2 · Ifit2−/− mice and coronavirus encephalitis via impaired macr
quizzes.stem: Butchi et al. (2013) infected Ifit2−/− mice with MHV-A59 and found higher brain viral titers and elevated mortality. When bone-marrow-derived macrophages (BMDMs) from these mice were examined ex vivo, what was the mechanistically decisive finding?
choices: Ifit2−/− BMDMs produced significantly less IFN-α/β mRNA and protein after MHV infection, establishing Ifit2 as an upstream amplifier of type I IFN induction in macrophages rather than a direct intracellular antiviral effector.

Ifit2−/− BMDMs failed to sequester viral 5′-triphosphate RNA in cytoplasmic complexes, demonstrating that Ifit2 functions analogously to Ifit1 as a direct cytoplasmic sensor that restricts translation of incoming coronavirus transcripts.
Ifit2−/− BMDMs were unable to scaffold IFIT3 onto the MAVS signalosome at mitochondria-associated membranes, preventing IRF3 phosphorylation and blocking downstream ISG transcription.

Ifit2−/− BMDMs showed selectively impaired TNF-α and IL-6 induction following MHV challenge while IFN-α/β production remained intact, positioning Ifit2 within the NF-κB rather than the IRF3/IFN amplification axis.
quizzes.explanation: Butchi et al. showed that Ifit2−/− BMDMs produced markedly less IFN-α/β mRNA and protein after ex vivo MHV infection, while pro-inflammatory cytokines were only modestly reduced — demonstrating that Ifit2's primary in vivo role is to amplify type I IFN production in macrophages rather than to restrict viral replication directly. This reframes Ifit2 as a positive regulator of the IFN axis, an upstream function conceptually parallel to IFIT3's MAVS-bridging role described by Liu et al. 2011 but operating in a different paralog, cell type, and viral context.
quizzes.citation: Butchi et al. (2013). Ifit2 deficiency results in uncontrolled neurotropic coronavirus replication and enhanced encephalitis via impaired alpha/beta interferon induction in macrophages. J Virol. 87(3):1326–37. PMID 24198415.

quizzes.stem: Which best explains why myeloma plasma cells are selectively vulnerable to proteasome inhibition by bortezomib compared with most normal somatic cells?
choices: Myeloma cells overexpress the β5 catalytic subunit of the 26S proteasome, increasing their intrinsic dependence on the targeted enzyme
The massive immunoglobulin secretory burden in myeloma plasma cells chronically saturates UPS capacity, leaving no reserve to tolerate further proteasome inhibition
Bortezomib preferentially accumulates in myeloma cells through upregulated peptide transporters expressed on their surface
Myeloma cells lack functional autophagy, forcing complete reliance on the UPS for clearance of misfolded secretory proteins
quizzes.explanation: Plasma cells engaged in constitutive high-level immunoglobulin synthesis operate chronically near proteotoxic capacity; β5 inhibition by bortezomib removes the residual UPS reserve and tips them into terminal UPR with IRE1/CHOP-driven apoptosis. Normal cells retain sufficient UPS reserve to tolerate partial proteasome inhibition without triggering irreversible ER stress.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · General §proteotoxic stress / UPS reserve

quizzes.stem: Which statement correctly describes the catalytic mechanism of RBR E3 ubiquitin ligases such as Parkin, HOIP, and ARIH1?
choices: RING1 positions the charged E2~Ub conjugate for direct substrate attack, while RING2 provides an accessory ubiquitin-binding scaffold that enhances processivity without forming a thioester intermediate.
RING1 recruits E2~Ub as a scaffold (RING-like), then ubiquitin is relayed onto a catalytic cysteine in RING2 (HECT-like) before final transfer to the substrate lysine.
RING2 recruits E2~Ub analogously to a canonical RING domain, while RING1 harbors the catalytic cysteine that forms the obligate E3~Ub thioester intermediate.
Both RING1 and RING2 independently accept ubiquitin onto catalytic cysteines, forming two sequential E3~Ub thioester intermediates before substrate transfer.
quizzes.explanation: RBR ligases are hybrids: RING1 engages the E2~Ub conjugate in a closed, RING-like conformation but does not permit direct substrate attack; instead, ubiquitin is relayed onto a catalytic Cys in the RING2 (also called RBR-C) domain, forming a HECT-like thioester before transfer to the substrate lysine. This obligate two-step mechanism distinguishes RBRs from pure RING E3s, which use a one-step transfer, and from pure HECT E3s, which lack a RING1-mediated E2-recruitment step.

quizzes.stem: Which statement best describes the immunoproteasome and explains why its formation is mechanistically relevant to IFN signaling?
choices: A. IFN-γ induces replacement of constitutive catalytic subunits β1, β2, and β5 with immunosubunits β1i (PSMB9), β2i (PSMB10), and β5i (PSMB8), shifting cleavage specificity toward hydrophobic and basic C-termini to optimize peptide loading onto MHC class I.

B. IFN-γ displaces the 19S regulatory caps of the 26S proteasome with PA28αβ activators, increasing peptide release rate without altering catalytic subunit identity or cleavage specificity, thereby boosting antigen presentation flux.

C. Type I IFNs (IFN-α/β) are the primary inducers of immunosubunit incorporation into the 20S core, while IFN-γ acts downstream by upregulating TAP1/TAP2 to transport the resulting peptides into the ER for MHC class I loading.

D. IFN-γ transcriptionally upregulates all seven β-subunits of the constitutive 20S proteasome, increasing overall proteolytic capacity and broadening the peptide repertoire available for MHC class I presentation.
quizzes.explanation: IFN-γ specifically induces the immunosubunits PSMB9, PSMB10, and PSMB8, which replace their constitutive counterparts within the 20S catalytic barrel; the resulting shift in cleavage preference toward hydrophobic and basic C-termini directly enriches the peptide pool for MHC class I grooves. This makes IFN signaling a direct upstream remodeler of the ubiquitin–proteasome system, bridging innate immune cytokine output to adaptive antigen presentation.

quizzes.stem: The distinct cellular fates of K48-linked versus K63-linked polyubiquitin chains arise primarily because
choices: compact K48 chains and extended K63 chains differentially expose the I44 hydrophobic patch, recruiting linkage-selective UBDs such as Rpn10/Rpn13 or TAB2-NZF, respectively
the proteasome's 19S regulatory particle harbors a DUB that actively disassembles K63-linked chains before substrate translocation, redirecting them toward signaling pathways
K48 chains reach the minimum four-ubiquitin threshold for proteasomal recognition more rapidly due to faster processivity of K48-specific E2 conjugating enzymes
the isopeptide bond geometry at K48 versus K63 directly and allosterically activates distinct downstream kinase cascades independent of any ubiquitin-binding adaptor protein
quizzes.explanation: Chain topology dictates 3D conformation: K48-linked chains fold into a compact structure that presents the I44 hydrophobic patch to proteasomal receptors Rpn10 and Rpn13, while K63-linked chains adopt an open, extended conformation selectively engaged by TAB2-NZF and NEMO-UBAN for non-degradative signaling. Linkage-specific DUBs (OTUB1 for K48; CYLD for K63/M1; OTULIN for M1) further enforce this topological code by restricting chain interconversion.
quizzes.citation: Ubiquitin code review · §chain topology / UBD selectivity

quizzes.stem: During a single round of ubiquitin conjugation to a substrate, what is the net ATP cost and the chemical nature of the covalent bond formed between ubiquitin and the substrate?
choices: One ATP hydrolyzed to AMP + PPi; an isopeptide bond between the Ub G76 carboxyl and the substrate lysine ε-amine
Two ATPs hydrolyzed to ADP + Pi each; an isopeptide bond between the Ub G76 carboxyl and the substrate lysine ε-amine
One ATP hydrolyzed to ADP + Pi; an isopeptide bond between the Ub G76 carboxyl and the substrate lysine α-amine
One ATP hydrolyzed to AMP + PPi; a thioester bond between the Ub G76 carboxyl and the E2 active-site cysteine
quizzes.explanation: E1 activates ubiquitin via adenylation (producing Ub-AMP + PPi, not ADP + Pi), loads it as a thioester onto E1 Cys, then transthiolates to E2 Cys — one net ATP per conjugation, driven forward by PPi hydrolysis. The final product is an isopeptide bond formed when the substrate lysine ε-amine (not the α-amine) attacks the E2~Ub thioester; the thioester is an obligate intermediate, not the end product.

quizzes.stem: Which statement best describes the minimum ubiquitin signal required for proteasomal degradation, accounting for both classical biochemical evidence and more recent refinements?
choices: K48-linked tetraubiquitin was established as the canonical minimum by Thrower et al., but this threshold is not absolute — shorter chains, branched K48/K11 linkages, and monoubiquitination can mediate degradation when shuttle factors such as UBQLN or Rad23 provide sufficient receptor avidity.
K63-linked tetraubiquitin is the primary proteasomal targeting signal; K48 linkages serve a secondary role and become relevant only when K63-chain assembly is impaired.

K11-linked diubiquitin is both necessary and sufficient for proteasomal targeting; longer K48 chains function mainly to protect substrates from deubiquitylation rather than to engage proteasomal receptors directly.

Multi-site monoubiquitination — modification of at least four distinct lysines — is required; single-site polyubiquitin chains cannot engage proteasomal ubiquitin receptors regardless of chain length.
quizzes.explanation: Thrower et al. (2000) established K48-linked tetraubiquitin as the minimal in vitro signal for efficient proteasomal recognition, giving rise to the 'tetraUb rule'; subsequent work demonstrated this rule is a useful heuristic, not an absolute requirement, because branched K48/K11 chains, shorter K48 chains, and even monoubiquitin can suffice when shuttle receptors (UBQLN, Rad23) bridge low-avidity signals to the 19S cap.

quizzes.stem: In Cullin-RING Ligases (CRLs), substrate specificity is primarily conferred by which component and mechanism?
choices: A. The substrate receptor (e.g., F-box or DCAF protein) recognizes short linear degrons on substrates, typically only after a priming post-translational modification such as phosphorylation or hydroxylation
B. The cullin scaffold directly contacts substrate hydrophobic degrons, with each of the ~7 cullin paralogs evolved to recognize a distinct substrate class
C. The RING domain selects among E2 ubiquitin-conjugating enzymes in a substrate-dependent manner, restricting which target proteins are ubiquitylated
D. Neddylation of the cullin C-terminal domain allosterically opens a substrate-binding groove that dictates specificity for the assembled complex
quizzes.explanation: CRL substrate specificity is entirely delegated to the interchangeable substrate receptor subunit (F-box, DCAF, BTB, or SOCS protein), which binds short linear degrons — usually only after a post-translational mark primes the substrate. This modular design allows ~7 cullin scaffolds to serve ~350 distinct receptors, with CAND1-mediated exchange dynamically reconfiguring the active repertoire; the cullin and RING subunits themselves do not contact substrate.

quizzes.stem: In hematopoietic stem cells, how does FBXW7 (FBW7) enforce G0 quiescence, and what cellular consequence follows from its loss?
choices: FBXW7 targets c-Myc for proteasomal degradation via the T58 phospho-degron (GSK3β-primed after S62 phosphorylation); loss of FBXW7 stabilizes c-Myc, drives HSCs out of G0, and leads to eventual HSC exhaustion.

FBXW7 degrades Notch1 intracellular domain, suppressing Notch-driven transcription of self-renewal genes; FBXW7 loss permits constitutive Notch signaling that traps HSCs in a quiescent, therapy-resistant state.
FBXW7 degrades MCL1 to limit pro-survival signaling; FBXW7 loss stabilizes MCL1, reduces apoptotic pruning of cycling HSCs, and paradoxically expands the long-term repopulating pool.
FBXW7 targets c-Myc via the S62 phospho-degron after GSK3β primes T58; FBXW7 loss blocks this priming step and prevents c-Myc from entering its transcriptionally active phospho-isoform.
quizzes.explanation: SCF^FBXW7 recognizes c-Myc at the T58 phospho-degron, which requires sequential phosphorylation: ERK/CDK8 phosphorylates S62 first, then GSK3β phosphorylates T58 to create the degron. Thompson et al. (2008 JEM) showed that conditional Fbw7 deletion in HSCs stabilizes c-Myc, forcing HSCs into rapid cycling that ultimately depletes the stem-cell pool through exhaustion.
quizzes.citation: Thompson 2008 JEM · §HSC quiescence / c-Myc T58 phospho-degron

quizzes.stem: The predominantly nuclear localization of CUL4B relative to CUL4A is best explained by which structural feature?
choices: An ~150-amino-acid N-terminal extension containing a nuclear localization signal (NLS)
A divergent WHB (winged-helix B) domain that selectively recruits nuclear DCAFs
Higher intrinsic affinity for DDB1, which is itself concentrated in the nucleus
A C-terminal extension that masks a nuclear export signal present in CUL4A
quizzes.explanation: CUL4B carries an ~150 aa N-terminal extension harboring a canonical NLS, directing it predominantly to the nucleus, whereas CUL4A lacks this extension and partitions more broadly between nuclear and cytoplasmic compartments. Both paralogs otherwise share the same DDB1 adaptor and DCAF substrate-receptor pool, accounting for their substantial—but not complete—substrate overlap.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · General CUL4A/B paralogy section

quizzes.stem: MLN4924 (pevonedistat) inhibits cullin-RING ligases through which primary mechanism?
choices: It is adenylated by NAE (UBA3) and loaded onto NEDD8, forming a covalent NEDD8–MLN4924 adduct that permanently traps the E1 and globally blocks cullin neddylation.
It competitively occupies the adenylation active site of UBA3, reversibly preventing NEDD8 activation without forming a covalent intermediate.
It covalently modifies the NEDD8 E2 conjugating enzyme UBE2M, blocking discharge of NEDD8 from the E1 to the E2.
It binds the cullin scaffold directly to prevent Rbx1 recruitment, disrupting CRL assembly independently of the neddylation cascade.
quizzes.explanation: MLN4924 is a suicide substrate: NAE (UBA3) adenylates it and loads it onto NEDD8, but the resulting covalent NEDD8–MLN4924 adduct cannot be discharged to UBE2M, so NAE is permanently sequestered. This global depletion of cullin neddylation inactivates all CRLs, causing accumulation of substrates such as CDT1, p27, and HIF-1α, ultimately triggering rereplication and apoptosis.

quizzes.stem: Which statement best describes the functional role of the CAND1 cycle in Cullin-RING ligase (CRL) regulation?
choices: CAND1 promotes re-neddylation of deneddylated cullins by competing with CSN for cullin binding, reactivating CRL complexes when substrate receptor occupancy is low.
CAND1 displaces substrate receptors from deneddylated cullins and is outcompeted by SRs when cognate substrate is abundant, enabling a limited cullin pool to dynamically serve a large SR repertoire.
CAND1 recruits the CSN complex to active, neddylated CRL complexes, triggering NEDD8 removal and initiating substrate receptor exchange.
CAND1 acts as a scaffold that tethers multiple substrate receptors simultaneously to a single cullin, increasing CRL processivity toward diverse ubiquitination targets.
quizzes.explanation: CAND1 is a HEAT-repeat exchange factor that binds deneddylated cullins after CSN removes NEDD8, displacing the resident substrate receptor and exposing the cullin to the cytosolic SR pool. Because an SR loaded with abundant cognate substrate outcompetes CAND1 for cullin binding, CRL composition is remodeled in proportion to substrate availability—allowing a small cullin pool to adaptively serve a large SR repertoire.

quizzes.stem: What is the primary structural consequence of NEDD8 conjugation on the cullin subunit of a CRL ubiquitin ligase?
choices: NEDD8 modification stabilizes the WHB subdomain in a rigid closed conformation, increasing RING-box affinity for charged E2 without altering cullin geometry.

NEDD8 conjugation on the cullin C-terminal lysine wedges the WHB subdomain outward, releasing the RING-box from a closed state into a flexible tether that repositions E2~Ub to reach substrate lysines.
NEDD8 modification bridges the N-terminal cullin domain to the substrate receptor adaptor, shortening the distance between the RING-bound E2 and the substrate.
NEDD8 conjugation displaces the F-box substrate receptor from the cullin N-terminal domain, repositioning it to face the RING-bound E2~Ub for direct ubiquitin transfer.
quizzes.explanation: NEDD8 attaches to a C-terminal lysine on the cullin and physically wedges against the WHB subdomain, displacing it and converting the RING-box from a closed, autoinhibited state into a flexible tether; this conformational change permits the E2~Ub conjugate to swing into range of substrate lysines, boosting ubiquitin transfer ~4–10-fold. Cryo-EM studies (Baek 2020, 2021) directly visualize this dynamic flexibility and show that the same modification sterically excludes CAND1, locking the ligase in an active conformation.

quizzes.stem: Which structural feature of DDB1 accounts for its ability to engage substrate receptors (DCAFs) that differ considerably in size and shape?
choices: Rotational flexibility of the BPB β-propeller relative to the BPA/BPC module, which adjusts the width of the DCAF-binding cleft
Intrinsic disorder in the CTD, which remodels upon DCAF engagement to present a complementary binding surface
Conformational switching of BPC between open and closed states, which repositions the CUL4 scaffold to create space for bulky DCAFs
A rigid, preformed hydrophobic groove in BPA that presents a universal docking surface for the WDxR motif shared by all DCAFs
quizzes.explanation: BPB is rotationally mobile relative to the BPA/BPC platform, allowing the inter-propeller cleft to widen or narrow and thereby accommodate DCAFs of diverse geometries; BPC's role is instead to dock onto the CUL4 cullin scaffold, anchoring DDB1 to the ligase core rather than contacting DCAFs.

quizzes.stem: In humans, DCAF7 maps to which chromosomal locus, and what is the disease significance of that region?
choices: A. 9p24.1; the locus coincides with JAK2 and is itself a direct MPN driver site
B. 17q23.2; the locus is not a known driver, though 17q undergoes recurrent cytogenetic alterations in myeloid malignancy
C. 17p13.1; the locus overlaps TP53 and contributes to genomic instability in myeloid disease
D. 5q31.1; the locus lies within the commonly deleted 5q region associated with MDS haploinsufficiency
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7 resides at 17q23.2, a region recurrently altered in myeloid malignancies, yet DCAF7 itself has not been identified as a driver at that locus — distinguishing passenger chromosomal context from direct oncogenic function. The other options place DCAF7 at loci harboring canonical myeloid drivers (JAK2, TP53, RPS14) with which it has no established co-localization.

quizzes.stem: Buckley et al. 2012 generated a K-GG diGly ubiquitinome atlas of mouse ESCs, cataloguing 2,578 ubiquitylation sites on 1,168 proteins. Which substrate–E3 ligase pair did this study identify as a node that actively modulates pluripotency?
choices: A. SCF^FBXW7 targeting phospho-Thr58 cMyc via a GSK3-primed phospho-degron
B. CRL4^DCAF12 targeting phospho-Ser111 NANOG for proteasomal degradation during the naive-to-primed transition
C. SCF^FBXO4 targeting phospho-Ser65 OCT4 via a CK2-primed phospho-degron to limit reprogramming efficiency
D. APC/C^Cdh1 targeting KLF4 through a D-box motif during the mesenchymal-to-epithelial transition of reprogramming
quizzes.explanation: Buckley et al. 2012 identified SCF^FBXW7-mediated degradation of phospho-Thr58 cMyc as a pluripotency-modulating axis within their ESC ubiquitinome, and separately showed that the 19S proteasome lid DUB PSMD14 (Rpn11) is required for both pluripotency maintenance and somatic cell reprogramming—collectively establishing the UPS as an active sculptor of ESC identity rather than a passive housekeeping system.

quizzes.stem: Which statement best explains why DCAFs are considered an opportunity for therapeutic targeting in cancer biology?
choices: Because DCAFs harbor the catalytic RING domain of CRL4, small molecules that competitively occupy this domain can suppress ubiquitin transfer to disease-relevant substrates with mechanistic precision.

As the substrate-recognition modules of CRL4, DCAFs can be pharmacologically reprogrammed by molecular glues or PROTACs to recruit neosubstrates for degradation, with lineage-restricted DCAFs conferring tissue selectivity.
Because DCAFs bridge the CRL4 complex to E2 ubiquitin-conjugating enzymes, competitive peptides that disrupt the DCAF–E2 interface can selectively stabilize oncogenic substrates.
Because many DCAFs are transcriptionally induced by cytokine signaling, BET bromodomain inhibitors that suppress DCAF expression can indirectly reduce turnover of pro-inflammatory substrates.
quizzes.explanation: DCAFs are the substrate-recognition subunits of CRL4 whose ligand-binding surfaces can be reshaped by molecular glues (e.g., IMiDs, CC-885, indisulam) to recruit neosubstrates, and PROTACs extend this logic by tethering any DCAF ligand to an arbitrary target. Lineage-restricted DCAFs such as DCAF7 add tissue selectivity unavailable to upstream CRL inhibitors like MLN4924.

quizzes.stem: In Gao 2015, which experimental finding most directly established DCAF7 as a hematopoietic-selective DDB1-associated substrate receptor (DCAF)?
choices: A. AP-MS of DDB1 in HL60 cells identified DCAF7 as the only DCAF copurified, whereas ESCs and 293T cells each pulled down DCAF4, DCAF8, DCAF11, DCAF15, and VprBP instead.
B. RNA-seq profiling of primary CD34+ HSPCs showed DCAF7 transcript levels were markedly higher than any other DCAF family member across non-hematopoietic cell lines.
C. AP-MS of DDB1 in ESCs identified DCAF7 as the predominant DCAF, while HL60 and K562 cells showed a distributed pattern across DCAF4, DCAF8, and DCAF15.
D. Conditional deletion of DCAF7 (Vav-Cre) was lethal by P7 with HSPC collapse, whereas DCAF4 deletion produced no hematopoietic phenotype, establishing lineage-specific dependency.
quizzes.explanation: Gao 2015 used affinity purification–mass spectrometry (AP-MS) of DDB1 to survey the DCAF repertoire across cell types; DCAF7 was the sole DCAF recovered from HL60 hematopoietic cells, while DCAF4/8/11/15 and VprBP were found in ESCs and 293T, directly demonstrating lineage-selective DDB1–DCAF7 complex assembly. Distractor D conflates this finding with the Ddb1 (not DCAF7) conditional-deletion phenotype, which establishes that CRL4 is required in HSPCs but does not itself prove receptor selectivity.
quizzes.citation: Gao 2015 · AP-MS/DCAF repertoire comparison (HL60 vs. ESC/293T)

quizzes.stem: Which combination of evidence most directly supports a hematopoietic-specific role for DCAF7?
choices: Tissue-biased expression in blood lineages, selective viability dependency across hematopoietic cancer lines in DepMap, and mass-spectrometric identification as the dominant DDB1-bound DCAF in HL60 cells
Tissue-biased expression in blood lineages, co-immunoprecipitation with JAK2V617F in hematopoietic progenitors, and rescue of erythroid colony formation upon DCAF7 overexpression
Selective DepMap dependency in hematopoietic lines, CRISPR screen identification as a hit in T-cell lymphoma, and dominant DDB1 binding demonstrated in HEK293T cells
Dominant DDB1 binding in HL60 cells, somatic DCAF7 loss-of-function mutations enriched in myeloproliferative neoplasms, and erythroid-specific promoter hypomethylation
quizzes.explanation: The case for a hematopoietic role rests on three independent, convergent lines: biased expression in blood-lineage tissues, selective essentiality in blood cancer lines per DepMap, and Gao et al. 2015 quantitative mass spectrometry showing DCAF7 is the predominant DDB1-associated DCAF specifically in the HL60 myeloid model. Distractors swap the HL60 finding for HEK293T (wrong cell context), introduce JAK2 co-IP or somatic mutations (not established in the source evidence), and add erythroid rescue or methylation data that are fabricated adjacencies.

quizzes.stem: In the context of DCAF7 biology, what does the timing of the postnatal day 4 (P4) DCAF7 expression surge suggest about its function?
choices: It coincides with peak IFN-α production in neonates, suggesting DCAF7 amplifies interferon signaling during that window.
It coincides with the onset of the perinatal IFN pulse, positioning DCAF7 as an initiator of the neonatal interferon response.
It coincides with the late phase of the perinatal IFN pulse, consistent with DCAF7 serving as a pulse terminator.
It coincides with neonatal HSC expansion, implicating DCAF7 in hematopoietic output rather than IFN regulation.
quizzes.explanation: The P4 surge maps to the late—not peak or early—phase of the perinatal IFN pulse (Li 2020), establishing a timing model in which DCAF7 is induced by that pulse and subsequently acts to shut it down rather than sustain or initiate it.

quizzes.stem: The DCAF7 alias 'HAN11' is considered a misnomer because the protein
choices: shares structural homology with ankyrin-repeat proteins in plants but lacks this fold in mammalian orthologs
carries WD40 β-propeller repeats, not the ankyrin repeats that the 'AN' designation implies
was mapped to chromosome 11 in an early annotation that was later corrected in human genome databases
was first characterized as a kinase-interacting scaffold before its CRL4 substrate-receptor function was identified
quizzes.explanation: HAN11 was named as though the protein harbored ankyrin (AN) repeats, but DCAF7/WDR68 is a canonical WD40 β-propeller; because distinct subfields (CRL, kinase, plant, Drosophila) independently named the same protein, the structurally inaccurate alias persisted alongside the accurate WDR68 designation.

quizzes.stem: Which statement best describes the evolutionary conservation of DCAF7 and its implications for cellular function?
choices: A. 100% amino acid identity across mammals, ~45% identity to petunia, and no known paralog — consistent with a non-redundant essential function
B. ~90% amino acid identity across mammals, ~70% identity to Drosophila, and one functionally redundant paralog — suggesting partial buffering of loss-of-function
C. 100% amino acid identity across vertebrates, ~65% identity to S. cerevisiae, and no known paralog — indicating ancient origin but dispensability in plants
D. ~85% amino acid identity across mammals, ~45% identity to petunia, and multiple paralogs within the DCAF subfamily — implying functional redundancy
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7 is 100% identical at the amino acid level across all examined mammals and retains ~45% identity even in petunia, indicating extreme evolutionary pressure to preserve its exact sequence. The complete absence of any paralog means no other protein can substitute for its function, providing a molecular argument for why DCAF7 perturbation produces non-redundant phenotypic consequences.

quizzes.stem: The WDXR motif is a conserved tetrapeptide found in many CRL4 substrate receptors. Which statement best describes this motif and DCAF7's relationship to it?
choices: A conserved Trp-Asp-X-Arg sequence used by DCAFs to engage DDB1; DCAF7 lacks this motif and must bind DDB1 through an alternate interface.
A conserved Trp-Asp-X-Arg sequence used by DCAFs to contact CUL4A directly; DCAF7 contains a canonical WDXR element in its seventh WD40 blade.
A conserved Trp-Asp-X-Arg sequence embedded within DDB1 that recruits substrate adaptors; DCAF7 encodes a degenerate version that retains partial affinity.
A conserved Trp-Asp-X-Arg sequence used by WD40 proteins to recognize substrate degrons; DCAF7 possesses this motif but it is masked by an autoinhibitory loop.
quizzes.explanation: The WDXR motif (Trp-Asp-X-Arg) is a conserved tetrapeptide present in many DCAFs that mediates direct binding to DDB1 (Jin 2006). DCAF7 lacks this canonical motif entirely, indicating it relies on a non-canonical surface to associate with DDB1 rather than the shared WDXR-mediated contact used by most family members.

quizzes.stem: Based on WD40 repeat enumeration and AlphaFold modeling, what three-dimensional fold is predicted for DCAF7?
choices: A. Six-bladed Kelch-type β-propeller
B. Seven-bladed WD40-type β-propeller
C. Eight-bladed WD40-type β-propeller
D. Armadillo (ARM) repeat α-helical superhelix
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7 (WDR68) encodes seven WD40 sequence motifs, and AlphaFold models the protein as a canonical seven-bladed β-propeller — one blade per WD40 repeat. Kelch repeats yield a similar but distinct six-bladed architecture used by CRL3 substrate receptors, while ARM repeats form an entirely different α-helical fold found in other ubiquitin-pathway scaffolds.
quizzes.citation: AlphaFold prediction + WD40 repeat enumeration · DCAF7 Biology §1

quizzes.stem: An IFN-signaling thesis committee chair would have the strongest mechanistic reason to interrogate a DCAF7/JAK2V617F candidate on Cullin-RING Ligases (CRLs) because:
choices: CUL5–SOCS1 complexes ubiquitinate activated JAK2 and cytokine receptors for proteasomal degradation, making CRL5 a direct brake on IFN signaling whose loss drives constitutive pathway activation and autoimmunity.
CRL4–DDB1–DCAF1 poly-ubiquitinates STING after cGAS activation, limiting excessive type-I IFN production during self-DNA sensing.
SCF–β-TrCP (CRL1) targets phospho-IRF3 for K48-linked ubiquitination after each innate immune wave, resetting the type-I IFN sensor for subsequent viral sensing.
CRL3–SPOP ubiquitinates IRF7 in plasmacytoid dendritic cells, preventing runaway type-I IFN amplification during sustained viral infection.
quizzes.explanation: CUL5–SOCS1 is the canonical CRL that poly-ubiquitinates activated JAKs and cytokine receptors, placing Cullin-RING biology at the mechanistic core of IFN pathway termination; loss of SOCS1 causes constitutive JAK2–STAT signaling and IFN-driven autoimmunity — a direct bridge between CRL function and IFN physiology that a committee chair would expect any JAK2/IFN candidate to know cold.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · General/Bonus §CRL-IFN links; Kamura 2004 (CUL5-SOCS elongin complex); Alexander & Hilton 2004 (SOCS1 autoimmunity)

quizzes.stem: A WD40 beta-propeller presents three distinct surfaces available for protein-protein interaction. Which combination correctly identifies those surfaces and the loop types that define them?
choices: Top face (A-B loops), bottom face (C-D loops), circumferential edge
Top face (B-C loops), bottom face (D-A loops), circumferential edge
Top face (D-A loops), bottom face (B-C loops), circumferential edge
Top face (D-A loops), bottom face (B-C loops), lateral insertion groove
quizzes.explanation: In a canonical WD40 propeller, the D-A loops (connecting strand D of repeat n to strand A of repeat n+1) cluster on the top face, while the B-C loops cluster on the bottom face; the circumferential edge formed by the outer strands constitutes the third interaction surface. Swapping these loop assignments (as in choices A and B) misidentifies which face each loop type decorates.

quizzes.stem: A panel of point mutations mapped across the DCAF7 surface (BC18630 mutagenesis) was used to identify residues required for DDB1 recruitment and substrate binding. What is the primary architectural conclusion drawn from this experiment?
choices: A surface patch outside the canonical WDXR motif and substrate-groove positions mediates DDB1 recruitment and substrate binding, indicating a non-canonical DCAF7 adaptor architecture.
Residues within the WDXR motif constitute the primary DDB1-binding interface, placing DCAF7 squarely within the canonical WD40-repeat DCAF subfamily.
Substrate-groove mutations coordinately abolish both DDB1 recruitment and substrate recognition, indicating that these two functions share a single overlapping surface at the classical DCAF site.
Core β-propeller mutations disrupt DDB1 engagement indirectly by destabilizing overall WD40 fold integrity rather than eliminating a discrete protein–protein contact surface.
quizzes.explanation: BC18630 mutagenesis identified a functionally critical surface patch on DCAF7 that is spatially distinct from both the classical WDXR motif and the canonical substrate-binding groove, demonstrating that DCAF7 uses a non-canonical interface for DDB1 recruitment and substrate engagement. This is inconsistent with canonical DCAF architecture, in which the WDXR motif is the defining DDB1 contact element.
quizzes.citation: BC18630 mutagenesis study · DCAF7 Biology §surface-patch analysis

quizzes.stem: Which of the following best explains why no crystal structure of DCAF7 has been published to date?
choices: Extensive glycosylation of its WD40 β-propeller surface disrupts the lattice contacts required for crystal formation
Intrinsically disordered linker regions flanking the WD40 domain prevent adoption of a stable, crystallizable conformation
Its small monomer size provides limited crystal-packing surfaces, and trapping a stable substrate complex for co-crystallization has proven difficult
Strong homo-oligomerization at concentrations required for crystallization produces heterogeneous aggregates incompatible with diffraction
quizzes.explanation: The model answer identifies two compounding obstacles: the small monomer offers few crystal-packing contacts, and substrate-bound conformations—typically needed to capture a biologically meaningful state of a CRL4 receptor—are difficult to trap in a homogeneous complex. These barriers leave the structure an open opportunity rather than a settled gap.
quizzes.citation: DCAF7 Biology · §structural gap (model answer, no primary crystal-structure citation exists)

quizzes.stem: DCAF7 (WDR68) has been assigned multiple molecular functions across independent lines of research. Which function was characterized first?
choices: Kinase scaffold, anchoring DYRK1A and HIPK2 to coordinate substrate phosphorylation
CRL4 substrate receptor, recruiting ubiquitylation targets to the DDB1–CUL4 E3 ligase
Transcriptional coactivator, stabilizing androgen receptor occupancy at target gene promoters
Chromatin reader, docking onto H3K4me3 nucleosomes through its WD40 β-propeller
quizzes.explanation: Ritterhoff 2010 and Miyata & Nishida 2011 established DCAF7 as a kinase-scaffolding protein — bridging MEKK1/HIPK2 and DYRK1A to their substrates — before its CRL4 substrate-receptor function was characterized. The WD40 β-propeller serves as the protein–protein interaction surface in both contexts, making the CRL4 role a plausible but later discovery.

quizzes.stem: Which statement best describes the current structural knowledge of DCAF7?
choices: DCAF7 contains six WD40 repeats, and its structure was determined by X-ray crystallography, revealing the geometry of its DDB1-binding surface.
DCAF7 contains seven WD40 repeats; its structure is available only as an AlphaFold prediction, and a small-molecule (BC18630) binding patch has been mapped by mutagenesis.
DCAF7 contains eight WD40 repeats and has been structurally characterized by cryo-EM in complex with DDB1, enabling direct visualization of its substrate-recruitment groove.
DCAF7 contains seven WD40 repeats with a crystal structure solved in complex with DYRK1A, experimentally confirming its β-propeller architecture.
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7 is predicted to adopt a seven-bladed β-propeller fold by AlphaFold, but no experimentally determined structure has been deposited. The binding site for the small molecule BC18630 was identified through mutational mapping rather than direct crystallographic or cryo-EM visualization.

quizzes.stem: Proteomic surveys have cataloged which combination of post-translational modifications on DCAF7?
choices: A. Multiple N-terminal phosphorylation sites and ubiquitination on several lysines, with functional roles largely uncharacterized
B. C-terminal phosphorylation and lysine SUMOylation, associated with regulated nuclear export
C. Arginine methylation and N-terminal acetylation, linked to control of proteasomal stability
D. WD40-domain serine/threonine phosphorylation and cullin neddylation, required for CRL4 complex assembly
quizzes.explanation: Proteomic databases annotate DCAF7 with multiple N-terminal phospho-sites and ubiquitination marks on several lysines; critically, systematic functional studies assigning roles to these specific modifications have not been reported, making them open mechanistic questions relevant to MPN signaling. Distractors B–D invoke real PTM classes (SUMOylation, methylation, neddylation) that operate in adjacent CRL4/nuclear-protein biology but are not the modifications cataloged in DCAF7 proteomics.

quizzes.stem: BC18630 represents a significant advance in DCAF7 biology primarily because it:
choices: A. Is one of very few DCAF7 tool compounds, engages a non-canonical surface, shows efficacy in MPN cells, and synergizes with IFN-α, making DCAF7 chemically actionable
B. Is the first PROTAC that recruits DCAF7 as substrate receptor to selectively degrade JAK2V617F over wild-type JAK2 in hematopoietic progenitors
C. Occupies the canonical WD40 substrate-binding pocket of DCAF7, blocking CRL4 complex assembly and phenocopying complete DCAF7 knockout
D. Acts as a covalent inhibitor of the DCAF7–DDB1 interface, preventing CUL4 neddylation and broadly suppressing interferon-stimulated gene transcription
quizzes.explanation: BC18630 is notable precisely because usable chemical tools for DCAF adaptors are rare; it engages a non-canonical surface (distinct from the substrate-docking WD40 face), retains activity in JAK2V617F MPN cells, and potentiates IFN-α, establishing proof-of-concept that DCAF7 can be pharmacologically targeted. The other options describe plausible but unsupported mechanisms — PROTAC substrate-receptor use, canonical pocket blockade, or DDB1-interface covalency — none of which are attributed to BC18630 in the model answer.

quizzes.stem: DCAF7 functions as a substrate receptor within the CRL4 E3 ligase complex. Which ubiquitin chain topology is characteristically assembled on DCAF7 substrates, and what is its primary cellular fate?
choices: K63-linked polyubiquitin chains that route substrates to the endolysosomal degradation pathway
K11-linked polyubiquitin chains associated with APC/C-driven mitotic substrate turnover
K48-linked polyubiquitin chains that target substrates to the 26S proteasome for degradation
Monoubiquitination at multiple lysines that signals substrate relocalization rather than proteolysis
quizzes.explanation: CRL4 complexes, including those using DCAF7 as the substrate receptor, canonically assemble K48-linked polyubiquitin chains on their targets; K48 linkages are the primary recognition signal for the 26S proteasome, distinguishing CRL4-mediated proteolytic turnover from K63-linked chains (which mediate endosomal sorting or DNA-damage signaling) or monoubiquitination (which governs non-proteolytic processes such as histone regulation).
quizzes.citation: Model answer · DCAF7 Biology §1 (CRL4 substrate degradation literature)

quizzes.stem: Which of the following correctly pairs a validated CRL4^DCAF7 substrate with its primary biological context?
choices: DNA ligase I (LIG1) — antiviral restriction of influenza polymerase activity
TFEB — maintenance of genome integrity at DNA replication forks
Menin (MEN1) — transcriptional dysregulation driving pancreatic neuroendocrine tumorigenesis
Influenza polymerase acidic subunit (PA) — lysosomal biogenesis and autophagy induction
quizzes.explanation: CRL4^DCAF7 polyubiquitinates menin (MEN1) to promote its proteasomal degradation, a mechanism linked to pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor (PanNET) pathogenesis (Xu 2023). The distractors deliberately swap biological contexts among genuine DCAF7 substrates: LIG1 is a replication-fork substrate (Peng 2016), TFEB is degraded in a coronavirus/autophagy context (Lear 2025), and influenza PA is targeted for antiviral restriction (Yu 2025).

quizzes.stem: Which cullin scaffold(s) does DCAF7 utilize as a CRL substrate receptor, and how well-characterized is its selectivity in vivo?
choices: A. CUL4A exclusively, as DCAF7 preferentially assembles into cytoplasmic CRL4A complexes away from the nuclear CUL4B pool
B. CUL4B exclusively, consistent with its specification in both the PanNET tumor suppression and antiviral innate immunity literature
C. Both CUL4A and CUL4B, with context-dependent cullin association whose in vivo selectivity has not been fully resolved across all substrate contexts
D. CUL3 in mitotically active cells and CUL4B in post-mitotic cells, reflecting cell-cycle-regulated switching between cullin scaffolds
quizzes.explanation: The PanNET paper and the antiviral (CRL4B) paper both specify CUL4B, but other contexts such as LIG1 regulation are more ambiguous, indicating DCAF7 can engage both CUL4A and CUL4B scaffolds; the mechanistic basis for cullin selectivity in vivo remains an open question.
quizzes.citation: DCAF7 Biology · PanNET paper; antiviral CRL4B paper

quizzes.stem: Gao 2015 performed co-immunoprecipitation of DDB1 across multiple cell lines to map DCAF binding partners. Which outcome best describes their key finding regarding DCAF7's cell-type selectivity?
choices: A. In HL60 cells DCAF7 was the sole DCAF co-precipitated with DDB1, whereas in 293T cells and ESCs DCAF7 was absent and multiple DCAFs (DCAF4, DCAF8, DCAF11, DCAF15, VprBP) occupied DDB1 instead.
B. DCAF7 co-precipitated with DDB1 equivalently across HL60, 293T, and ESC lines, indicating a constitutive, cell-type-independent CRL4 assembly.
C. In HL60 cells DCAF7 and VprBP co-occupied DDB1 simultaneously, while in 293T cells only DCAF4 and DCAF15 were detected, establishing reciprocal hematopoietic enrichment.
D. In 293T cells DCAF7 displaced DCAF4 and DCAF15 from DDB1, while in HL60 cells multiple DCAFs competed for the same DDB1 binding surface.
quizzes.explanation: Gao 2015 found that DDB1 pulls down exclusively DCAF7 in the hematopoietic HL60 line, while the same DDB1 bait in 293T and ESCs recruits a distinct set of DCAFs (DCAF4/8/11/15, VprBP) with no detectable DCAF7, establishing DCAF7 as a hematopoietic-selective CRL4 substrate receptor. This cell-type specificity in DDB1 partner usage underpins the rationale for DCAF7's selective role in MPN hematopoietic signaling.

quizzes.stem: Which combination of experimental findings best supports the conclusion that IFIT3 is a direct substrate of DCAF7-dependent proteolysis in JAK2V617F MPN cells?
choices: IFIT3 is the top-ranked accumulated protein in DCAF7-KO HEL cells by TMT proteomics, its protein half-life is prolonged by cycloheximide chase, and its mRNA level is unchanged — consistent with post-translational DCAF7-mediated degradation.

IFIT3 mRNA is upregulated in DCAF7-KO HEL cells and protein levels correlate with interferon pathway activation, indicating transcriptional rather than post-translational control downstream of DCAF7 loss.
IFIT3 co-immunoprecipitates with CRL4–DCAF7 and accumulates upon MG132 treatment, demonstrating proteasomal targeting without additional evidence from protein stability assays.

IFIT3 protein is modestly elevated in DCAF7-KO cells by Western blot, but its mRNA is also increased, making it difficult to distinguish transcriptional induction from post-translational stabilization.
quizzes.explanation: The triad of unbiased proteomic accumulation in KO cells, extended half-life by cycloheximide chase, and unchanged mRNA together satisfy all three criteria for a post-translational substrate — the stable mRNA rules out transcriptional induction, and the prolonged half-life directly implicates DCAF7 in protein turnover rather than synthesis.
quizzes.citation: DCAF7 Biology · TMT proteomics / cycloheximide chase / mRNA stability

quizzes.stem: Which statement best characterizes current understanding of the relationship between DCAF7's scaffolding and E3-adapter functions?
choices: The functions appear largely separable; DCAF7 retains scaffolding activity when DDB1-interaction surfaces are disrupted, consistent with CRL4-independent operation.
Evidence supports at least three competing models—independent, degradation-coupled, and context-switched—with no consensus yet reached, suggesting the relationship is likely context-dependent.
Scaffolding and E3-adapter activities are mechanistically coupled; substrate binding by DCAF7 requires concurrent CRL4 assembly and ubiquitin transfer to the bound substrate.
The two functions are mutually exclusive in a given cellular context, with upstream signaling state dictating whether DCAF7 engages CRL4 or acts as a kinase-complex scaffold.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer identifies three mechanistic models—independent, degradation-coupled, and context-switched—each with supporting evidence but no definitive resolution, making 'unresolved and likely context-dependent' the most accurate characterization. Choices A, C, and D each promote one of those three models to the status of established fact, overstating what the current evidence supports.

quizzes.stem: Which statement correctly identifies the common miscitation error associated with Yu 2025 in the DCAF7 literature?
choices: Yu 2025 is often cited for DCAF7 kinase scaffolding activity, but the paper actually characterizes DCAF7 as a substrate receptor mediating antiviral restriction of influenza PA
Yu 2025 is often cited for DCAF7-dependent ubiquitination of IFN-α receptor subunits, but the paper actually reports DCAF7-driven JAK2 proteasomal degradation in myeloid cells
Yu 2025 is often cited for DCAF7 regulation of IFIT3 translational efficiency, but the paper actually identifies DCAF7 as a CRL4 adaptor targeting p21 for turnover
Yu 2025 is often cited for MPN-specific DCAF7 transcriptional upregulation, but the paper actually demonstrates DCAF7 haploinsufficiency in hematopoietic progenitors
quizzes.explanation: Yu 2025 characterizes DCAF7 in its substrate-receptor capacity within an antiviral context — specifically restriction of the influenza polymerase acidic (PA) subunit — not as a kinase scaffold. Conflating these roles misattributes the paper's mechanistic contribution and incorrectly imports a 'kinase scaffolding' function that is drawn from earlier DCAF7-DYRK1A literature, not from this work.

quizzes.stem: Which statement most accurately describes the prevalence and primary citation for DCAF7 L340F mutations in Down Syndrome acute megakaryoblastic leukemia (DS-AMKL)?
choices: A. L340F mutations occur in approximately 5–10% of DS-AMKL cases and are most directly documented in Labuhn et al. 2019 Cancer Cell.
B. L340F mutations occur in approximately 20–30% of DS-AMKL cases and are the primary subject of Sakaguchi et al. 2013 Nat Genet.
C. DCAF7 copy number amplification, rather than point mutation, underlies leukemogenesis in ~5–10% of DS-AMKL cases per Labuhn et al. 2019.
D. DCAF7 L340F recurs at ~5–10% frequency in DS-AMKL, with Yoshida et al. 2013 Nat Genet serving as the primary citable source for this finding.
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7 L340F is a recurrent point mutation (~5–10% of DS-AMKL) best attributed to Labuhn et al. 2019 Cancer Cell. The label 'Yoshida 2013' in some proposals is a misattribution of Sakaguchi et al. 2013 Nat Genet, a paper that does not foreground DCAF7 and therefore cannot serve as primary evidence for this mutation.
quizzes.citation: Labuhn 2019 Cancer Cell; Sakaguchi 2013 Nat Genet · DCAF7 Biology §DS-AMKL

quizzes.stem: DCAF7 loss selectively impairs the fitness of JAK2V617F hematopoietic cells while leaving wild-type cells largely unaffected. Which mechanism best explains this mutant-specific dependency?
choices: JAK2V617F directly phosphorylates DCAF7 on a conserved degron motif, stabilizing the adaptor and creating a synthetic dependency not present in wild-type cells.

Wild-type progenitors express a compensatory CRL4 adaptor (DCAF8) that redundantly targets IFIT3 for degradation; JAK2V617F transcriptionally suppresses DCAF8, leaving DCAF7 as the sole IFIT3 E3 adaptor in mutant clones.

JAK2V617F clones generate chronic autocrine/paracrine IFN signaling, making their survival contingent on DCAF7-mediated IFIT3 degradation to dampen that signal; wild-type cells lack this tonic IFN pressure and tolerate IFIT3 accumulation without consequence.

IFIT3 directly suppresses JAK2V617F kinase activity through a protein–protein interaction; wild-type JAK2 is structurally insensitive to IFIT3, so only mutant cells require DCAF7 to prevent IFIT3-mediated kinase inhibition.
quizzes.explanation: The selective advantage of the JAK2V617F clone depends on evading the antiproliferative effects of the chronic IFN signal it generates; DCAF7 sustains this evasion by targeting IFIT3 for proteasomal degradation, making DCAF7 loss synthetically detrimental only in the IFN-dependent mutant context. The observed upregulation of DCAF7 in MPN CD34+ cells further supports a compensatory role in maintaining IFN dampening within the clone.

quizzes.stem: Hematopoietic-specific deletion of DCAF7 (Vav-Cre KO) produces which phenotype, and what mechanism has been proposed to explain it?
choices: A. Postnatal lethality (~30–40%) within the first week, with P4 extramedullary LSK expansion and myeloid bias; proposed to result from failure to terminate the perinatal IFN pulse when DCAF7 normally surges at P4
B. Embryonic lethality by E14.5 with collapsed fetal liver hematopoiesis; proposed to result from unrestrained NF-κB activation in HSCs lacking DCAF7-dependent IκBα ubiquitination

C. Normal postnatal development followed by adult-onset myeloproliferative disease with splenomegaly; proposed to result from constitutive IFIT3-driven IFN-α feedback that amplifies residual JAK2 signaling
D. Postnatal runting with selective T-cell lymphopenia and intact myeloid output; proposed to result from DCAF7 loss disrupting WNK1-OXSR1-dependent lymphocyte trafficking
quizzes.explanation: E14.5 hematopoiesis is normal, ruling out an embryonic requirement; the phenotype emerges postnatally with ~30–40% lethality, extramedullary LSK expansion, and myeloid skewing specifically at P4. The proposed mechanism is that DCAF7, which normally surges at P4, is required to extinguish the perinatal IFN pulse—its absence leaves interferon signaling unresolved, driving aberrant HSC proliferation and lineage bias.

quizzes.stem: BC18630 (a DCAF7 inhibitor) and IFN-α show a Loewe synergy score of ~7.15 in JAK2V617F MPN cells. What does this result most directly imply about the mechanistic relationship between DCAF7 inhibition and IFN-α signaling?
choices: A. DCAF7 normally acts as a brake on IFN output, so its inhibition and exogenous IFN-α stimulation engage the same non-redundant axis, producing genuine synergy rather than simple additivity.
B. BC18630 sensitizes cells to IFN-α by stabilizing IFNAR1 on the cell surface, increasing receptor availability independently of DCAF7's role in the IFN pathway.
C. The two agents converge on parallel, independent antiproliferative pathways, yielding an additive — but not synergistic — reduction in JAK2V617F cell viability.
D. DCAF7 inhibition potentiates IFN-α signaling by blocking proteasomal degradation of phospho-STAT1, prolonging transcriptional responses downstream of IFNAR.
quizzes.explanation: A Loewe score of ~7.15 indicates genuine synergy, ruling out mere additivity (choice C) and implying the two interventions are non-redundant within the same pathway axis. The model interprets this as DCAF7 functioning as a brake on IFN output: pharmacological release of that brake (BC18630) and direct pathway activation (IFN-α) amplify each other because they hit different nodes of the same circuit rather than independent ones.

quizzes.stem: In a DCAF7 conditional knockout model driven by Mx1-Cre, hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs) remain largely unaffected while disease-relevant progenitors are eliminated. Which explanation best accounts for this compartment-selective outcome?
choices: Mx1 promoter induction requires robust type I interferon signaling, which is attenuated in quiescent HSCs, producing incomplete Cre-mediated recombination and residual DCAF7 expression
HSCs upregulate a paralogous CRL4 substrate receptor that compensates for DCAF7 loss by maintaining ubiquitination of shared target substrates

The multipotent progenitor and megakaryocyte-erythroid progenitor (MP/MEP) compartment — not HSC/MPP cells — is selectively dependent on DCAF7, creating a therapeutic window that spares normal stem cells
DCAF7 protein has a shorter half-life in HSCs than in committed progenitors, limiting cumulative functional loss after Cre-mediated gene deletion
quizzes.explanation: The model answer locates DCAF7 functional dependency specifically in the MP/MEP compartment rather than the HSC/MPP pool; because HSCs do not rely on DCAF7 for normal function, Mx1-Cre deletion selectively impairs the disease-driving progenitor populations while leaving the stem cell reservoir intact. This differential dependency is the mechanistic basis of the therapeutic window.
quizzes.citation: DCAF7 Biology · Mx1-Cre model / MP-MEP therapeutic window

quizzes.stem: A study in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (PanNETs) reports that DCAF7 promotes malignant growth by targeting tumor-suppressor proteins for proteasomal degradation. What is the primary concern this finding raises for systemic DCAF7-directed therapy in JAK2V617F MPN?
choices: A. Systemic DCAF7 inhibition risks paradoxical pro-tumorigenic effects in non-hematopoietic tissues where DCAF7 degrades tumor suppressors, narrowing the therapeutic window to strategies that achieve hematopoietic-selective target engagement.

B. Competition between endogenous tumor-suppressor substrates and IFIT3 for the DCAF7 WD40-repeat surface would reduce IFIT3 polyubiquitination efficiency and blunt the intended anti-proliferative IFN-α signal in MPN cells.

C. MEN1 protein, destabilized by DCAF7 in PanNETs, directly represses JAK2 transcription; its restoration upon DCAF7 inhibition would therefore paradoxically amplify JAK2V617F-driven proliferation in MPN.

D. Because DCAF7 is amplified across neuroendocrine tumors, MPN patients with concurrent PanNET disease would clear a DCAF7 inhibitor more rapidly through a tumor sink effect, reducing drug exposure in the hematopoietic compartment.
quizzes.explanation: The PanNET data establish that DCAF7 can function as an oncogene in non-hematopoietic tissue by promoting tumor-suppressor degradation, so a systemic inhibitor carries context-dependent risk of de-repressing residual tumor-suppressor loss in other organs. The mitigating argument is exploitation of a hematopoietic-selective DCAF7 dependency, which confines therapeutic benefit and risk to the target compartment.

quizzes.stem: What is the current experimental status of separating DCAF7's CRL4 adapter function from its kinase-scaffolding function?
choices: A. A DDB1-binding patch mutant has been validated in cells to retain DYRK1A scaffolding while losing CRL4 ubiquitin-ligase activity, confirming that the two functions are separable.
B. Structural analyses show that the DDB1-binding surface and DYRK1A DH-box interface overlap on DCAF7, indicating the two functions are intrinsically inseparable.
C. Separation-of-function mutants targeting the DDB1-binding patch versus the DYRK1A DH-box interface have been proposed but have not yet been experimentally validated.
D. DCAF7-null rescue experiments using a ubiquitylation-dead CUL4B variant demonstrate that CRL4 ligase activity is dispensable for DYRK1A scaffolding in primary cells.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer classifies this as experimentally open: while predicted point mutants in the DDB1-binding patch would selectively disrupt CRL4 adapter function, and mutations at the DYRK1A DH-box interface would selectively disrupt scaffolding, neither class of mutant has been introduced and phenotypically characterized in cells. The separation therefore remains a structural prediction rather than a validated functional distinction.

quizzes.stem: According to the proposal's logic, which single experimental unit is most critical for converting the DCAF7–IFIT3 substrate claim from correlative to mechanistically definitive?
choices: A. In vitro ubiquitination reconstitution with purified DDB1–DCAF7–CUL4, E1/E2, ubiquitin, IFIT3, and ATP demonstrating K48-linked chain formation on IFIT3, paired with a catalytic separation-of-function rescue in cells
B. Co-immunoprecipitation of endogenous DCAF7 and IFIT3 under proteasome inhibition in JAK2V617F cells, confirming a physical substrate interaction
C. Cycloheximide chase assay showing prolonged IFIT3 half-life after DCAF7 depletion, reversed by wild-type but not WD40-domain-mutant DCAF7 re-expression
D. Ubiquitin remnant proteomics (K-ε-GG enrichment) in DCAF7-overexpressing cells identifying the specific lysine residues on IFIT3 that carry K48-linked chains
quizzes.explanation: Only the fully reconstituted in vitro assay — purified DDB1–DCAF7–CUL4 complex with E1/E2/ubiquitin/ATP and recombinant IFIT3 — proves biochemical sufficiency, establishing that this CRL4 adapter alone can install K48 chains on IFIT3 independent of confounding cellular factors. The separation-of-function rescue (catalytic mutant fails to rescue IFIT3 levels and IFN-α output) then converts that biochemical result into causal, cell-level evidence; options B–D all remain correlative because they either show interaction or degradation without proving direct enzymatic activity.

quizzes.stem: Which combination of experiments would most rigorously satisfy a dissertation committee that IFIT3 is a direct substrate of CRL4^DCAF7, rather than an indirect or bystander target?
choices: In vitro ubiquitination reconstituted with purified DDB1–DCAF7 and IFIT3; rescue experiment showing a substrate-binding-deficient DCAF7 mutant fails to reduce IFIT3 levels; and MLN4924 treatment phenocopying DCAF7 depletion on IFIT3 protein.
Co-immunoprecipitation of endogenous DCAF7 with IFIT3 in MPN cells; cycloheximide chase demonstrating extended IFIT3 half-life upon DCAF7 knockdown; and MG132 rescue of IFIT3 in DCAF7-depleted cells.
Denaturing immunoprecipitation of poly-ubiquitinated IFIT3; WT DCAF7 re-expression restoring IFIT3 depletion after knockdown; and DDB1 siRNA phenocopying DCAF7 loss on IFIT3 levels.

Mass spectrometric mapping of IFIT3 ubiquitination sites; IFIT3 lysine-to-arginine point mutants resistant to proteasomal degradation; and proximity ligation assay confirming nuclear DCAF7–IFIT3 co-localization.
