B. As a driver of sustained, supra-basal IFN-α elevation, because only chronic above-threshold IFN output — as exemplified by SCA — is sufficient to produce myeloid skewing
C. As a window-specific gatekeeper acting during the fetal-to-adult HSC transition, when progenitors are transiently hypersensitive to IFN-α before adult bone marrow niches are established
D. As a post-receptor checkpoint controlling IFNAR1 ubiquitylation and recycling, since basal IFN effects are limited by surface receptor density rather than by ligand concentration
quizzes.explanation: Li et al. 2023 demonstrated that changes in basal IFN tone have minimal impact on steady-state adult hematopoiesis, effectively excluding a loss-of-basal-tone model as sufficient to explain pathological myeloid skewing. The SCA precedent shows that only sustained, supra-basal IFN elevation recapitulates that phenotype, positioning DCAF7 as an effector whose dysregulation drives IFN-α above — rather than merely modulates — the physiological set point.
quizzes.citation: Li et al. 2023 Blood Adv · §Fetal-to-Adult Hematopoietic Transition

quizzes.stem: Which of the following correctly pairs the timing of the murine perinatal window with the molecular event uniquely confined to it during the fetal-to-adult HSC transition?
choices: A. E12.5–E16.5; peak Sox17 expression sustains fetal HSC self-renewal in the fetal liver before bone marrow seeding
B. E17.5–postnatal day 3; irreversible downregulation of Lin28b and accumulation of let-7 miRNAs that reprograms HSC identity from fetal to adult
C. Postnatal days 7–21; establishment of HSC quiescence via Cdk6 downregulation as the bone marrow niche matures
D. E10.5–E12.5; Runx1-dependent endothelial-to-hematopoietic transition that generates the first definitive HSCs in the aorta-gonad-mesonephros region
quizzes.explanation: The perinatal window (E17.5–P3) is defined by the extinction of Lin28b and the consequent accumulation of let-7 miRNAs, which together dismantle the fetal transcriptional program (Hmga2, Igf2bp2) and install adult HSC identity; forced Lin28b expression through this window is sufficient to preserve fetal-type self-renewal and lymphoid output, demonstrating that the switch is time-limited and fate-instructive rather than a passive consequence of niche relocation.
quizzes.citation: Copley et al. 2013 Nat Cell Biol · §Fetal-to-Adult HSC Transition

quizzes.stem: In Vav-Cre Dcaf7 conditional knockout mice, hematopoiesis appears grossly normal at E14.5 but is severely disrupted by P4. Public transcriptomic datasets show that Dcaf7 expression peaks at P4. Which statement BEST explains why this temporal pattern supports a stage-specific developmental role rather than a constitutive housekeeping function?
choices: Vav-Cre-mediated recombination is incomplete in fetal liver HSCs at E14.5, so residual DCAF7 protein masks the phenotype until it is fully depleted by P4.
Peak Dcaf7 expression at P4 coincides with the fetal-to-adult hematopoietic transition, indicating that DCAF7 is rate-limiting for adult HSC pool establishment at this specific developmental window.
A redundant DCAF paralog compensates for DCAF7 loss during fetal hematopoiesis but is transcriptionally silenced by P4, unmasking the stage-specific requirement.
DCAF7 protein is post-translationally stabilized in fetal HSCs at E14.5, preserving ubiquitin-ligase activity despite Cre-mediated deletion; the P4 phenotype reflects loss of this compensation.
quizzes.explanation: Because public hematopoietic datasets show Dcaf7 transcript levels peaking precisely at P4, the coincidence of expression maximum and phenotype onset is most parsimoniously explained by a rate-limiting requirement during the fetal-to-adult HSC transition rather than a ubiquitous function. Incomplete Cre recombination, paralog compensation, and post-translational protein stabilization are equally plausible alternative interpretations that must be experimentally excluded before inferring a true developmental role.
quizzes.citation: Vav-Cre Dcaf7 cKO phenotypic data · public HSC transcriptomic datasets · §Fetal-to-Adult Hematopoietic Transition

quizzes.stem: According to Li et al. 2020, which transcription factor motifs are most enriched at enhancers that gain H3K27ac marking during the fetal-to-adult hematopoietic transition, and why is this finding mechanistically significant?
choices: IRF motifs, indicating that the adult hematopoietic chromatin landscape is epigenetically primed for interferon-pathway activation
GATA1/2 motifs, reflecting the dominance of erythroid transcriptional programs in steady-state adult bone marrow
NF-κB motifs, consistent with a constitutively pro-inflammatory enhancer landscape in adult hematopoietic progenitors
ETS/PU.1 motifs, marking the myeloid-biased regulatory architecture established in adult long-term HSCs
quizzes.explanation: Li et al. 2020 identified IRF motif enrichment at adult-activated enhancers, demonstrating that the adult hematopoietic chromatin is pre-configured for interferon signaling; this epigenetic priming means that the chronic low-level IFN-α pulse in JAK2V617F MPN can engage pre-established regulatory elements to sustain IFIT3 upregulation, rather than requiring de novo enhancer activation.
quizzes.citation: Li et al. 2020 · Fetal-to-Adult Hematopoietic Transition §

quizzes.stem: A Vav-Cre–driven conditional knockout (cKO) of a transcription factor is analyzed at postnatal day 4 (P4) and shows reduced HSC frequency in the fetal liver. The perinatal niche harbors a transient Nestin+ NG2+ portal vessel-associated pericyte population at this stage (Khan et al. 2016). What is the primary interpretive challenge this niche poses for the P4 cKO phenotype?
choices: Because Vav-Cre spares the Nestin+ NG2+ pericyte compartment, the phenotype cannot distinguish a cell-intrinsic HSC requirement from a failure of HSC–pericyte niche crosstalk.
Because Nestin+ NG2+ pericytes transiently activate the Vav promoter at P4, pericyte-specific recombination becomes an uncontrolled variable that confounds cell-type assignment of the phenotype.
Because the Nestin+ NG2+ pericyte population does not mature until after P4, its niche signals are temporally irrelevant to phenotypes scored at this time point.
Because Nestin+ NG2+ pericytes secrete redundant HSC maintenance factors including SCF and CXCL12, any cell-intrinsic defect is buffered, causing systematic underestimation of phenotype severity.
quizzes.explanation: Vav-Cre recombines only hematopoietic cells, leaving the transient Nestin+ NG2+ pericyte niche genetically intact and unexamined; any phenotype at P4 could therefore arise from a cell-intrinsic transcription factor requirement within HSCs or from impaired crosstalk between cKO HSCs and this wild-type niche, making the two contributions inseparable without a complementary pericyte-specific genetic tool.
quizzes.citation: Khan et al. 2016 Science · §Fetal-to-Adult Hematopoietic Transition

quizzes.stem: If DCAF7 promotes proteasomal degradation of SOX17, which experimental finding in Dcaf7 cKO fetal HSCs would most directly demonstrate that elevated SOX17 protein reflects post-translational stabilization rather than transcriptional upregulation?
choices: SOX17 protein is elevated at E14.5 and P0 in Dcaf7 cKO, with Sox17 mRNA levels unchanged relative to WT by qRT-PCR
Sox17 mRNA is upregulated at E14.5 in Dcaf7 cKO, with SOX17 protein levels proportionally increased
SOX17 protein half-life is prolonged in Dcaf7 cKO by cycloheximide chase, but Sox17 mRNA is also elevated at the same timepoints
SOX17 protein is absent at adult timepoints in both WT and Dcaf7 cKO, consistent with transcriptional silencing of Sox17 in adult HSCs
quizzes.explanation: Elevated SOX17 protein with no corresponding increase in Sox17 mRNA directly implicates DCAF7-dependent proteolysis rather than transcriptional feedback — precisely the discrimination the model answer demands by pairing protein-level readouts with mRNA quantification. The fetal window (E14.5–P0) is the diagnostic timepoint because Sox17 is transcriptionally active then; adult HSCs silence Sox17 regardless of DCAF7 status, rendering that timepoint uninformative for separating the two mechanisms.
quizzes.citation: Kim, Saunders, Morrison 2007 Cell · §Fetal-to-Adult Hematopoietic Transition

quizzes.stem: Fetal-origin tissue-resident macrophages (microglia, Kupffer cells, alveolar macrophages) are established during the perinatal IFN-α pulse and persist largely without monocyte replacement. In a mouse with DCAF7 conditionally deleted in fetal hematopoietic progenitors, which long-term tissue-macrophage phenotype is most consistent with the DCAF7→IFIT3→IFN-α signaling axis?
choices: A. A persistently hypo-inflammatory set-point with reduced baseline ISG expression, because DCAF7-driven IFN-α output through IFIT3 is required for full macrophage priming during the perinatal seeding window
B. A hyperactivated, M1-skewed phenotype, because DCAF7 normally ubiquitinates and degrades pro-inflammatory NF-κB co-activators in fetal liver progenitors, restraining macrophage activation
C. A phenotypically normal adult macrophage pool, because postnatal bone marrow–derived monocyte waves continuously replenish all tissue-resident compartments and bypass the fetal DCAF7 defect
D. Selective microglial depletion with sparing of Kupffer cells and alveolar macrophages, because CSF1 but not GM-CSF signaling requires DCAF7 for receptor stabilization in the brain niche
quizzes.explanation: Because DCAF7 positively drives IFN-α production via IFIT3, its conditional loss blunts the perinatal IFN pulse at the precise developmental window when fetal macrophage progenitors are seeding tissues, leaving them epigenetically under-primed. Microglia, Kupffer cells, and alveolar macrophages are long-lived, self-renewing populations not replaced by adult bone marrow monocytes, so this hypo-priming persists and is detectable by ATAC-seq or bulk RNA-seq of sorted tissue-resident macrophages from adult cKO versus littermate-control mice.

quizzes.stem: Which statement best characterizes CHIP (Clonal Hematopoiesis of Indeterminate Potential) as a clinical entity?
choices: A. CHIP reflects normal clonal aging dynamics of the stem cell pool but also confers measurable excess cardiovascular and all-cause mortality, making it a clinically meaningful risk-stratification marker even in the absence of hematologic malignancy.
B. CHIP is best understood as a pre-malignant condition analogous to MGUS, in which early cytoreductive intervention is warranted once variant allele frequency exceeds the diagnostic threshold.
C. CHIP is a benign aging epiphenomenon with no clinical consequences unless it progresses to overt cytopenia or bone marrow dysplasia meeting criteria for MDS.
D. CHIP is classified as a clonal myeloid neoplasm because its driver mutations (TET2, DNMT3A, ASXL1) are identical to those found in MDS and MPN.
quizzes.explanation: The term 'indeterminate potential' was coined precisely to hold two simultaneous truths: CHIP arises from normal clonal competition in a long-lived stem cell pool under mutational selection pressure, yet population-level data show excess cardiovascular and all-cause mortality even when overt malignancy never develops. This dual nature distinguishes CHIP from both a purely benign aging variant and a frank pre-malignant disease requiring intervention.

quizzes.stem: Among the four mechanistic hypotheses for DCAF7 function at the perinatal hematopoietic transition — IFIT3 regulation, fetal-specific factor degradation, DYRK1A/HIPK2 scaffolding, and unknown substrate — which is most tractable to address first in Aim 2, and why?
choices: DYRK1A/HIPK2 scaffolding, because co-immunoprecipitation of kinase complexes is technically routine and requires no new substrate identification

IFIT3 regulation, because the DCAF7–IFIT3 axis is already validated in the JAK2V617F context, enabling an immediate antibody-based readout of DCAF7-dependent protein abundance in fetal versus adult HSCs
Fetal-specific factor degradation, because BCL11A and LIN28B are well-characterized candidates whose cycloheximide-chase kinetics can be measured directly in DCAF7-knockout fetal progenitors
Unknown substrate identification, because unbiased ubiquitinome proteomics would simultaneously test all four hypotheses in a single experiment
quizzes.explanation: The IFIT3 regulation hypothesis is most tractable because DCAF7's control of IFIT3 protein levels is already mechanistically established in the JAK2V617F MPN model, providing validated antibodies and a defined protein-abundance readout that can be directly compared between fetal and adult HSC compartments without requiring de novo substrate discovery. This existing foothold minimizes technical risk relative to the other three hypotheses, which each require identifying or confirming a substrate before any mechanistic readout is possible.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · §Fetal-to-Adult Hematopoietic Transition · Aim 2 tractability rationale

quizzes.stem: A patient with JAK2V617F MPN is found to carry a pre-existing DNMT3A-mutant clonal hematopoiesis (CH) clone from which the JAK2V617F subclone was acquired. Which statement best describes how this ancestral DTA clone affects the hypothesis that DCAF7 inhibition will control MPN?
choices: DCAF7 inhibition selectively targets the JAK2V617F clone because the DCAF7–IFIT3 axis is engaged downstream of JAK2V617F; the ancestral DTA clone lacks this activation, will persist after therapy, and can regenerate JAK2V617F disease, requiring a combined DTA-directed modality and independent monitoring of both clone VAFs.

DTA mutations globally impair DNA methylation and thereby sensitize all hematopoietic progenitors, including the DTA clone itself, to DCAF7 inhibition, making a separate anti-DTA strategy unnecessary.

Pre-existing DTA CH accelerates JAK2V617F subclone expansion but does not alter the therapeutic index of DCAF7 inhibition, since clinical endpoints measure only suppression of the dominant JAK2V617F VAF.
DNMT3A and TET2 loss-of-function mutations upregulate interferon-stimulated genes including IFIT3, rendering the DTA clone equally sensitive to DCAF7 inhibition as the JAK2V617F clone.
quizzes.explanation: Because the DCAF7–IFIT3 axis is activated specifically downstream of JAK2V617F, DCAF7 inhibition exerts selective pressure only on the mutant subclone; the ancestral DTA clone, which predates and is mechanistically independent of JAK2V617F, is not targeted and persists as a reservoir for molecular relapse — mandating a complementary DTA-directed intervention (e.g., IL-1β blockade) and dual-clone VAF surveillance.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · General section (DTA persistence & combination strategy)

quizzes.stem: In longitudinal studies of IFN-α therapy in JAK2V617F MPN, which pattern of clonal dynamics best supports the concern that IFN-α may preferentially select for DTA (DNMT3A/TET2/ASXL1) mutant clones?
choices: A. JAK2V617F VAF and DTA VAF decline in parallel, confirming that IFN-α eliminates all malignant subclones with equal efficacy
B. DTA VAF remains stable or increases as JAK2V617F VAF declines, consistent with relative IFN resistance of DTA-mutant progenitors
C. DTA VAF transiently rises during early treatment then normalizes, reflecting inflammatory stem-cell mobilization rather than true clonal selection
D. DTA VAF declines more slowly than JAK2V617F VAF but reaches comparable low levels after extended therapy, indicating delayed but equivalent IFN-α sensitivity
quizzes.explanation: Hasan 2013 and subsequent longitudinal cohorts show that DTA VAF does not fall—and may rise—during IFN-α therapy even as JAK2V617F VAF declines, mirroring the IFN-γ resistance documented in DNMT3A-mutant (Avagyan 2021) and TET2-mutant (Hormaechea-Agulla 2021) stem cells. This divergent clonal kinetic indicates that IFN-based regimens impose selective pressure favoring DTA clones, which can subsequently drive molecular relapse after therapy ends.

quizzes.stem: An MPN patient with pre-existing DNMT3A and JAK2V617F mutations achieves undetectable JAK2V617F by standard qPCR after IFN-α therapy. Which residual disease monitoring strategy is most appropriate going forward?
choices: Declare complete molecular remission and transition to annual surveillance, as JAK2V617F clearance is the primary therapeutic endpoint for IFN-α response

Perform ddPCR for JAK2V617F (~0.01% VAF sensitivity) and a patient-specific assay for the DNMT3A variant, reporting each allele burden separately, since a persistent DNMT3A clone indicates ongoing relapse risk despite JAK2V617F clearance
Monitor IFIT3 mRNA expression in peripheral blood mononuclear cells as a functional surrogate for residual IFN-pathway dysregulation
Combine JAK2V617F and DNMT3A variant allele frequencies into a single composite clonal burden score to streamline longitudinal tracking
quizzes.explanation: ddPCR achieves the 0.01–0.1% VAF sensitivity required to confirm true JAK2V617F clearance, while a separate patient-specific DNMT3A assay tracks the DTA clone independently; because the DNMT3A-mutant clone is a clonal reservoir from which JAK2V617F subclones can re-emerge, a patient with 0% JAK2V617F but 30% DNMT3A VAF remains at lifelong risk and requires sustained surveillance — which merging the two scores into one would obscure.

quizzes.stem: Which statement most accurately describes the current evidence for DCAF7 involvement in clonal hematopoiesis (CH)?
choices: DCAF7 is recurrently mutated in CH, with enrichment in JAK2V617F-positive clones identified across multiple large exome sequencing cohorts.
The Sakaguchi 2013 JMML exome study identified DCAF7 as a recurrent somatic driver, providing the primary published evidence linking it to myeloid clonal evolution.
DCAF7 protein levels are consistently reduced in CH-associated hematopoietic stem cells, implicating expression loss rather than somatic mutation as the mechanism.
DCAF7 has not been reported as a recurrent CH driver in large sequencing cohorts; the Sakaguchi 2013 JMML exome study, sometimes cited as evidence, does not identify DCAF7 as a recurrent lesion.
quizzes.explanation: No large CH cohort has reported DCAF7 as a recurrent somatic driver, and the Sakaguchi 2013 JMML exome study — occasionally invoked in derivative summaries — does not in fact contain DCAF7 as a recurrent lesion. Whether expression- or function-level changes occur in CH-associated conditions remains an open empirical question distinct from mutational evidence.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · §General; Sakaguchi 2013 JMML exome (miscitation flag)

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: A researcher uses Mx1-Cre to delete DCAF7 in hematopoietic cells but then switches to Rosa26-CreERT2 for the definitive Aim 1a experiment. What is the primary reason for this change in the context of a thesis focused on DCAF7–IFIT3–IFN-α signaling in JAK2V617F MPN?
choices: A. Poly(I:C) administration required to induce Mx1-Cre activates TLR3/MDA5/RIG-I and drives endogenous Type I IFN production, which confounds experiments designed to interrogate IFN-responsive biology.
B. Mx1-Cre produces mosaic recombination in long-term hematopoietic stem cells, leaving a DCAF7-expressing reservoir that dilutes the observed phenotype.
C. Rosa26-CreERT2 permits deletion to be induced after JAK2V617F disease is established, enabling temporal dissection of DCAF7 function from its role in disease initiation.
D. Poly(I:C) directly phosphorylates JAK2 through a TLR3-dependent mechanism, creating a spurious JAK2 activation signal that mimics the JAK2V617F gain-of-function.
quizzes.explanation: Mx1-Cre requires poly(I:C) for induction, and poly(I:C) is a potent TLR3/MDA5/RIG-I agonist that stimulates endogenous Type I IFN production; because the thesis centers on IFN-responsive programs (IFIT3, ISG upregulation, JAK2V617F-inflamed states), this induction makes it impossible to establish an IFN-naive baseline. Rosa26-CreERT2 is induced by tamoxifen, which does not activate innate immune sensing pathways, yielding a genuinely IFN-clean experimental arm.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems §Aim 1a

quizzes.stem: To functionally test whether DCAF7 loss alters JAK2V617F clonal trajectory in vivo, which experimental system best captures both the driver-mutation context and the dynamics of clonal competition over time?
choices: DCAF7 conditional knockout mice crossed with Jak2-V617F knock-in mice, followed by serial competitive transplantation with VAF tracking across primary and secondary recipients
Xenograft transplantation of patient-derived JAK2V617F CD34+ cells into NSG mice with inducible DCAF7 shRNA knockdown, measuring chimerism by flow cytometry at 12 weeks
Germline DCAF7 knockout mice transplanted with wild-type competitor bone marrow in a 1:1 ratio, with serial complete blood counts to track hematopoietic output
In vitro co-culture of JAK2V617F and wild-type HSPCs with a DCAF7 pharmacologic inhibitor, quantifying colony-forming unit output at 14 days
quizzes.explanation: Crossing a hematopoietic-specific DCAF7 conditional knockout with the Jak2-V617F knock-in preserves physiologic HSC context and endogenous oncogene expression; serial competitive transplantation then allows VAF tracking across primary and secondary recipients, directly measuring whether DCAF7 loss accelerates or attenuates the clonal advantage conferred by JAK2V617F. The xenograft and germline-KO designs either lack the knock-in driver context or introduce confounds unrelated to DCAF7's cell-autonomous role in CH.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · General §Mouse genetics / competitive transplantation approach

quizzes.stem: DCAF7 loss stabilizes IFIT3 and potentiates interferon signaling. What is the predicted phenotypic outcome of DCAF7 loss in HSCs, and how does it differ between wild-type and JAK2V617F backgrounds?
choices: A. Anti-CH in both backgrounds via distinct mechanisms — IFN-driven HSC exhaustion in WT and IFIT3-mediated sensitization of the JAK2V617F clone to depletion.
B. CH-promoting in JAK2V617F HSCs, because amplified IFIT3 signaling reinforces constitutive JAK-STAT output and sustains proliferative self-renewal.
C. Anti-CH only in WT HSCs via IFN-driven exhaustion; JAK2V617F HSCs are phenotypically unaffected because constitutive JAK2 activity overrides IFIT3-mediated growth suppression.
D. CH-promoting in WT HSCs, because chronically elevated IFN tone selectively expands stress-resistant HSC clones that gain competitive advantage over IFN-sensitive neighbors.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer predicts anti-CH outcomes in both contexts but through mechanistically distinct routes: in WT HSCs, amplified IFN responsiveness drives exhaustion, whereas in JAK2V617F HSCs, the enhanced IFIT3-dependent arm sensitizes the mutant clone to depletion. The context-dependence is why lineage-tracing competitive transplant experiments in DCAF7-floxed mice with and without JAK2V617F are required to distinguish these outcomes empirically.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · General section (DCAF7→IFIT3→IFN-α thesis context)

quizzes.stem: What does VEXAS syndrome most directly demonstrate about the relationship between ubiquitin-proteasome system (UPS) dysfunction and innate immune signaling, and why is this analytically relevant to the DCAF7→IFIT3→IFN-α axis in JAK2V617F MPN?
choices: A somatic UBA1 loss-of-function mutation restricted to myeloid progenitors produces a CH-like clonal architecture in which UPS disruption amplifies rather than suppresses IFN-driven inflammation.
Ubiquitin E1 enzyme dysfunction preferentially activates NFκB rather than JAK-STAT signaling, indicating that IFN upregulation in myeloid clones requires an E3 ligase-independent second hit.
Vacuolization of myeloid precursors caused by UPS failure is the rate-limiting step for clonal expansion in somatic inflammatory hematopoietic syndromes.
Biallelic inactivation of ubiquitin-activating enzymes in hematopoietic stem cells is required to generate the systemic inflammatory phenotype characteristic of clonal hematopoietic disorders.
quizzes.explanation: VEXAS functions as a natural human experiment: a single somatic UBA1 mutation (E1 ubiquitin-activating enzyme) confined to myeloid progenitors is sufficient to create a CH-like clonal state with severe IFN-driven systemic inflammation rather than immune quiescence, directly paralleling the DCAF7-IFIT3 model in which UPS substrate-receptor dysfunction unleashes rather than suppresses interferon amplification.

quizzes.stem: Which statement most accurately captures the mechanistic link between DCAF7 function in JAK2V617F MPN and a unifying interest in ubiquitin-proteasome system (UPS) control of interferon signaling?
choices: A. DCAF7, functioning as a CRL4 substrate receptor, promotes proteasomal degradation of IFIT3; loss of DCAF7 in JAK2V617F MPN stabilizes IFIT3 and amplifies IFN-α output, directly coupling UPS activity to innate-immune tone.
B. IFIT3, functioning as a CRL4 co-adaptor, recruits DCAF7 to ubiquitinate IRF9; constitutive JAK2V617F signaling overrides this axis and drives IFN-α hypersecretion in MPN cells.
C. DCAF7 facilitates ISGylation of IFIT3 rather than its proteasomal degradation; impaired ISGylation in JAK2V617F MPN destabilizes the IFIT3 scaffold and attenuates IFN-α signaling output.
D. DCAF7 acts as a proteasomal chaperone that targets mutant JAK2V617F for degradation; its loss allows JAK2V617F to constitutively activate STAT1 and drive IFN-α transcription in MPN clones.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer specifies that DCAF7 acts as a CRL4 substrate receptor whose job is to degrade IFIT3, an interferon-stimulated scaffold; loss of this E3 activity in JAK2V617F MPN cells stabilizes IFIT3 and amplifies IFN-α output, placing the work squarely at the intersection of CRL biology and innate-immune signaling. Distractors B–D each invert or misidentify the molecular role (substrate vs. receptor, ISGylation vs. ubiquitination, JAK2 vs. IFIT3 as the substrate) in ways consistent with adjacent but incorrect mechanisms.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: In a CreERT2-based model of Jak2V617F-driven MPN, which experimental design best controls for the direct HSC-activating effects of tamoxifen?
choices: Compare tamoxifen-treated Cre-positive mice to untreated, age-matched wild-type littermates to isolate Jak2V617F-driven phenotypes
Use tamoxifen-treated Cre-negative littermates as the primary comparator and allow a 2–4 week washout before phenotypic assays
Switch to a polyI:polyC-inducible Mx1-Cre system to eliminate tamoxifen-related HSC activation entirely
Perform all phenotypic assays within 48 hours of tamoxifen delivery before sustained Jak2V617F-driven clonal expansion can confound the readout
quizzes.explanation: Because tamoxifen itself mobilizes and activates HSCs independently of Cre-mediated recombination (Sanchez-Aguilera 2014, Kim 2013), using tamoxifen-treated Cre-negative littermates as the comparator ensures that observed HSC phenotypes are attributable to Jak2V617F expression rather than the drug. A 2–4 week washout further separates acute tamoxifen effects from the steady-state MPN phenotype before assay.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: A Vav-Cre; Dcaf7^{fl/fl} mouse develops a P4 myeloproliferative phenotype. To determine whether this phenotype is hematopoietic-cell-autonomous rather than a consequence of off-target Cre activity in endothelium, which experiment is most informative?
choices: A. Generate a Tek-Cre; Dcaf7^{fl/fl} line to selectively delete DCAF7 in endothelium and compare vasculature histopathology with the Vav-Cre model
B. Transplant Dcaf7-null fetal liver cells into lethally irradiated wild-type recipients and monitor for myeloproliferative reconstitution
C. Switch to a Rosa26-CreERT2; Dcaf7^{fl/fl} line and induce deletion with tamoxifen in adult mice to limit fetal endothelial recombination
D. Replace Vav-Cre with Vav-iCre, which carries lower endothelial leak, and reassess the myeloproliferative phenotype
quizzes.explanation: Transplanting Dcaf7-null fetal liver cells into wild-type irradiated hosts physically uncouples the hematopoietic from the endothelial compartment; recapitulation of the P4 phenotype in recipients with intact wild-type endothelium directly confirms hematopoietic cell autonomy. The other strategies reduce or characterize endothelial Cre activity but cannot fully dissociate hematopoietic from vascular contributions to the phenotype.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: Which property most directly justifies selecting the Mullally Jak2V617F conditional knock-in allele over the Akada and Li alleles as the primary model for studying DCAF7–IFIT3–IFNα signaling in JAK2V617F MPN?
choices: A. It uses a BAC transgene that preserves endogenous JAK2 promoter stoichiometry, minimizing transgene dosage artifacts relative to single-copy knock-ins.
B. It produces a reproducible polycythemia vera phenotype, is compatible with both Mx1-Cre and Rosa26-CreERT2 driver systems, and carries the largest body of published comparator data.
C. Its Rosa26 locus insertion drives ubiquitous constitutive expression, making it optimal for cell-of-origin tracing experiments without requiring hematopoietic-specific Cre drivers.
D. It encodes a homozygous JAK2V617F configuration that better recapitulates the high mutant allele burden characteristic of secondary myelofibrosis.
quizzes.explanation: The Mullally allele's strength lies in its dual compatibility with inducible Cre systems and its standardized, well-characterized PV phenotype that enables direct comparison with a rich body of published MPN literature. The Akada (BAC, heterozygous) and Li (Rosa26) alleles offer complementary advantages—endogenous stoichiometry and cell-of-origin flexibility, respectively—but provide less standardized experimental backgrounds for mechanistic signaling studies.
quizzes.citation: Mullally 2010; Akada 2010; Li 2010 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: A committee member questions why Rosa26-CreERT2 was chosen over Fgd5-CreER — described as the cleanest HSC-specific driver — for conditional DCAF7 deletion in a JAK2V617F MPN model. What best justifies this choice?
choices: A. Rosa26-CreERT2 enables deletion across both HSCs and multipotent progenitors where DCAF7 may also function, and achieves higher recombination efficiency per HSC than the narrower Fgd5-CreER driver.
B. Fgd5-CreER expression is silenced in JAK2V617F-mutant HSCs, making tamoxifen-induced recombination unreliable in the disease-relevant cell population.
C. Fgd5-CreER marks only quiescent long-term HSCs, missing the cycling short-term HSC compartment that is preferentially expanded by JAK2V617F.
D. Rosa26-CreERT2 avoids the endothelial lineage recombination inherent to Scl-CreER, which would otherwise confound interpretation of hematopoietic-specific MPN phenotypes.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer specifies that Rosa26-CreERT2 was chosen to capture deletion across HSCs and committed progenitors — where DCAF7 may have additional roles beyond the HSC — and because it provides higher recombination efficiency per HSC than narrower drivers. Fgd5-CreER remains a valid complementary experiment for testing strictly HSC-autonomous deletion, not a disqualified tool.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: Which combination of evidence most rigorously establishes that a Dcaf7-floxed allele produces a true null after Cre-mediated excision?
choices: RT-qPCR spanning the excised junction, western blot on FACS-sorted HSPCs with a validated antibody, and cDNA rescue to confirm that the observed phenotype is DCAF7-dependent
Southern blot confirming loxP-flanked segment removal, flow cytometric profiling of HSPC subsets, and comparison of phenotypes to Cre-only littermate controls

RT-qPCR showing reduced Dcaf7 transcript in bulk bone marrow, western blot on total splenocytes with a commercial antibody, and phenotypic severity comparison between heterozygous and homozygous knockouts

Allele-specific PCR confirming greater than 90% recombination efficiency, co-immunoprecipitation to detect residual DCAF7 protein-complex formation, and competitive bone marrow transplantation to quantify functional deficits
quizzes.explanation: A true null requires convergent evidence at three levels: mRNA loss confirmed by RT-qPCR with a primer pair spanning the excised junction (ruling out retained cryptic transcript), protein loss confirmed by western blot on sorted HSPCs with a validated antibody (confirming absence in the relevant cell type), and functional specificity confirmed by cDNA rescue (distinguishing DCAF7-dependent phenotypes from off-target Cre toxicity or haploinsufficiency at flanking loci). The other choices either omit protein-level confirmation in the correct compartment, replace the rescue with a phenotypic dosage comparison, or substitute indirect biochemical readouts that do not prove absence of the protein.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: In a competitive bone marrow transplant experiment, Mercier et al. (2019) showed that CD45.1 congenic cells can carry an independent fitness phenotype that confounds chimerism readouts. Which strategy BEST controls for this confound?
choices: A. Use CD45.1/CD45.2 F1 double-positive cells as competitors so any background fitness effect is distributed symmetrically across compartments, or perform arm-reversal transplants placing the test mutation on the CD45.1 background.
B. Normalize competitor cell input to equal phenotypic LSK (Lin−Sca1+cKit+) frequency between CD45.1 and CD45.2 donors to correct for stem cell pool size differences.
C. Use age-matched, littermate-derived CD45.1 competitors in every experiment to minimize donor-to-donor engraftment variation within the CD45.1 colony.
D. Replace the competitive transplant design with serial non-competitive transplants and use quantitative PCR-based chimerism to track test cell expansion independently of a competitor.
quizzes.explanation: CD45.1 congenic mice carry SJL-derived background variants that can confer an intrinsic engraftment or self-renewal advantage unrelated to the test allele; the F1 double-positive competitor strategy distributes this fitness effect equally across both test and competitor compartments, while arm-reversal transplants directly test whether observed fitness advantages track with the mutation rather than the congenic marker. Normalizing by LSK frequency (B) addresses stem cell number but not intrinsic fitness, and serial non-competitive transplants (D) measure a different parameter rather than controlling the confound within the competitive assay.
quizzes.citation: Mercier 2019 Exp Hematol · competitive transplant controls

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: Your scRNA-seq data reveal expansion of JAK2V617F hematopoietic progenitors, but population-level sequencing cannot distinguish whether this reflects uniform proliferation across many independent clones or dominance by a small number of founder clones. Which experimental strategy would most directly resolve this ambiguity?
choices: A. Re-cluster the existing scRNA-seq dataset at higher resolution and apply differential abundance testing to detect rare dominant subpopulations
B. Apply mitochondrial DNA somatic variant calling on the existing scRNA-seq reads to infer clonal relationships retrospectively
C. Introduce a Cre-dependent stochastic multicolor (Confetti) reporter to label individual progenitors and measure clone sizes by fluorescence imaging
D. Transplant lentiviral-barcoded (LARRY-style) JAK2V617F hematopoietic stem cells and track individual clone contributions prospectively by barcode sequencing
quizzes.explanation: Population-level scRNA-seq assigns transcriptional identity but not clonal membership, so apparent expansion could arise from many small equal-sized clones or a single dominant founder—two biologically distinct scenarios. LARRY-style lentiviral barcoding uniquely marks each founding HSC before transplant, so every daughter cell inherits a heritable barcode that unambiguously links it to its clonal ancestor, directly resolving uniform expansion from clonal dominance.
quizzes.citation: Weinreb et al. 2020 Cell (LARRY) · Model answer §clonal-dynamics-stretch-goal

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: Which experimental design strategy best controls for microbiome-driven phenotypic variability when comparing conditional knockout mice to littermate controls in a conventional facility?
choices: A. Use a heterozygous intercross breeding scheme so that animals of all genotypes are born in the same litter and co-housed from weaning, ensuring identical cage-of-origin microbiome exposure.
B. Administer broad-spectrum antibiotics to all experimental and control animals for two weeks before endpoint to deplete commensal bacteria equally across genotypes.
C. Rederive all mouse lines into germ-free isolators and colonize them with a defined synthetic microbiome prior to phenotypic assessment.
D. Perform 16S rRNA sequencing on cecal contents at endpoint to confirm that microbiome composition is matched between experimental and control cohorts before interpreting results.
quizzes.explanation: Heterozygous intercross breeding places animals of every genotype in the same litter and cage from weaning, making microbiome exposure identical across experimental and control groups by design rather than by measurement or chemical perturbation. Antibiotic treatment and germ-free rederivation alter the biological context being studied, while sequencing documents variability after the fact without preventing it.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · §Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 2 · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems
quizzes.stem: Before committing to definitive bone marrow transplant cohorts, you need to confirm Cre recombination efficiency in the relevant progenitor compartment. Which approach provides rigorous per-cell quantification in sorted HSPCs?
choices: A. Cross to R26-mTmG; quantify GFP-positive cells in sorted HSPCs by flow cytometry, confirmed by excision-sensitive qPCR on sorted LSK cells
B. Cross to R26-LacZ; measure bulk β-galactosidase activity in unfractionated bone marrow lysates by colorimetric assay
C. Amplify genomic DNA from unfractionated whole bone marrow by PCR with primers flanking the floxed region to detect the deleted band
D. Cross to R26-tdTomato; quantify tdTomato-positive cells in sorted HSPCs by flow cytometry
quizzes.explanation: The R26-mTmG dual reporter switches membrane-Tomato to membrane-GFP upon Cre-mediated excision, enabling per-cell quantification by flow cytometry in sorted HSPCs; orthogonal excision-sensitive qPCR on sorted LSK cells provides molecular confirmation, and a pre-defined >80% threshold gates entry into definitive cohorts. Bulk assays (B, C) lack per-cell resolution in the HSPC compartment, and single-color tdTomato (D) cannot distinguish recombined from unrecombined cells without the built-in Tomato-to-GFP switch.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Mouse Genetics and Cre Recombinase Systems

quizzes.stem: In a bottom-up LC-MS/MS proteomics workflow, after database searching with MSFragger or Andromeda, which approach is used to filter peptide-spectrum matches (PSMs) to an acceptable false discovery rate?
choices: PSMs are scored against both a target (UniProt human) and a reversed-sequence decoy database, accepting hits at the threshold where decoy matches constitute 1% of total accepted PSMs
PSMs are ranked by Mascot ion score and filtered with a fixed threshold of >20, independent of the observed decoy hit rate
Each PSM is assigned a p-value from a Poisson model of random fragment co-occurrence and corrected with Benjamini-Hochberg at q < 0.01
PSMs are accepted only when the same peptide sequence is independently identified in two or more MS2 spectra within the same run
quizzes.explanation: The target-decoy strategy uses reversed protein sequences as a null model: the score cutoff is set so that decoy hits represent ≤1% of accepted PSMs, directly estimating peptide-level FDR. Peptides passing this filter are then assembled into proteins via parsimony rules and subjected to a second 1% protein-level FDR filter.

quizzes.stem: In a study characterizing CRL4-DCAF7 as the E3 ligase responsible for IFIT3 turnover, which single experiment would most decisively establish direct catalytic activity independent of cellular context?
choices: A. In vitro ubiquitination assay using purified CRL4-DCAF7 complex and recombinant IFIT3 as substrate
B. K-to-R mutagenesis of mapped ubiquitination sites on IFIT3, followed by re-expression in DCAF7-KO cells
C. Co-immunoprecipitation of endogenous DCAF7 with IFIT3 in JAK2V617F-expressing hematopoietic cells
D. Ubiquitin remnant profiling (K-ε-GG enrichment mass spectrometry) comparing DCAF7-KO versus wild-type cells
quizzes.explanation: The in vitro ubiquitination assay with purified components is decisive because it demonstrates that CRL4-DCAF7 alone is sufficient for catalytic transfer of ubiquitin onto IFIT3, eliminating any contribution from bystander cellular ligases. K-to-R mutagenesis (B) is the strongest complementary experiment but depends on cellular context and requires prior site mapping, making it logically downstream of the biochemical reconstitution.

quizzes.stem: Aim 1b uses TMT-SPS-MS3 as the primary K-εGG strategy yet retains a separate SILAC K-εGG run. What is the principal justification for including a SILAC-based experiment rather than consolidating all K-εGG profiling into the TMT 16-plex?
choices: A. SILAC provides the highest quantitative precision for a simple two-condition contrast, and the confirmatory design is methodologically coherent because IFIT3 was already identified as a hit in prior SILAC whole-proteome profiling.

B. SILAC pulse-chase labeling can distinguish newly assembled ubiquitin conjugates from pre-existing steady-state substrates, capturing the kinetics of JAK2V617F-driven ubiquitin remodeling that isobaric tags cannot resolve.

C. TMT-SPS-MS3 introduces ratio compression severe enough to render fold-change estimates unreliable for low-stoichiometry ubiquitination events, making SILAC the only quantitatively valid platform for this comparison.
D. TMT reagents acylate lysine ε-amines before trypsin digestion, chemically occluding the diGly remnant tag and preventing efficient K-εGG antibody enrichment in multiplexed experiments.
quizzes.explanation: For a binary WT-vs-KO comparison, SILAC avoids the co-isolation interference that limits isobaric quantification and achieves superior precision on a simple two-condition contrast; retaining SILAC for the confirmatory K-εGG run is additionally justified because IFIT3 first emerged as a hit at the whole-proteome level by SILAC, making cross-platform confirmation methodologically coherent.

quizzes.stem: The Buckley et al. (Cell Stem Cell 2012) K-GG ubiquitinomics study mapped ubiquitination sites across pluripotent stem cells. Which statement best describes their key finding regarding cMyc?
choices: A. FBXW7 ubiquitinates cMyc on a Thr58 phospho-degron, targeting it for proteasomal degradation and thereby regulating pluripotency
B. USP28 removes Lys48-linked chains from cMyc at Ser62, stabilizing cMyc to sustain self-renewal transcription programs in HSCs
C. HUWE1 conjugates Lys63-linked ubiquitin chains to cMyc, redirecting it toward transcriptional co-activation rather than proteasomal degradation
D. SCF–β-TrCP targets cMyc via a Ser62 phospho-degron to promote differentiation over self-renewal in hematopoietic progenitors
quizzes.explanation: Buckley et al. used diGly-remnant immunoprecipitation mass spectrometry to identify 2,578 ubiquitination sites in pluripotent cells, among which FBXW7-mediated ubiquitination of cMyc at the Thr58 phospho-degron was pinpointed as a regulator of pluripotency. This reframed the UPS as an active axis of stem-cell identity rather than a passive housekeeping degradation system.
quizzes.citation: Buckley et al., Cell Stem Cell 2012 · §Ubiquitinomics Methodology

quizzes.stem: In a target-decoy proteomics database search, the false discovery rate (FDR) at a given score threshold is estimated as:
choices: A. The number of decoy peptide-spectrum matches divided by the number of target peptide-spectrum matches surviving that threshold
B. The number of decoy peptide-spectrum matches divided by the combined total of decoy and target peptide-spectrum matches surviving that threshold
C. One minus the fraction of target peptide-spectrum matches whose posterior error probability, computed from a mixture model of correct and incorrect score distributions, falls below 0.05
D. The fraction of target peptide-spectrum matches with a score lower than the median score observed among all decoy peptide-spectrum matches
quizzes.explanation: Reversed-sequence decoys cannot produce genuine peptide matches, so every decoy PSM is by construction a false identification; dividing decoy PSMs by target PSMs at a score threshold (e.g., 100/10,000 = 1%) directly estimates the proportion of target PSMs that are also false. This exploits the assumption that incorrect target matches and decoy matches share the same score distribution at any given threshold.

quizzes.stem: When configuring a K-εGG ubiquitin remnant experiment in MaxQuant, which combination of variable modification mass and tryptic search setting is correct for the diglycine-tagged lysine?
choices: A. +114.04293 Da on Lys as a variable modification, with one additional missed cleavage allowed at modified sites
B. +114.04293 Da on Lys as a variable modification, with no additional missed cleavages required
C. +72.021 Da on Lys as a variable modification, with one additional missed cleavage allowed at modified sites
D. +383.228 Da on Lys as a variable modification, with two additional missed cleavages allowed at modified sites
quizzes.explanation: Trypsin cleaves the isopeptide bond between Ub-G76 and the substrate Lys, leaving the C-terminal Gly75–Gly76 dipeptide covalently attached (+114.04293 Da). Because this diglycine tag occludes the modified Lys from trypsin recognition, the peptide carries one obligate missed cleavage that must be explicitly allowed in the MaxQuant search.
quizzes.citation: Ubiquitinomics Methodology · K-εGG search parameters (model answer)

quizzes.stem: Which combination of evidence most strongly distinguishes IFIT3 as a high-confidence DCAF7 substrate over other hits identified in the SILAC proteomics screen?
choices: Protein stabilization in DCAF7-KO without a matched transcript change in companion RNA-seq, extended half-life by CHX chase, pilot co-IP with DCAF7, and coherent placement in the IFN-stimulated gene pathway — all criteria satisfied simultaneously
The largest fold-change enrichment ratio in SILAC proteomics across biological replicates, exceeding all other candidate substrates in the screen
Concurrent upregulation of IFIT3 mRNA in companion RNA-seq, supporting a transcriptional mechanism downstream of DCAF7 loss
Documented ubiquitination sites on IFIT3 in curated proteomics databases, providing orthogonal support for proteasomal targeting independent of the screen
quizzes.explanation: IFIT3 is distinguished by satisfying every required line of evidence at once: the absence of a transcript change in RNA-seq rules out transcriptional artifacts and confirms post-translational stabilization, CHX chase independently validates slower degradation in DCAF7-KO cells, and pilot co-IP confirms physical association — while the other screen hits satisfy only a subset of these criteria.

quizzes.stem: In a K-GG diGly proteomics experiment comparing DCAF7-KO to wildtype cells, which combination of criteria best identifies a protein as a direct CRL4-DCAF7 substrate rather than an indirect bystander?
choices: Decreased K-εGG signal in DCAF7-KO, increased protein abundance in both DCAF7-KO and MLN4924-treated cells, and physical association with DCAF7 by IP-MS or TurboID proximity labeling
Decreased K-εGG signal in DCAF7-KO and unchanged mRNA level by RNA-seq, confirming that stabilization is post-transcriptional rather than transcriptionally driven
Increased protein abundance in both DCAF7-KO and MLN4924-treated cells plus enrichment in DCAF7 IP-MS, without requiring a change at a specific K-εGG site
Decreased K-εGG signal in DCAF7-KO and physical interaction with DCAF7 by IP-MS, without requiring an independent CRL-inactivation stability test
quizzes.explanation: The model answer requires all three filters simultaneously: site-level evidence (K-εGG decrease in DCAF7-KO), protein-level evidence (abundance increase in both DCAF7-KO and the pan-CRL inhibitor MLN4924), and physical proximity (IP-MS or TurboID). Indirect substrates — stabilized because DCAF7-KO perturbs another E3 or a transcription factor — will pass one or two filters but fail the physical interaction criterion or, definitively, in vitro reconstitution with purified CRL4-DCAF7.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Ubiquitinomics Methodology §direct vs. indirect substrates

quizzes.stem: Your K-εGG proteomics identifies IFIT3 peptides whose diglycine signal decreases in DCAF7-knockout HEL cells. A committee member notes that HERC5 co-translationally ISGylates interferon-stimulated genes and that ISG15 leaves an identical diglycine tryptic remnant on target lysines. Which experiment most directly establishes that the observed K-εGG modification on IFIT3 derives from ubiquitin rather than ISG15?
choices: Immunoprecipitate IFIT3 from DCAF7-knockout lysates and immunoblot sequentially with anti-ubiquitin (FK2) and anti-ISG15 antibodies to compare relative high-molecular-weight smear intensities.
Deplete ISG15 by siRNA in wild-type HEL cells and confirm that DCAF7-dependent K-εGG sites on IFIT3 are unchanged, ruling out ISGylation as their source.
Reconstitute IFIT3 modification in vitro with purified CRL4A-DDB1-DCAF7-RBX1 and recombinant ubiquitin in the complete absence of ISG15, directly confirming modifier identity.
Treat HEL cells with MLN4924 and confirm that IFIT3 K-εGG signal decreases in parallel with DCAF7-knockout, demonstrating CRL-scaffold dependence rather than HECT-E3-mediated ISGylation.
quizzes.explanation: An in vitro reconstitution with only purified CRL4A-DCAF7 machinery and recombinant ubiquitin physically excludes ISG15 from the reaction, proving by defined-component sufficiency that DCAF7-CRL4 alone deposits the K-εGG mark on IFIT3. Cell-based approaches such as ISG15 siRNA or MLN4924 are corroborating controls but do not directly demonstrate which modifier is installed by which E3; only the biochemical reconstitution unambiguously establishes modifier identity.

quizzes.stem: In K-εGG diglycine-remnant proteomics of IFIT3, which biochemical property of ISG15 creates a confound that prevents unambiguous assignment of a detected K-εGG signal to ubiquitination?
choices: ISG15 is cleaved by trypsin to yield an identical diglycine (GG) remnant on modified lysines as ubiquitin does, so K-εGG enrichment cannot distinguish ISGylation from ubiquitination by mass spectrometry alone.

HERC5 ISGylates IFIT3 co-translationally at the same lysine residues targeted by CRL4-DCAF7, creating direct competition for substrate occupancy that suppresses measurable ubiquitination stoichiometry in K-εGG proteomics.
MLN4924 inhibits both CRL-mediated ubiquitination and HERC5-mediated ISGylation by blocking the shared NEDD8/ISG15 E1-activating enzyme, making it unable to serve as a CRL-specific positive control.

ISG15 conjugation to IFIT3 recruits USP18, which cleaves K48-linked ubiquitin chains at DCAF7-targeted lysines, causing ISGylation and ubiquitination to vary inversely and produce indistinguishable K-εGG profiles under DCAF7 perturbation.
quizzes.explanation: ISG15 is cleaved by trypsin to leave the same two-glycine (GG) remnant as ubiquitin, so diglycine-remnant enrichment is blind to modifier identity; because HERC5 co-translationally ISGylates ISGs including IFIT3, every K-εGG site on IFIT3 could reflect ISGylation rather than ubiquitination, requiring ISG15-null cells and in vitro reconstitution with purified recombinant ubiquitin to unambiguously assign the modifier.

quizzes.stem: In a SILAC-based K-εGG ubiquitin-remnant proteomics study of JAK2V617F signaling, what is the primary rationale for using HEL cells as the discovery platform rather than primary MPN CD34+ cells?
choices: HEL cells yield sufficient protein mass (≥10 mg) for K-εGG IP, support SILAC metabolic labeling, and tolerate multi-week CRISPR expansion for DCAF7-KO clones — constraints that primary MPN CD34+ cells cannot satisfy due to limiting yield (<1 mg per aspirate), inability to incorporate isotopic amino acids, and intolerance of prolonged culture.

Primary MPN CD34+ cells are predominantly JAK2V617F heterozygous, producing insufficient constitutive IFN-α/β output to drive detectable steady-state IFIT3 ubiquitination above background noise in K-εGG immunoprecipitation.

The diGly-remnant antibody cross-reacts with ISG15-modified lysines, and primary CD34+ cells express higher ISG15 than HEL cells, inflating apparent ubiquitination stoichiometry and precluding reliable SILAC ratio measurement.

Primary MPN CD34+ cells differentiate toward mature erythroid progeny under the culture conditions required for SILAC labeling, shifting the proteome away from the stem/progenitor state in which JAK2V617F-driven IFN signaling and DCAF7 activity are operative.
quizzes.explanation: K-εGG IP requires ≥10 mg total input protein, but a single MPN aspirate yields <1 mg from CD34+ cells; primary cells also cannot sustain the metabolic isotope incorporation required by SILAC or the multi-week culture required to establish isogenic DCAF7-KO clones by CRISPR. HEL cells satisfy all three constraints as a homozygous, chronically IFN-active JAK2V617F line, making them the appropriate discovery vehicle before validation in primary material by LFQ and CHX-Western.

quizzes.stem: In a SILAC-based K-εGG ubiquitinomic screen for CRL4^DCAF7 substrates, candidate X shows a 1.8-fold decrease in diGly-peptide abundance across biological triplicates (BH-adjusted p = 0.03) and passes IP-MS co-precipitation, CHX chase, and in vitro ubiquitination assays. Candidate Y shows a 5-fold decrease but fails all three orthogonal assays. Which conclusion best reflects best-practice substrate validation?
choices: Candidate Y is the stronger substrate because its 5-fold change far exceeds the standard ≥2× threshold, outweighing its failure of orthogonal assays
Candidate X is better supported because orthogonal validation (IP-MS, CHX chase, in vitro ubiquitination) is weighted more heavily than fold-change magnitude alone
Neither candidate qualifies until both exceed a strict 2× fold-change threshold with BH-adjusted p < 0.05 across all biological replicates
Statistical reproducibility across triplicates is the primary validation criterion, making fold-change magnitude a secondary consideration for both candidates
quizzes.explanation: Although initial filters apply a ≥1.5–2× fold-change cutoff with BH-adjusted p < 0.05 across biological triplicates, orthogonal evidence—MLN4924 phenocopy, IP-MS physical interaction, CHX chase, and in vitro ubiquitination—is explicitly weighted above raw magnitude; a reproducible 1.8× hit that clears orthogonal tests outweighs a 5× change that fails them.

quizzes.stem: For a multiplexed ubiquitinomics experiment comparing DCAF7-WT versus knockout JAK2V617F HEL cells using diGly-K enrichment, which acquisition strategy and instrument pairing best ensures quantitative accuracy for isobaric-labeled samples?
choices: TMTpro-16plex with SPS-MS3 on an Orbitrap Exploris 480 or Orbitrap Astral, searched with MaxQuant or FragPipe TMT-Integrator at 1% peptide and site FDR
SILAC K-εGG labeling with DDA on an Orbitrap Exploris 480, used as the primary multiplexed quantification strategy across all 16 conditions
TMTpro-16plex with MS2-level HCD fragmentation on a Q Exactive HF, searched with Proteome Discoverer at standard FDR thresholds
Data-independent acquisition (DIA) with a pre-built spectral library on an Orbitrap Astral, using TMT tags solely for sample pooling
quizzes.explanation: SPS-MS3 isolates TMT reporter ions through a third fragmentation stage, eliminating ratio compression caused by co-isolated co-eluting peptides that systematically distorts quantification in MS2-level TMT workflows. SILAC K-εGG provides high-precision binary validation but does not scale to 16-plex multiplexing and is designated the confirmatory rather than primary strategy.

quizzes.stem: In the ubiquitinomics experimental design described, what is the correct replication scheme for the TMT-16plex and SILAC arms?
choices: Two biological replicates per TMT condition, each defined as a technical repeat from the same cell passage, and a single forward-labeled SILAC experiment
Three biological replicates per TMT condition, each from an independent cell passage and independent lysis, paired with three label-swap SILAC replicates (heavy-KO/light-WT and its inverse)
Four biological replicates per TMT condition pooled before digestion to reduce technical noise, with SILAC label-swap performed only once
Three biological replicates per TMT condition processed from a single pooled cell lysis, and two SILAC replicates without label swapping
quizzes.explanation: The design requires a minimum of three biological replicates per TMT condition, where 'biological replicate' is explicitly defined as an independent cell passage coupled with independent lysis — not a technical repeat. The SILAC arm complements this with three label-swap replicates (heavy-KO/light-WT plus the reciprocal labeling) to control for label-incorporation artifacts and confirm directionality of ubiquitylation changes.

quizzes.stem: A researcher performs K-εGG immunoprecipitation-based ubiquitinomics from 15 mg of HEL-cell lysate using TMT-SPS-MS3 quantification and high-pH reverse-phase fractionation on a modern Orbitrap platform. Approximately how many K-εGG sites across how many proteins should a well-executed experiment yield?
choices: A. ~2,000–3,000 sites across ~800–1,500 proteins
B. ~5,000–8,000 sites across ~2,000–3,000 proteins
C. ~10,000–20,000 sites across ~4,000–6,000 proteins
D. ~35,000–50,000 sites across ~10,000–15,000 proteins
quizzes.explanation: Modern TMT-SPS-MS3 with deep high-pH fractionation routinely identifies 10,000–20,000 K-εGG sites across 4,000–6,000 proteins from ~15 mg input; the lower range (~2,578 sites) reflects early-era Orbitrap conditions as in Buckley 2012, before multiplexed quantification and extensive fractionation became standard, not a benchmark for current instruments.

quizzes.stem: Which combination of evidence most directly establishes that DCAF7 functions as a substrate receptor for CRL4 rather than a different cullin-RING ligase?
choices: A. An F-box motif enabling SKP1 interaction, co-IP recovery of endogenous CUL1, and reconstituted ubiquitination with recombinant CUL1–SKP1–RBX1
B. A WDxR motif enabling DDB1 interaction, co-IP recovery of endogenous DDB1 and CUL4, and reconstituted ubiquitination with recombinant CUL4A–DDB1–RBX1
C. A BTB domain enabling direct CUL3 interaction, co-IP recovery of endogenous CUL3, and reconstituted ubiquitination with recombinant CUL3–RBX1
D. A SOCS box enabling elongin B/C interaction, co-IP recovery of endogenous CUL2, and reconstituted ubiquitination with recombinant CUL2–elongin B/C–RBX2
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7 harbors the canonical WDxR motif that defines DDB1-binding DCAFs; co-IP of endogenous DDB1 and CUL4 provides the physical signature of a CRL4 complex, and in vitro reconstitution with recombinant CUL4A–DDB1–RBX1 confirms the specific cullin scaffold identity. The other choices describe bona fide substrate-receptor signatures for CRL1 (F-box/SKP1), CRL3 (BTB/CUL3), and CRL2 (SOCS box/elongin B/C), each with its own cognate cullin.

quizzes.stem: Aim 1a yields a null result: conditional Dcaf7 knockout fails to ameliorate MPN in Jak2V617F mice. Which response strategy best preserves the scientific viability of the thesis?
choices: Repeat the conditional KO using an inducible Cre driver to achieve complete recombination, treating incomplete deletion as the most probable technical explanation for the null result.

Treat the null mouse result as informative, pivot the in vivo arm to a transplant-based acute-induction system, and strengthen the thesis through converging pharmacologic (BC18630), biochemical, and human primary CD34+ CRISPR-KO evidence arms.
Conclude that DCAF7 is dispensable for MPN maintenance in vivo and reframe the thesis around IFIT3 as the proximal effector, supported by the existing pharmacologic and biochemical data.
Abandon the in vivo mouse arm entirely and reorient the thesis around BC18630 pharmacology in iPSC-derived CD34+ organoids as the primary evidence for therapeutic relevance.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer's core principle is that a thesis built on converging genetic, pharmacologic, biochemical, and human cell-based evidence survives a null in any single arm; the null result itself is informative — it constrains whether the relevant cellular context is fetal-derived HSC, microenvironmental, or both — rather than negating the DCAF7–IFIT3 hypothesis. Pivoting to a transplant-based acute-induction system eliminates systemic Jak2V617F developmental confounds while the remaining arms (BC18630 rescue, Aim 1b biochemistry, primary CD34+ CRISPR competition assay) maintain independent mechanistic and translational weight.
quizzes.citation: Model Answer · §Aim 1a null contingency / Alternative Experimental Approaches

quizzes.stem: If the Li 2020 perinatal IFN pulse cannot be reproduced, which contingency most directly tests whether the Aim 2 phenotype (DCAF7 essentiality at P4) is IFN-dependent?
choices: A. Cross Dcaf7-Vav-Cre mice with Ifnar1^-/- mice to assess whether the P4 essentiality phenotype is rescued by IFN receptor ablation
B. Redirect Aim 2 to HMGA2 as the primary DCAF7 substrate driving fetal HSC self-renewal, independent of IFN signaling
C. Reconstitute the perinatal IFN pulse in Dcaf7-Vav-Cre pups by administering recombinant IFN-α at P0–P4
D. Test SOX17 as an alternative DCAF7 substrate mediating the fetal-to-adult HSC transition without an IFN requirement
quizzes.explanation: The Ifnar1^-/- × Dcaf7-Vav-Cre cross genetically decouples the IFN-dependence question from whether Li 2020's specific perinatal pulse is reproducible, providing a more rigorous upstream test of the signaling axis. Only if the phenotype persists despite IFN receptor loss do HMGA2 (fetal HSC self-renewal) and SOX17 (fetal-to-adult switch) become leading alternative substrate candidates for DCAF7's perinatal function.
quizzes.citation: Model Answer · Aim 2 Contingency — Li 2020 backup frames

quizzes.stem: In a diGly (K-εGG) ubiquitinomics experiment comparing DCAF7-KO to isogenic WT JAK2V617F cells, which result would most directly falsify the model that CRL4-DCAF7 ubiquitinates IFIT3 at defined lysine residues to promote its proteasomal degradation?
choices: A. IFIT3 K-εGG peptide abundance is significantly reduced in DCAF7-KO relative to WT after normalization to total IFIT3 protein level
B. MLN4924 treatment of DCAF7-WT cells phenocopies DCAF7-KO in reducing normalized IFIT3 K-εGG site occupancy
C. IFIT3 K-εGG site abundance is statistically unchanged in DCAF7-KO relative to WT cells after normalization to total IFIT3 protein level
D. IFIT3 K48-linked polyubiquitin chains are selectively abolished in DCAF7-KO cells while K63-linked chains are preserved
quizzes.explanation: CRL4-DCAF7 is proposed as the substrate receptor that presents IFIT3 to the CRL4 E3 machinery; if K-εGG site occupancy on IFIT3—normalized to protein abundance—is statistically unchanged upon DCAF7 deletion, those ubiquitin marks are deposited independently of DCAF7, severing the direct catalytic connection central to the model. Options A, B, and D each describe results consistent with DCAF7-dependent ubiquitination of IFIT3 and would support rather than falsify the hypothesis.

quizzes.stem: A committee member challenges co-immunoprecipitation as the primary method for detecting DCAF7-IFIT3 association, arguing that CRL-substrate interactions are too transient for reliable pulldown. What is the most defensible experimental response?
choices: Pretreat cells with MG132 to block proteasomal degradation of IFIT3, accumulating it at DCAF7 long enough for co-IP to serve as the definitive primary assay.
Use co-IP only as an initial screen; designate TurboID proximity labeling as the primary approach, with in vitro reconstitution using purified components as a third option.
Crosslink cells with DSP prior to lysis to stabilize transient DCAF7-IFIT3 complexes, validating co-IP as the primary readout before considering alternatives.
Replace TurboID with BioID2, whose extended labeling window provides broader cumulative capture of transient CRL-substrate contacts than rapid proximity labeling.
quizzes.explanation: Because CRL-substrate interactions are short-lived, co-IP is deprioritized to a first-pass screen only; TurboID's rapid proximity-dependent biotinylation (~10 min labeling) captures fleeting DCAF7-IFIT3 contacts in living cells without requiring stable complex formation, while in vitro reconstitution with purified components provides mechanistic confirmation as a third tier.

quizzes.stem: Which of the following best justifies using Vav-Cre and Rosa26-CreER in a sequential rather than parallel experimental design for studying DCAF7 in hematopoiesis?
choices: A. Sequential design concentrates resources on Vav-Cre as the discovery experiment establishing DCAF7 essentiality; Rosa26-CreER then serves as a quantitative extension distinguishing adult from perinatal hematopoietic requirements, avoiding tripling of colony and scRNA-seq costs without proportional gain in scientific yield.
B. Tamoxifen required for Rosa26-CreER induction produces myelosuppression that would confound direct phenotypic comparisons with constitutive Vav-Cre knockouts if both cohorts are run concurrently.

C. Recombination efficiency in HSCs differs substantially between the two Cre drivers, rendering parallel phenotypic comparisons statistically underpowered without substantially enlarged cohort sizes.

D. Rosa26-CreER produces incomplete deletion in adult HSCs, so its phenotypes cannot be meaningfully interpreted without first establishing a complete loss-of-function baseline from constitutive Vav-Cre experiments.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer frames Vav-Cre as the discovery experiment — it establishes whether DCAF7 is essential in hematopoiesis at all — while Rosa26-CreER is a contingent, quantitative extension that tests whether the requirement is adult-specific or perinatal. Because the second experiment's scientific value depends on the first demonstrating essentiality, running both simultaneously triples resource expenditure without tripling yield.

quizzes.stem: Which pair of pre-registered criteria constitute the go/no-go decision for advancing BC18630 in Aim 1c?
choices: A. IC50 < 1 μM in primary MPN CD34+ CFU AND loss of BC18630 activity in IFIT3 knockdown cells
B. IC50 < 10 μM in primary MPN CD34+ CFU AND loss of BC18630 activity in Dcaf7 KO cells
C. IC50 < 10 μM in JAK2V617F cell lines AND rescue of DCAF7 protein levels confirmed by immunoblot
D. IC50 < 50 μM in primary MPN CD34+ CFU AND reduction in phospho-STAT5 as the on-target readout
quizzes.explanation: The go/no-go requires both a potency threshold (IC50 < 10 μM in clinically relevant primary MPN CD34+ colony-forming units, not just immortalized lines) and genetic on-target confirmation (activity is lost in Dcaf7 KO cells, ruling out off-target killing). Achieving the IC50 only in cell lines — but not primary cells — triggers a pre-specified pivot to BC18813 or the dTAG-Dcaf7 genetic-pharmacologic approach.
quizzes.citation: Model Answer · Aim 1c · Alternative Experimental Approaches

quizzes.stem: If a Dcaf7 single knockout in JAK2V617F HSCs produces no detectable phenotype, which experimental approach most directly tests whether DCAF6 or DCAF8 compensates for DCAF7 loss?
choices: A. Apply a DCAF-focused small-molecule inhibitor panel (e.g., Lear 2025 compounds) to measure pan-DCAF functional activity and identify overlapping family members
B. Perform a genome-wide CRISPR synthetic lethality screen in the Dcaf7 KO background to discover which paralog(s) sustain proliferation
C. Execute simultaneous (CoDrive) CRISPR knockout of DCAF7 and its most highly expressed homolog (DCAF6 or DCAF8) in HSCs
D. Conduct sequential single-gene CRISPR knockouts of DCAF7 then DCAF6, comparing phenotype magnitude after each individual deletion
quizzes.explanation: The CoDrive paired-KO strategy eliminates DCAF7 and its closest expressed paralog in the same cellular background simultaneously, directly unmasking compensation that would be invisible in either single KO; existing single-KO phenotype data from the lab argue against complete redundancy but do not rule out underestimated paralog buffering that only paired deletion would reveal.
quizzes.citation: Model answer §A · Alternative Experimental Approaches (CoDrive approach)

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: If BC18630, the small-molecule DCAF7 inhibitor used throughout several thesis aims, were rendered unusable due to off-target concerns or supplier retraction, which feature of the experimental design would most directly preserve the mechanistic conclusions of Aim 1?
choices: A. Parallel genetic Dcaf7 knockout experiments designed to accompany every BC18630 pharmacological result
B. Availability of chemotypically distinct DCAF7 inhibitors from related chemotype classes that could substitute for BC18630
C. DCAF7-targeted siRNA knockdown studies that reproduce the loss-of-function phenotype through an orthogonal silencing mechanism
D. BC18630's validated selectivity profile, which argues against off-target effects as the primary driver of observed phenotypes
quizzes.explanation: The genetic Dcaf7 KO is explicitly positioned as the mechanistic anchor of Aim 1, with every BC18630 experiment paired with a KO counterpart; this means the genetic arm constitutes a complete, self-sufficient mechanistic story regardless of the small molecule's status. BC18630 functions as the translational bridge to a pharmacological intervention, not as the foundation of mechanistic evidence.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Aim 1 experimental redundancy design

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: Of the three core premises underlying this thesis, which carries the greatest technical vulnerability to falsification, and what is the primary basis for that uncertainty?
choices: Premise 1 — DCAF7 functions as a catalytic E3 ligase adapter — because no published in vitro reconstitution has unambiguously demonstrated this enzymatic role; falsification would redirect the model toward a scaffolding mechanism without eliminating the therapeutic implication of BC18630

Premise 3 — the DCAF7–IFIT3 axis confers selectivity for JAK2V617F-mutant cells — because its falsification would be maximally disruptive to the therapeutic rationale and therefore demands validation across the broadest panel of disease models

Premise 2 — IFIT3 bridges DCAF7 activity to downstream IFN-α production — because IFIT proteins are canonically interferon-stimulated genes, making their role as upstream IFN-α drivers mechanistically unprecedented

Premise 1 — DCAF7 functions as a catalytic E3 ligase adapter — because BC18630 has not yet been validated in primary patient-derived MPN cells, leaving the clinical translatability of the catalytic model uncertain
quizzes.explanation: The model answer identifies P1 as technically most vulnerable because no published in vitro reconstitution has unambiguously demonstrated DCAF7's catalytic E3 ligase adapter role — the Glenewinkel scaffolding interpretation remains a credible alternative. Crucially, P1's falsification would redirect rather than collapse the thesis, since BC18630 disrupting a scaffolding interaction preserves the therapeutic implication even under the alternative mechanistic framing.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Glenewinkel scaffolding interpretation · §P1 technical vulnerability

quizzes.stem: To determine whether DCAF7-mediated ubiquitination of IFIT3 is causally required for the MPN-relevant phenotype — rather than IFIT3 being a coincidental bystander substrate — which experimental approach most directly resolves the question?
choices: A. Deplete IFIT3 by siRNA in JAK2V617F MPN cells and confirm the resulting phenotype phenocopies Dcaf7 loss, establishing that reduced IFIT3 abundance alone recapitulates DCAF7 deficiency
B. Re-express wild-type IFIT3 in Dcaf7 KO cells and ask whether the MPN phenotype is restored, using protein level normalization as a surrogate for causal involvement

C. Re-express a K-to-R IFIT3 mutant (ubiquitination-dead at validated sites) in Dcaf7 KO cells; rescue of both IFIT3 protein levels and the MPN phenotype places IFIT3 on the causal path, whereas protein normalization without phenotype rescue indicates a bystander role
D. Perform ubiquitin remnant profiling (K-ε-GG enrichment) across DCAF7 substrates in MPN cells to determine whether IFIT3 ubiquitination abundance correlates with disease severity
quizzes.explanation: The K-to-R rescue experiment separates correlation from causation at the protein level: if a ubiquitination-dead IFIT3 mutant stabilizes in Dcaf7 KO cells and co-rescues the MPN phenotype, controlling IFIT3 abundance via ubiquitination is confirmed as the operative mechanism; if only protein normalizes while the phenotype persists, IFIT3 is incidental to DCAF7's causal biology. Choice B (WT rescue) cannot distinguish whether DCAF7-mediated ubiquitination itself — versus protein abundance controlled by other means — is the relevant variable.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: In a PhD thesis centered on DCAF7 → IFIT3 → IFN-α signaling in JAK2V617F MPN, which strategy best guards against serial post-hoc pivoting if primary substrate candidates fail to validate functionally?
choices: Pre-specify a multi-gate filter and a single go/no-go checkpoint before testing any candidate; if no candidate clears all gates, anchor the thesis on the robust Dcaf7-loss IFN-hypersensitivity phenotype framed as a proteostasis mechanism, deferring substrate identity to future work.

Require each candidate to achieve statistically significant functional rescue in two independent assays before advancing, then test the next candidate sequentially only if the primary fails both criteria.

Characterize all candidate substrates in parallel from the project outset, eliminating candidates that fail any single criterion, without pre-specifying the phenotypic threshold that would constitute commitment to one over another.

Designate IFIT3 as the sole primary candidate and require formal committee approval before initiating functional testing of any alternative, thereby restricting unplanned pivots to a single pre-specified contingency.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer avoids serial pivoting by making gate criteria and the commitment checkpoint explicit before any candidate is tested — converting what would be ad hoc decisions into pre-registered go/no-go rules. Critically, the substrate-agnostic fallback (proteostasis mechanism; IFN-hypersensitivity phenotype on Dcaf7 loss as the defensible anchor) ensures the thesis survives even if no individual effector substrate clears the filter, because the phenotypic result is robust independently of its molecular identity.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Table R2, month-6 checkpoint

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: A committee member challenges the use of Mx1-Cre as the primary inducible system, suggesting that poly(I:C)-driven innate immune activation could confound IFIT3/IFN-α readouts and that Rosa26-CreERT2 should have been used from the start. Which response best justifies the experimental design?
choices: Mx1-Cre is the field-standard inducible hematopoietic Cre for MPN modeling and provides rapid deletion kinetics; Rosa26-CreERT2 was incorporated from the outset as a pre-planned orthogonal control for the poly(I:C) confound, not added as a corrective afterthought.

Rosa26-CreERT2 is unsuitable as a primary tool in an IFN-α readout study because tamoxifen independently activates JAK-STAT signaling in hematopoietic progenitors, producing the same confound as poly(I:C).
Mx1-Cre was prioritized because Rosa26-CreERT2 had not yet been validated for efficient deletion in JAK2V617F knockin hematopoietic stem cells at the time the study was designed.

The poly(I:C) induction step was included as a positive control for maximal IFIT3 expression, so any innate immune activation it produces was a calibration feature rather than an experimental confound.
quizzes.explanation: Mx1-Cre is the established field-standard for inducible hematopoietic deletion in MPN models precisely because of its speed and penetrance; the Rosa26-CreERT2 arm (Ventura 2007) was written into the orthogonal-validation portion of the aim specifically to disentangle JAK2V617F-driven signaling from poly(I:C) effects, making the two-system sequence a deliberate design feature rather than a methodological retreat.
quizzes.citation: Ventura 2007 · Aim orthogonal-validation; model answer §Mx1-Cre rationale

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: A DCAF7-dependent vulnerability in the innate IFN-α axis is validated in JAK2V617F-mutant MPN cells but not in CALR-mutant disease. Which of the following best justifies pursuing this genotype-restricted dependency as a clinically meaningful therapeutic target?
choices: JAK2V617F drives ~95% of PV and ~50–60% of ET and MF cases, and a well-characterized mutation-specific dependency is more clinically actionable than a pan-MPN claim that does not hold up mechanistically.
Approved JAK inhibitors such as ruxolitinib preferentially suppress JAK2V617F-driven signaling over CALR-mutant disease, establishing regulatory precedent for mutation-selective MPN therapy.
CALR mutations are confined to rare triple-negative MPN cases and collectively account for fewer than 5% of all MPN patients, making coverage of this population clinically negligible.
