CALR-mutant cells signal primarily through MPL-dependent pathways that bypass JAK2, so IFIT3 induction remains constitutively active in this subtype and DCAF7 inhibition would have no discernible effect.
quizzes.explanation: JAK2V617F is the predominant MPN driver (~95% of PV, ~50–60% of ET and MF), so a mechanistically validated genotype-specific vulnerability reaches the majority of patients; critically, current standard-of-care JAK inhibitors (ruxolitinib, fedratinib) are not CALR-selective either, demonstrating that mutation-agnostic pan-MPN coverage is not the existing clinical bar—a narrow but rigorously validated dependency is more valuable than a broad claim that fails scrutiny.
quizzes.citation: Model answer · Section: Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: In a dissertation examining DCAF7 function across two temporally distinct hematopoietic contexts—adult JAK2V617F MPN and the perinatal HSC transition—what is the primary justification for designing Aim 1 and Aim 2 to remain independently viable?
choices: A. Independent aims protect against a single technical or model-system failure eliminating all dissertation outcomes.

B. Independent aims provide program-level resilience for an ambitious unified thesis: if mechanistic unification across the two contexts proves elusive, each context is independently important and constitutes an acceptable outcome.
C. Independent aims signal to the committee that the candidate has identified a weak point in the unifying hypothesis and is pre-emptively hedging against its failure.
D. Independent aims are structurally required when aims address biologically distinct disease phases in order to satisfy NIH training-grant milestone requirements.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer frames aim independence not as a concession of disbelief but as program-level risk management: joining two temporally distinct hematopoietic contexts under one unified mechanism is explicitly ambitious, and a dissertation that yields two well-characterized context-specific stories—even without full mechanistic unification—remains a valid and valuable outcome. Resilience is how a five-year project manages that ambition without staking everything on a single mechanistic link.
quizzes.citation: Dissertation design rationale · Aim independence / unified-thesis framing

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: Vav-Cre;Dcaf7fl/fl mice die at postnatal day 4, but Vav-Cre is active in endothelial progenitors as well as hematopoietic cells. Which single experiment most directly determines whether this lethality reflects a cell-autonomous hematopoietic defect rather than a vascular or stromal effect?
choices: A. Transplant Dcaf7-KO fetal liver cells competitively into lethally irradiated wild-type hosts, placing knockout hematopoietic cells in a normal vascular and stromal environment.
B. Switch to an HSC-restricted CreERT2 driver (e.g., SCL-CreERT2) and induce Dcaf7 deletion postnatally to test whether hematopoietic-confined loss recapitulates the P4 lethal phenotype.
C. Transplant wild-type bone marrow into lethally irradiated Vav-Cre;Dcaf7fl/fl neonates to determine whether normal hematopoietic cells can rescue the lethal phenotype.
D. Cross the Dcaf7fl allele onto a Tie2-Cre driver and compare the timing and severity of lethality with Vav-Cre knockouts to map the responsible cell compartment.
quizzes.explanation: Competitive transplantation of Dcaf7-KO fetal liver cells into wild-type irradiated recipients places the knockout hematopoietic cells in a normal vascular and stromal niche; any failure of engraftment or hematopoietic reconstitution in this context is therefore intrinsic to the hematopoietic lineage and cannot be attributed to Vav-Cre activity in endothelial progenitors. Conversely, normal reconstitution would implicate a non-hematopoietic compartment and reframe the P4 phenotype as a stromal or vascular niche story.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Tier 3 · The Broader Innate Immunity and Inflammation Landscape
quizzes.stem: In a DCAF7 → IFIT3 → IFN-α thesis plan, two checkpoints fail simultaneously: the in vitro reconstitution (month 6) and the patient-cohort concordance (month 12). Which statement best describes the consequence for the thesis narrative?
choices: Because these checkpoints co-vary, their joint failure functions as a single epistemic event; the thesis pivots to the scaffolding model and re-anchors on genetic KO, producing a coherent alternative Aim 1 centered on DCAF7-mediated IFIT3 stabilization.
Two simultaneous failures exceed the recovery threshold for Aim 1, so the thesis immediately shifts its central claims to Aim 2 rather than proposing a revised Aim 1 narrative.
The two failures are treated as independent events, each addressed by its own contingency arm without requiring integration into a unified revised narrative.
Because checkpoint co-variation reduces their collective falsificatory weight, the two failures leave the core mechanistic claims of Aim 1 unrevised.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer specifies that these checkpoints are not independent but co-vary, so their joint failure constitutes a single coordinated pivot — to the scaffolding model (R2) and genetic KO (R5) — yielding a defensible Aim 1 narrative in which DCAF7 scaffolds a kinase-substrate complex and its genetic loss produces IFN hypersensitization via IFIT3 stabilization. Only three independently falsified premises, not two co-varying ones, render Aim 1 genuinely untenable and force reliance on Aim 2 (R9).
quizzes.citation: Model answer · §'What happens if two checkpoints are missed simultaneously'

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: According to Johnson 2018, which structural difference between human IFIT3 and mouse Ifit3 makes a constitutive Ifit3-knockout mouse an inadequate model for studying human IFIT3 loss-of-function in the DCAF7→IFIT3→IFN-α axis?
choices: Mouse Ifit3 is not transcriptionally induced by type I interferons in hematopoietic progenitors, making its deletion irrelevant to JAK2V617F-driven ISG output
Mouse Ifit3 lacks the C-terminal IFIT1-interacting domain present in human IFIT3, so knockout phenotypes would not faithfully recapitulate human IFIT3 loss-of-function
Ifit3-null mice are not viable because Ifit3 provides a non-redundant scaffolding role for IFITsome assembly during early embryonic innate immune development
Mouse Ifit3 is functionally compensated by Ifit1 through overlapping TPR-domain interactions, masking any discernible phenotype in a single-gene knockout
quizzes.explanation: Johnson 2018 demonstrated that the C-terminal IFIT1-interacting domain of human IFIT3 is absent from mouse Ifit3, meaning the mouse protein cannot serve as a structural proxy for the human protein in DCAF7-dependent stabilization studies. A humanized knockin or conditional DCAF7-deletion model is therefore required to interrogate this axis faithfully.
quizzes.citation: Johnson 2018 · §1 IFIT Family Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In JAK2V617F MPN cells, the stabilized IFIT1/2/3 heterotrimer most likely engages which class of RNA substrate?
choices: Cap-1-methylated host mRNAs sequestered from translation via the IFIT1 5'-cap-binding tunnel
Endogenous Pol III 5'-triphosphate transcripts and hypomethylated cap-0 RNAs accumulating under JAK2V617F replication stress
Exogenous retroviral 5'-triphosphate RNA introduced by MPN-associated viral co-infection
Double-stranded RNA intermediates resolved from R-loops at constitutively transcribed JAK2-target loci
quizzes.explanation: The IFIT heterotrimer binds 5'-PPP RNA with nanomolar affinity through a conserved basic groove (R187; Pichlmair 2011) and discriminates cap-0 over cap-1 as non-self (Daffis 2010; Abbas 2017); in MPN, JAK2V617F replication stress elevates endogenous Pol III transcripts and pre-tRNAs — the identical PPP-RNA class IFIT5 sequesters in uninfected cells (Katibah 2014) — making these hypomethylated self-RNAs the most probable endogenous ligands rather than exogenous viral RNA.
quizzes.citation: Pichlmair 2011; Daffis 2010; Abbas 2013, 2017; Katibah 2014 · §1 Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: DCAF7 loss stabilizes IFIT3. Given that IFIT1 is the primary cap-binding subunit, which mechanistic account best explains how elevated IFIT3 protein alone increases antiviral translation inhibition?
choices: IFIT3 harbors an independent cap-0 recognition pocket within its TPR repeats, allowing it to sequester viral mRNA in parallel with IFIT1 without requiring heterotrimer assembly or IFIT2 recruitment.

IFIT3's C-terminal domain stabilizes IFIT1 and allosterically enforces cap-0 selectivity; the assembled heterotrimer inhibits translation ~10-fold more potently than any monomer, with IFIT2 contributing an additional independent eIF3-c contact.
Stabilized IFIT3 competitively displaces eIF4E from cap-0 mRNA structures, reducing ribosome recruitment independently of IFIT1 cap recognition or heterotrimer formation.
IFIT3 promotes K63-linked ubiquitination of IFIT1, extending its half-life and increasing total cap-binding capacity without altering cap-0 versus cap-1 substrate selectivity.
quizzes.explanation: Johnson 2018 demonstrated that IFIT3's C-terminal domain serves a dual scaffolding role: it stabilizes IFIT1 protein and allosterically restricts cap recognition to cap-0 substrates, while Fleith 2018 showed the fully assembled IFIT1/IFIT2/IFIT3 heterotrimer is ~10-fold more potent than any monomer at blocking translation. Terenzi 2006 further established that IFIT2 contributes a distinct eIF3-c binding contact, meaning stabilized IFIT3 amplifies output through three synergistic mechanisms simultaneously.
quizzes.citation: Johnson 2018 · Fleith 2018 · Terenzi 2006 · §1 IFIT Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which experimental strategy most directly distinguishes IFIT3's amplifier function at the MAVS–TBK1 interface from its effector function within the IFIT1/IFIT2/IFIT3 heterotrimer?
choices: Co-immunoprecipitate endogenous IFIT3 from JAK2V617F cells under MAVS knockdown versus TBK1 inhibition conditions to map which interaction is selectively lost in each context.
Reconstitute IFIT3 E164A/E165A (ablating MAVS contact) and a C-terminal IFIT3 truncation (ablating IFIT1 binding) in IFIT3-null MPN cells, then compare IFN-α rescue to wild-type IFIT3.
Delete IFIT1 in IFIT3-expressing MPN cells and assess whether TBK1 autophosphorylation at K38 is retained, to determine whether the amplifier signal is IFIT1-independent.
Introduce IFIT3 point mutations at residues under positive selection across vertebrate orthologs and score which substitutions selectively reduce MAVS co-IP versus IFN-α secretion.
quizzes.explanation: Liu 2011 mapped IFIT3 E164/E165 to the MAVS contact surface and the N-terminus to TBK1 K38, while Johnson 2018 identified a physically distinct C-terminal domain that mediates IFIT1 binding; because these two surfaces are separable, point ablation of only one interface (E164A/E165A for amplifier vs. C-terminal truncation for heterotrimer) in IFIT3-null MPN cells allows IFN-α rescue experiments to assign each function independently.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which prior finding provides the strongest precedent for a cullin-RING E3 ligase complex directing proteasomal degradation of IFIT3?
choices: A. Vaccinia virus protein C9 bridges IFIT1/2/3 to SCF(cullin-1–SKP1) via F-box and ankyrin-repeat domains, directing proteasomal turnover of the IFIT complex
B. UBE2O directly ubiquitinates IFIT3 at K236 in hepatocellular carcinoma, establishing site-specific proteasomal targeting independent of a cullin scaffold
C. FBXO11 recognizes a phosphorylated degron in IFIT3 and recruits it to SCF(cullin-1) for K48-linked polyubiquitination during antiviral responses
D. TRIM25, a RING-domain E3 ligase, conjugates K63-linked chains to IFIT3 to modulate its assembly with IFIT1 and IFIT2 into the antiviral complex
quizzes.explanation: Liu 2019 demonstrates that IFIT1/2/3 are bona fide substrates of an SCF (cullin-1–SKP1–RBX1) complex, with vaccinia C9 functioning as a bipartite F-box/ankyrin-repeat adaptor — the closest structural and mechanistic precedent for a CRL adaptor–IFIT3 axis. UBE2O (Li 2023) confirms site-specific ubiquitination of IFIT3 but operates as an E2 conjugating enzyme without a cullin scaffold, making it UPS precedent but not cullin-RING precedent.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: To measure IFIT3-dependent IFN amplification in vivo in a JAK2V617F MPN mouse model, which experimental strategy best captures the amplifier-loss phenotype modeled on Butchi 2013?
choices: Sort bone marrow pDCs and challenge ex vivo with CpG-ODN; quantify IFN-α secretion by ELISA and compare wild-type versus DCAF7-knockout cells

Sort HSPCs, granulocyte-monocyte progenitors, and megakaryocytic precursors; measure Ifna/Ifnb and downstream ISG induction ex vivo; and compare amplifier-loss phenotype in DCAF7-deficient versus -sufficient cells ± IFN-α challenge
Collect serum at peak disease burden and quantify IFN-α by ELISA, correlating levels with bone marrow fibrosis grade across genotypes
Perform single-cell RNA-seq on whole bone marrow at steady state and score cells for an ISG module without ex vivo stimulation or genetic perturbation
quizzes.explanation: Butchi 2013 established the amplifier paradigm by sorting myeloid cells, stimulating ex vivo, and comparing IFN output between genotypes; the MPN analog sorts disease-relevant progenitors (HSPCs, GMPs, megakaryocytic precursors), challenges them with IFN-α, and contrasts Ifna/Ifnb and ISG induction in DCAF7-deficient versus -sufficient cells to isolate a cell-autonomous amplifier-loss phenotype. CpG-ODN/pDC assays test innate sensing, not IFN re-amplification, while serum ELISA and bulk scRNA-seq lack the causal genetic comparison required to attribute the phenotype to DCAF7-IFIT3.
quizzes.citation: Butchi 2013 · §1 — IFIT Family Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which combination of mechanistic evidence best justifies CRL4-DCAF7 inhibition as a druggable therapeutic strategy in JAK2V617F MPN?
choices: A. Viral SCF-mediated IFIT degradation (Liu 2019) implies that pharmacologic CRL4-DCAF7 blockade stabilizes IFIT3; stabilized IFIT3 enforces p21/p27 cell-cycle arrest in myeloid cells (Xiao 2006) and primes JAK2V617F clones for venetoclax-synergistic GSDME pyroptosis (He 2024).

B. IFIT3 stabilization by DCAF7 inhibition amplifies MAVS oligomerization and downstream NF-κB activation, providing an IFN-independent anti-leukemic effect that makes concurrent IFN-α therapy unnecessary.

C. Because DCAF7 co-degrades IFIT1 and IFIT2 alongside IFIT3, therapeutic stabilization of the intact IFIT1–IFIT2–IFIT3 heterotrimer—rather than IFIT3 alone—is required for antiproliferative activity in myeloid malignancies.

D. CRL4-DCAF7 inhibition is predicted to synergize with ruxolitinib rather than venetoclax, because JAK2 inhibition transcriptionally upregulates IFIT3 and DCAF7 blockade prevents its subsequent proteasomal clearance.
quizzes.explanation: Liu 2019 established that virus-induced SCF-mediated IFIT degradation is mechanistically reversible by ubiquitin-ligase blockade, directly rationalizing CRL4-DCAF7 inhibition to stabilize IFIT3; Xiao 2006 then demonstrated that elevated IFIT3 drives p21/p27-dependent myeloid cell-cycle arrest, and He 2024 showed IFIT3 is required for GSDME-mediated pyroptosis that synergizes with venetoclax in leukemia and myeloma. Together these three papers form the complete druggability rationale—upstream stabilization, downstream anti-proliferative mechanism, and a combination partner.
quizzes.citation: Liu 2019 · Xiao 2006 · He 2024 · §1 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: DCAF7-dependent degradation of IFIT3 has been proposed as a mechanism by which JAK2V617F MPN clones evade IFN-α signaling. Which statement best explains why stabilizing IFIT3 in JAK2V617F myeloid cells is expected to suppress clonal expansion, despite IFIT3's documented ability to restrain caspase activation in some contexts?
choices: Stabilized IFIT3 displaces IFIT2 from the IFIT1–IFIT2–IFIT3 complex; liberated IFIT2 then drives cytochrome c release and intrinsic apoptosis selectively in JAK2V617F hematopoietic progenitors.
JAK2V617F directly phosphorylates IFIT3 at a conserved tyrosine, converting it from a caspase scaffold into a STAT5 antagonist that transcriptionally represses BCL-2 and MCL-1 survival factors.

IFIT3's caspase-restraining activity is documented in virus-infected epithelial cells; in JAK2V617F myeloid cells its dominant outputs—G1/S arrest via p27 stabilization and c-myc suppression, reinforced by IFN-α feedback—are antiproliferative independently of caspase status.

GSDME-dependent pyroptosis is the sole cytotoxic mechanism driven by IFIT3 in myeloid malignancies; JAK2V617F clones are intrinsically insensitive to IFIT3-mediated cell-cycle arrest, making pyroptosis the only relevant output.
quizzes.explanation: Xiao 2006 showed IFIT3/RIG-G drives G1/S arrest via p27 stabilization and c-myc/p21 suppression specifically in myeloid cells (APL, U937), establishing that the anti-apoptotic function Hsu 2013 documented in virus-infected A549 epithelium is cell-type-specific rather than universal; He 2024 adds GSDME-dependent pyroptosis as a second IFIT3-dependent cytotoxic output in hematologic malignancies, and together these antiproliferative activities—amplified by IFN-α feedback—dominate over caspase suppression in the JAK2V617F myeloid context.
quizzes.citation: Xiao 2006 · Hsu 2013 · Stawowczyk 2011 · He 2024 · §1 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which statement best explains why DCAF7's established role as a kinase scaffold is structurally compatible with its function as a CRL4 E3 adaptor?
choices: A. DDB1 contacts the top surface of the WD40 propeller while kinase clients such as DYRK1A and HIPK2 engage flanking residues, so both interfaces can be occupied simultaneously.
B. DCAF7 cycles between kinase scaffold and CRL4 adaptor states via phosphorylation-dependent conformational changes that alternately expose each binding surface.
C. Kinase co-occupancy is a prerequisite for CRL4^DCAF7 ubiquitylase activity because kinase-mediated substrate phosphorylation generates the degron recognized by the complex.
D. The two functions are mutually exclusive in vivo, but CRL4^DCAF7 ubiquitylase activity predominates under conditions where cognate kinase concentrations are low.
quizzes.explanation: The WD40 propeller presents non-overlapping binding surfaces: DDB1 docks on the propeller face while kinases (DYRK1A, HIPK2, MEKK1) contact flanking residues, making simultaneous engagement structurally permissible. Peng 2016 and Lear 2025 confirm this coexistence biochemically by reconstituting CRL4^DCAF7-dependent substrate ubiquitylation in defined in vitro systems independent of kinase co-occupancy.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Zemke & Berk 2017 identified a role for DCAF7 in type-I interferon regulation in primary human airway epithelium. Which statement best describes the molecular mechanism they reported?
choices: Adenovirus E1A subverts a DCAF7–FOXK–CtBP repressor complex, causing ~10-fold post-translational stabilization of IRF3 protein with no corresponding change in IRF3 mRNA, and driving induction of IFIT2, ISG15, and OASL.
Adenovirus E1A redirects CRL4–DCAF7 to ubiquitinate STAT2, suppressing JAK–STAT signaling by eliminating STAT2 before it can activate ISG transcription.
DCAF7 phosphorylates IRF3 at Ser386 through an intrinsic kinase domain, and E1A inhibits this modification to block IRF3 nuclear translocation and ISG induction.
A DCAF7–CtBP complex silences ISG promoters via histone deacetylation, and E1A activates ISG transcription by upregulating IRF3 mRNA through an AP-1–dependent mechanism.
quizzes.explanation: Zemke & Berk 2017 showed that E1A disrupts a DCAF7–FOXK–CtBP complex, leading to ~10-fold IRF3 protein accumulation without a mRNA change — establishing post-translational, CRL4-substrate-receptor-dependent control of IRF3 abundance as the mechanism. Distractors B–D misattribute STAT2 degradation (the Trilling 2011/MCMV finding) to E1A, or invent a kinase or transcriptional mRNA route inconsistent with the protein-only stabilization data.
quizzes.citation: Zemke & Berk 2017; Trilling 2011 · §2 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: A selective small-molecule inhibitor (BC18630) that disrupts the DCAF7–substrate protein–protein interaction was developed to validate which CRL4^DCAF7 substrate?
choices: A. TFEB, characterized in BEAS-2B airway epithelial cells
B. DNA ligase I, characterized in HEK293T cells
C. IRF3, characterized in THP-1 monocyte-derived macrophages
D. CDT1, characterized in U2OS osteosarcoma cells
quizzes.explanation: Lear 2025 established TFEB as a CRL4^DCAF7 substrate in BEAS-2B cells via cycloheximide chase in DCAF7-knockout cells and full recombinant in vitro reconstitution; BC18630 was then developed as a selective chemical probe that blocks the DCAF7–TFEB protein–protein interaction, providing orthogonal mechanistic evidence distinct from the diGly proteomics and MG132-rescue approach used by Peng 2016 for DNA ligase I.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which statement best describes the structural basis by which DCAF7 is incorporated as a substrate receptor into the CRL4 E3 ligase complex?
choices: DCAF7's WD40 β-propeller contacts DDB1's double-β-propeller via WDXR-family motif interactions — the same platform that accommodates CRBN and DCAF15.
DCAF7 harbors an F-box domain that docks into SKP1 on the Cullin4–RBX1 scaffold, adapting the CRL1 substrate-receptor grammar to the CRL4 complex.
DCAF7 uses a BTB/POZ domain to contact Cullin4A directly, bypassing DDB1 and positioning its WD40 surface for substrate capture analogously to CRL3.
DCAF7 engages the RBX1 RING domain through a degenerate RING-interacting loop, anchoring it to the catalytic core of CRL4 rather than to the DDB1 adaptor.
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7 is a WD40 β-propeller protein that docks into CRL4 via WDXR-family motif contacts with DDB1's double-β-propeller platform — the conserved substrate-receptor grammar validated by Angers 2006 and Jin 2006. Fischer 2014 confirmed that the identical DDB1 surface accommodates CRBN, establishing this as the shared architectural logic for CRL4 DCAFs.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Why must hematopoietic studies of DCAF7 in JAK2V617F MPN employ conditional knockout strategies (e.g., Vav-Cre, Mx1-Cre, or inducible shRNA) rather than germline deletion?
choices: DCAF7 is functionally redundant with DCAF8 in the embryo proper, so germline deletion is phenotypically silent and conditional strategies are needed to prevent paralog compensation in adult HSCs.

Constitutive DCAF7 deletion is embryonic lethal, presenting with intrauterine growth restriction, HIF-1 pathway activation, and broad developmental gene deregulation, which precludes postnatal hematopoietic analysis.
Germline DCAF7 loss causes perinatal lethality from cardiac outflow tract defects, making hematopoietic-restricted conditional deletion necessary to isolate MPN-relevant phenotypes.
Constitutive DCAF7 deletion triggers p53-dependent apoptosis in fetal liver HSCs, exhausting the hematopoietic progenitor pool before birth and preventing engraftment in transplantation assays.
quizzes.explanation: Wu 2025 demonstrated that constitutive DCAF7 loss is embryonic lethal, with affected embryos showing intrauterine growth restriction, activated HIF-1 signaling, and widespread developmental gene dysregulation. Because mice cannot survive to the stage where JAK2V617F MPN phenotypes are studied, tissue-restricted conditional strategies are the only viable approach for hematopoietic analysis.
quizzes.citation: Wu 2025 · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: A researcher observes proteasome-dependent degradation of IFIT3 and wants to attribute it specifically to CRL4^DCAF7, excluding parallel E3 ligases. Which pharmacologic agent provides the most CRL-selective mechanistic evidence?
choices: MG132, a reversible 26S proteasome inhibitor that stabilizes polyubiquitylated substrates of all E3 ligases indiscriminately
MLN4924 (pevonedistat), a NEDD8-activating enzyme inhibitor that selectively blocks cullin neddylation and thereby inactivates all cullin-RING ligases
PYR-41, an E1 ubiquitin-activating enzyme inhibitor that abolishes the entire ubiquitin-conjugation cascade globally
Carfilzomib, an irreversible 20S proteasome β5 inhibitor that accumulates substrates of every ubiquitin E3 ligase simultaneously
quizzes.explanation: MLN4924 targets NAE, preventing cullin neddylation required for CRL conformational activation; because HECT ligases (e.g., NEDD4) and neddylation-independent RING E3s (e.g., MDM2) are unaffected, a MLN4924 phenocopy specifically implicates the CRL superfamily rather than all proteasomal degradation. DDB1 siRNA knockdown and in vitro reconstitution with recombinant CUL4A/NEDD8/RBX1/DDB1/DCAF7 then localize responsibility to the CRL4^DCAF7 complex specifically.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which statement best describes current pharmacological tractability of DCAF7 and the mechanism of its available tool compounds?
choices: Pevonedistat (MLN4924) selectively inhibits DCAF7 by blocking the neddylation required for CRL4^DCAF7 complex activation.
IMiD-class molecular glues such as lenalidomide redirect DCAF7 substrate specificity by stabilizing a neo-substrate interface within CRL4^DCAF7.
BC18630 and BC18813 are selective small-molecule inhibitors of DCAF7 that disrupt the DCAF7–substrate interface.
DCAF7 is currently undruggable; the only validated strategy uses dTAG-mediated targeted protein degradation to acutely deplete it.
quizzes.explanation: Lear 2025 developed BC18630 and BC18813 as selective DCAF7 inhibitors that specifically block the DCAF7–substrate interface, demonstrating direct pharmacological tractability; the broader IMiD/molecular-glue literature (Fischer 2014; Petzold 2016; Matyskiela 2018; Słabicki 2020) establishes medicinal-chemistry precedent for modulating CRL4 substrate recruitment but involves CRBN-directed compounds, not DCAF7-directed ones.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Among the four co-equal 2005 JAK2V617F discovery papers, which experimental contribution was unique to James et al. and could not be claimed by Baxter, Kralovics, or Levine?
choices: A. Demonstration that JAK2V617F is sufficient to cause myeloproliferative disease in a murine bone marrow transplantation model
B. Identification of the V617F mutation using loss-of-heterozygosity–guided sequencing of chromosome 9p
C. Establishment of somatic origin by sequencing matched germline DNA from T cells or buccal swabs
D. Correlation of mutant allele burden with clinical phenotype severity and thrombotic risk
quizzes.explanation: James et al. expressed JAK2V617F in murine hematopoietic progenitors and reconstituted irradiated recipients, producing erythrocytosis and splenomegaly that established in vivo oncogenic sufficiency — a transplantation readout absent from the other three papers. Baxter used LOH-guided sequencing, Kralovics correlated allele burden with phenotype, and Levine took a candidate-gene functional approach; all three used human cohort data with germline controls to prove somatic origin, but none employed a murine reconstitution model.
quizzes.citation: Baxter 2005 (Lancet) · Kralovics 2005 (NEJM) · Levine 2005 (Cancer Cell) · James 2005 (Nature) · §3 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In JAK2V617F-positive MPN, 9pLOH arises predominantly through which mechanism, and what is its direct consequence for the JAK2 locus?
choices: Hemizygous deletion of the wild-type 9p allele, reducing JAK2 copy number to one and leaving a single JAK2V617F allele
Uniparental disomy generated by mitotic recombination, converting heterozygous JAK2V617F to homozygous without changing total copy number
Chromosomal nondisjunction producing monosomy 9p, with selective retention of the JAK2V617F-bearing chromosome
Somatic second-hit mutation of the wild-type JAK2 allele, yielding compound heterozygosity at the JAK2 locus
quizzes.explanation: Mitotic recombination duplicates the JAK2V617F-bearing homolog while eliminating the wild-type copy, producing uniparental disomy and leaving total 9p copy number intact — which is why standard karyotyping misses it. The resulting homozygosity increases JAK2V617F gene dosage, correlates with clinical severity (Kralovics 2005 cohort; Akada 2010 mouse gene-dose experiments), and provided the cytogenetic footprint that directed Baxter and Kralovics to the JAK2 driver.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: The Melo-Cardenas lab uses the Mullally 2010 conditional Jak2V617F knock-in rather than the Akada 2010 knock-in for its DCAF7 studies. What is the primary reason for this choice?
choices: The Akada allele drives JAK2V617F from a heterologous ROSA26 promoter, producing non-physiological expression levels that complicate downstream signaling analysis.
The Mullally JAK2V617F allele has already been bred into the lab's DCAF7fl/fl and Mx1-Cre background strains, making it the operative model for the proposed genetic crosses.
The Mullally model develops a predominantly erythrocytosis phenotype that more faithfully mirrors human polycythemia vera than the thrombocytosis-dominant disease in the Akada model.
The Mullally allele supports homozygous versus heterozygous gene-dose comparisons, enabling discrimination of DCAF7-dependent effects across a range of JAK2 signaling intensities.
quizzes.explanation: Both models are equivalent conditional knock-ins with Cre-inducible JAK2V617F expression from the endogenous Jak2 promoter; the distinction is practical availability — the Mullally allele has been crossed into the DCAF7fl/fl and Mx1-Cre lines already maintained in the Melo-Cardenas lab. Notably, it is the Akada allele (not Mullally) that permits het-vs-hom gene-dose comparisons, making choice D an instructive inversion of the actual experimental reality.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which observation most directly supports the conclusion that a single CRL4–DCAF assembly can recognize structurally diverse substrates, making a novel client like IFIT3 plausible for CRL4^DCAF7?
choices: CRL4^CRBN ubiquitinates structurally unrelated clients — IKZF1, CK1α, SALL4, and C2H2 zinc-finger proteins — through a single receptor subunit, and CRL4B engages cyclin K for degradation in the complete absence of a dedicated receptor, demonstrating that the DDB1 platform is intrinsically flexible.

DCAF WD40 β-propeller domains adopt pre-organized, rigid substrate-binding clefts analogous to SCF F-box receptors that recognize phosphodegrons, restricting each CRL4 assembly to a structurally homologous set of clients.

Neddylation of CUL4 triggers sequential allosteric transitions in the DDB1 scaffold that expose distinct docking surfaces, enabling a receptor-free CRL4 complex to engage structurally unrelated clients at discrete cell-cycle phases.

DDB1's BPB β-propeller acts as a direct substrate-contact module independently of the assembled DCAF receptor, providing a receptor-agnostic ubiquitination surface that accounts for cross-client promiscuity.
quizzes.explanation: The model answer points to two convergent lines of evidence: CRBN's documented breadth across structurally unrelated IMiD-dependent substrates (IKZF1, CK1α, SALL4, C2H2 zinc fingers) within a single receptor, and Słabicki/Mayor-Ruiz 2020's demonstration that CRL4B recruits cyclin K without any dedicated receptor — together establishing that inherent DDB1 platform flexibility, not rigid receptor pre-organization, underlies CRL4 substrate diversity.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Williams 2022 and Van Egeren 2021 together show that JAK2V617F is acquired decades before MPN diagnosis. Which therapeutic implication most directly follows from this timing?
choices: A. Because JAK2V617F expands over ~30 years within a long-lived HSC clone, a durable cell-intrinsic intervention — such as targeted DCAF7 disruption — is preferred over intermittent systemic IFN-α dosing.

B. Because the ~30-year pre-clinical period reflects low JAK2V617F clonal fitness, short-course JAK2 inhibitor therapy (ruxolitinib) can control symptomatic disease without requiring a permanent intervention.

C. Because JAK2V617F originates in committed myeloid progenitors rather than long-lived HSCs, cytoreductive strategies targeting differentiated progeny are more appropriate than stem-cell-directed approaches.

D. Because the extended pre-clinical window allows time to force JAK2V617F HSCs into cycle, initiating systemic pegylated IFN-α2a at first molecular detection is the optimal clonal eradication strategy.
quizzes.explanation: Williams 2022 and Van Egeren 2021 establish that the disease-initiating mutation resides in a long-lived HSC clone acquired in childhood, meaning therapy must durably silence the clone at its source across decades. Intermittent systemic IFN-α cannot maintain sustained cell-intrinsic suppression the way a genetic DCAF7 loss or selective small-molecule DCAF7 disruptor would.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: The COMFORT-I trial showed ruxolitinib reduces splenomegaly and symptoms but produces no molecular remission. Based on Mullally 2010 and 2013, which statement best explains ruxolitinib's failure to cure MPN AND correctly predicts the mechanistic level at which DCAF7 loss should operate?
choices: A. Ruxolitinib achieves cytoreduction but spares JAK2V617F disease-initiating HSCs; this HSC persistence implies DCAF7 loss must act through a mechanism active at the HSC level — specifically amplified IFN/ISG signaling — that is functionally orthogonal to JAK2 kinase inhibition.

B. Ruxolitinib achieves cytoreduction but is undermined by acquired JAK1 kinase-domain mutations that restore downstream STAT signaling; this resistance implies DCAF7 loss must act downstream of JAK1/2 to remain effective regardless of kinase mutation status.

C. Ruxolitinib achieves cytoreduction but cannot reduce the JAK2V617F variant allele frequency in neutrophils; this allele burden persistence implies DCAF7 loss must promote asymmetric division of mutant HSCs to dilute the oncogenic allele over time.

D. Ruxolitinib achieves cytoreduction but triggers compensatory thrombopoietin–MPL signaling that sustains malignant HSC self-renewal; this bypass implies DCAF7 loss must act by suppressing MPL expression through CRL4DCAF7-mediated transcriptional repression.
quizzes.explanation: COMFORT-I showed symptomatic benefit without molecular response, and Mullally 2010 established that JAK2 kinase inhibition leaves disease-initiating HSCs intact — meaning ruxolitinib's curative failure is HSC persistence, not acquired kinase resistance or allele-burden dynamics. Mullally 2013 demonstrated that IFN/ISG signaling is active at the HSC level, placing DCAF7-amplified ISG output in a mechanistically distinct compartment that is complementary to, not redundant with, ruxolitinib.
quizzes.citation: COMFORT-I (Verstovsek 2012) · Mullally 2010 · Mullally 2013 · §3 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Based on published signaling data and the proposed DCAF7-IFIT3 mechanism, which statement best predicts whether this pathway would be operative in CALR-mutant MPN?
choices: The mechanism is predicted to generalize because CALR-mutant MPN converges on the same MPL-JAK2-STAT5 downstream axis as JAK2V617F, and DCAF7-IFIT3 targets IFN signaling and JAK1 rather than a V617F-specific structural feature.

The mechanism is unlikely to generalize because CALR mutations activate MPL through an extracellular interaction that does not require constitutive JAK2 kinase activity, decoupling the DCAF7-IFIT3 regulatory axis from CALR-driven disease.
The mechanism would generalize only if DCAF7 directly ubiquitinates the JAK2V617F pseudokinase domain, a structural dependency absent in wild-type JAK2 activated downstream of CALR-mutant MPL.

The mechanism is predicted to generalize only to CALR type I frameshift mutations, because CALR type II insertions preferentially engage EPOR rather than MPL, bypassing the JAK2-STAT5 axis shared with JAK2V617F disease.
quizzes.explanation: Elf 2016 and Marty 2016 establish that CALR-mutant MPN signals through MPL-JAK2-STAT5, the same downstream axis as JAK2V617F; because DCAF7-IFIT3 is proposed to modulate JAK1 and IFN signaling rather than the V617F kinase conformation specifically, the pathway is predicted to be mutation-agnostic, with a CALR-knockin × DCAF7-flox cross as the definitive future test.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: DCAF7 loss is predicted to phenocopy pharmacological IFN-α in depleting JAK2V617F disease-initiating HSCs. Which statement best identifies the mechanistic distinction between DCAF7 loss and a simple IFN-α agonist, and names the experiment that would formally establish it?
choices: DCAF7 loss stabilizes IFIT3 through a cell-intrinsic ubiquitin-pathway mechanism; JAK2V617F HSC depletion persisting in IFNAR1-deficient recipients would formally exclude autocrine Type I IFN as the driver.

DCAF7 loss prevents IRF3 ubiquitination, amplifying endogenous IFN-α transcription in mutant HSCs; distinguished from pharmacological IFN-α by IRF3 knockout abolishing, but IFNAR1 knockout preserving, the depletion phenotype.

DCAF7 loss selectively engages STAT1 homodimer-driven transcription rather than ISGF3-driven programs; distinguished from IFN-α by IFNGR1 co-deletion abolishing, but IFNAR1 co-deletion not affecting, mutant HSC frequency.

DCAF7 loss reduces JAK2V617F clonal expansion by directing hyperphosphorylated JAK2 to CRL4-mediated proteasomal degradation; distinguished from IFN-α agonism by additive rather than epistatic effects when combined with ruxolitinib.
quizzes.explanation: Mullally 2013 and Hasan 2013 establish that pharmacological IFN-α acts systemically to drive mutant HSCs from quiescence and deplete disease-initiating cells on secondary transplant; the DCAF7→IFIT3 model predicts the same cellular outcome but through cell-intrinsic ISG stabilization independent of extracellular IFN-α. IFNAR1 co-deletion is the decisive experiment: if JAK2V617F HSC depletion persists without the Type I IFN receptor, autocrine signaling is formally excluded as the true driver.
quizzes.citation: Mullally 2013; Hasan 2013; Williams 2022 · §3 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: A colleague asks whether the DCAF7→IFIT3→IFN-α signaling axis would remain therapeutically relevant in JAK2 exon 12 polycythemia vera (Scott 2007). Which reasoning best justifies predicting that it would?
choices: Exon 12 and V617F mutations both release JAK2 autoinhibition through the same pseudokinase-domain mechanism, ensuring identical pathway activation.
The DCAF7 mechanism operates downstream of IFN receptor engagement, rendering it insensitive to which upstream JAK2 activation allele is present.
DCAF7 directly remodels JAK2 kinase-domain conformation, and exon 12 alleles preserve the same catalytic surface targeted by V617F-directed therapy.
IFN-α is contraindicated in exon 12 PV because altered STAT1/STAT5 ratios restrict therapeutic IFN signaling to V617F disease contexts.
quizzes.explanation: Exon 12 mutations release JAK2 autoinhibition through a mechanistically distinct route from V617F, but both funnel through downstream JAK-STAT signaling and respond to IFN-α clinically; because DCAF7 acts after IFN receptor engagement rather than at JAK2 itself, the mechanism is agnostic to the specific activating allele — a predicted strength of the approach.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In Pichlmair 2011, Ifit1−/− mice showed enhanced susceptibility to VSV and Rift Valley fever virus but not to influenza A virus, establishing influenza as the negative control. What is the mechanistic basis for influenza's failure to produce a susceptibility phenotype in Ifit1−/− animals?
choices: Influenza acquires 5′-methylguanosine caps from host pre-mRNAs via cap-snatching, so its transcripts present a capped 5′ terminus rather than the free 5′-triphosphate recognized by IFIT1
Influenza NS1 protein directly binds and sequesters IFIT1, rendering IFIT1-dependent restriction irrelevant to viral replication outcome
Influenza genomic RNA forms a 5′-panhandle duplex that sterically occludes the 5′ terminus from IFIT1 binding regardless of phosphorylation state
Influenza replicates in the nucleus during early infection, compartmentally separating viral RNA from cytoplasmic IFIT1 at the critical recognition step
quizzes.explanation: IFIT1 specifically binds 5′-triphosphate (5′-ppp) RNA; because influenza polymerase cap-snatches capped oligomers from host pre-mRNAs to prime its own transcription, influenza mRNAs are capped and thus invisible to IFIT1, explaining the absent phenotype in Ifit1−/− mice. By analogy, IFIT3—which shares the TPR-domain architecture mediating 5′-ppp recognition—would be predicted to engage uncapped, 5′-triphosphorylated RNA species such as cytoplasmic viral genomes or mitochondrial transcripts.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Which finding most directly quantifies a JAK2V617F fitness advantage at the HSC level, and what best explains why competitive murine BMT experiments failed to detect the same advantage?
choices: Van Egeren 2021 reconstructed human JAK2V617F clonal dynamics in situ and quantified an HSC-level fitness advantage; Mullally 2010 is negative because murine BMT compresses competition into weeks, under-reading an advantage that accrues over human decades.

Hasan 2013 demonstrated age-dependent LSK/SLAM expansion with the Villeval allele; Mullally 2010 is negative because young irradiated recipients lack the aged marrow niche signals required to manifest the V617F fitness advantage.

Scott 2006 showed that homozygous V617F erythroid cells outcompete heterozygous cells; Mullally 2010 used exclusively heterozygous donors, masking a selective advantage that appears only upon allele duplication.

Nangalia 2013 identified TET2 co-mutations that synergize with V617F for clonal dominance; Mullally 2010's clean V617F model lacks the cooperating lesions present in most human MPN clones, explaining the absent competitive advantage.
quizzes.explanation: Van Egeren 2021 used phylogenetic reconstruction of human clonal histories to quantify a V617F fitness advantage accruing at the HSC level over decades; the Mullally 2010 competitive BMT assay, which reads out weeks of murine engraftment, lacks the temporal resolution to detect this slowly accumulating advantage.
quizzes.citation: Van Egeren 2021 · Mullally 2010 · §3 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Clinical data indicate that a patient's baseline interferon-stimulated gene (ISG) signature predicts response to IFN-α therapy in JAK2V617F MPN. If DCAF7 loss elevates basal IFIT3-driven ISG output in MPN HSCs, what is the most direct therapeutic implication for patients who are currently IFN-α non-responders?
choices: A. By pre-activating the ISG signature associated with IFN-α response, DCAF7 loss could convert non-responders to responders or lower the effective therapeutic dose required.

B. By impairing CRL4DCAF7-mediated ubiquitylation of JAK2V617F, DCAF7 loss stabilizes the oncoprotein and amplifies STAT5-driven proliferation, sensitizing HSCs to IFN-α–induced differentiation pressure.
C. By preventing SOCS1 from entering the CRL4DCAF7 ubiquitylation complex, DCAF7 loss sustains STAT1 phosphorylation downstream of IFNAR, converting a transient to a durable IFN-α signaling response.

D. By targeting IRF2 for CRL4-mediated proteasomal degradation, DCAF7 loss de-represses ISG promoters in a JAK-STAT–independent manner, bypassing the upstream signaling defect that underlies non-response.
quizzes.explanation: Hasan 2013 and Kiladjian-era clinical data establish that a high baseline ISG signature correlates with IFN-α response in MPN; DCAF7 loss, by upregulating IFIT3 and expanding basal ISG output, could push non-responders above this threshold or reduce the dose needed to clear JAK2V617F HSCs — a hypothesis directly testable in the Mullally mouse model.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: ISG15 deficiency causes paradoxical hyper-IFN by destabilizing USP18, the principal negative regulator of IFNAR–JAK–STAT signaling. Based on the observation that DCAF7 loss elevates IFIT3 and amplifies IFN-α in JAK2V617F MPN, which statement best characterizes the mechanistic relationship between the DCAF7→IFIT3 axis and the ISG15→USP18 precedent?
choices: A. Analogous: both represent UPS-associated dampening of IFN output, but through opposite regulatory polarities — DCAF7 degrades a positive IFN effector (IFIT3), whereas ISG15 stabilizes a negative IFN regulator (USP18); loss of either removes a brake on IFN amplitude.

B. Analogous: DCAF7 stabilizes a USP18-equivalent negative regulator of IFNAR signaling, so DCAF7 loss mimics ISG15 deficiency by removing the same inhibitory checkpoint through identical molecular polarity.

C. Inverse: because IFIT3 is a positive IFN effector rather than a negative regulator, DCAF7 loss was predicted to attenuate IFN-α output, making the two systems mechanistically opposite in direction.

D. Inverse: DCAF7 drives IFIT3 transcription downstream of JAK2V617F–STAT signaling, so elevated IFIT3 upon DCAF7 loss reflects relief of transcriptional repression rather than ubiquitin-mediated protein turnover.
quizzes.explanation: DCAF7, as a CRL4 substrate receptor, normally targets IFIT3 for ubiquitin-mediated degradation, functioning as a UPS-based brake on IFN amplitude — mechanistically parallel to ISG15's role in shielding USP18 from proteasomal turnover. Both systems are functionally analogous (loss of either → hyper-IFN), even though the molecular polarity differs (removing a positive effector via degradation vs. protecting a negative regulator via stabilization).

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In JAK2V617F MPN cells with DCAF7 knockdown, you treat with the STING inhibitor H-151 and measure IFIT3 protein levels alongside the broader ISG score. Which result would most directly support the conclusion that DCAF7-loss amplification of IFIT3 acts downstream of cGAS-STING?
choices: A. H-151 abolishes both the baseline ISG score and the DCAF7-knockdown-dependent IFIT3 increment, restoring IFIT3 to control levels.
B. H-151 suppresses the baseline ISG score and IFN-α secretion but leaves the DCAF7-knockdown-dependent IFIT3 elevation intact.
C. H-151 has no effect on either the ISG score or IFIT3 in DCAF7-knockdown cells, suggesting cGAS-STING is not the principal IFN driver in this context.
D. H-151 reduces IFIT3 in control cells but paradoxically elevates IFIT3 in DCAF7-knockdown cells through compensatory JAK-STAT feedback.
quizzes.explanation: If DCAF7-loss amplifies IFIT3 downstream of cGAS-STING—by stabilizing IFIT3 protein or enhancing JAK-STAT transduction after IFN receptor engagement—then STING inhibition would extinguish the upstream IFN trigger and collapse the baseline ISG score while leaving the DCAF7-dependent IFIT3 increment intact. Only if STING inhibition also abolished the knockdown increment (choice A) would the epistasis support an upstream or co-incident role for DCAF7 at the level of cGAS-STING activation.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Applying the Walter 2015 and Wilson 2015 framework to a DCAF7-loss + JAK2V617F mouse model, which of the following best predicts the HSC phenotype at single-cell resolution, the outcome of serial limiting-dilution transplantation at 16 weeks, and the finding in the DCAF7-loss + JAK2-WT negative-control arm?
choices: A. Preferential depletion of the deeply quiescent HSC subset via IFN-α-driven dormancy exit and DNA damage; reduced HSC frequency by limiting dilution; DCAF7-loss alone (JAK2-WT) shows no significant attrition, implicating JAK2V617F-amplified IFN-α as the cooperating driver.

B. Selective loss of actively cycling short-term HSCs from replication stress, sparing the deeply quiescent pool; normal LT-HSC frequency by limiting dilution; DCAF7-loss alone shows an equivalent cycling defect, indicating an IFN-independent mechanism.

C. Expansion of the deeply quiescent HSC subset through IFN-α-mediated cell-cycle arrest; increased HSC frequency by limiting dilution; DCAF7-loss alone produces identical expansion, indicating DCAF7 acts upstream of JAK2 regardless of mutational status.

D. Symmetric depletion across all HSC subsets without single-cell bias; unchanged HSC frequency but impaired multi-lineage output by limiting dilution; DCAF7-loss alone (JAK2-WT) shows greater attrition than the double mutant due to synthetic lethality at the dormancy checkpoint.
quizzes.explanation: Walter 2015 established that IFN-α forces deeply dormant HSCs out of quiescence, exposing them to replication-associated DNA damage and progressive attrition; Wilson 2015 demonstrated at single-cell resolution that this attrition is concentrated in the most quiescent HSC subset. In the DCAF7-loss + JAK2V617F model, elevated IFIT3-mediated IFN-α output amplifies this dormancy-exit pressure, selectively depleting deep-quiescent LT-HSCs and reducing functional HSC frequency in serial limiting-dilution assays, while JAK2-WT controls—lacking the JAK2V617F-driven IFN amplification loop—serve as evidence that the attrition requires oncogene cooperativity rather than DCAF7 loss alone.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In the Stark & Darnell (2012) framework, JAK-STAT output is governed by the balance between receptor-proximal activation (ligand–IFNAR–JAK) and negative regulators (SOCS proteins, USP18, IFNAR1 turnover). Which statement correctly places the DCAF7–IFIT3 axis within this hierarchy and explains why that placement matters therapeutically?
choices: A. Receptor-proximal: DCAF7 stabilizes IFNAR1 at the plasma membrane, prolonging JAK1/TYK2 trans-phosphorylation and amplifying STAT1/2 activation upstream of ISG transcription.

B. Transcription-factor level: DCAF7 shields STAT2 from proteasomal degradation in the nucleus, enhancing ISG promoter occupancy in a manner functionally analogous to USP18-mediated STAT2 stabilization.

C. Post-translational/effector level: DCAF7 regulates the ISG-encoded protein IFIT3 after STAT-driven transcription, so inhibiting DCAF7 modulates IFN-α–driven output without directly perturbing JAK or STAT activity.
D. Negative-regulatory level: DCAF7 ubiquitinates SOCS1 to relieve JAK1 feedback inhibition, amplifying rather than restraining IFN-α signaling in JAK2V617F cells.
quizzes.explanation: Because IFIT3 is itself an ISG, DCAF7's regulation of IFIT3 protein occurs after STAT-driven transcription—placing the axis at the post-translational/effector tier rather than at the receptor or STAT level. This distinction means DCAF7 inhibition can modulate IFN-α amplification through a mechanism orthogonal to JAK inhibitors, potentially avoiding the receptor-proximal on-target hematopoietic toxicity associated with ruxolitinib-class agents.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: DCAF7 knockout mice exhibit an HSC defect at P4, before the Bowie-defined fetal-to-adult transition (~3–4 weeks postnatally). Which single qPCR panel, applied to sorted DCAF7-KO P4 HSCs, would best discriminate among DCAF7 acting as (a) a Lin28b co-factor within the fetal program, (b) a premature driver of the fetal-to-adult switch, or (c) a factor independent of the Lin28b–let-7–Hmga2 axis?
choices: A. Lin28b mRNA, Hmga2 mRNA, and mature let-7 miRNA
B. Sox17, Igf2bp2, and Igf2bp3 (Hmga2 transcriptional targets marking fetal HSC identity)
C. IFIT1, IFIT3, and Mx1 (interferon-stimulated gene panel reflecting DCAF7 downstream output)
D. Cdkn1c (p57), EGR1, and Slamf1 (CD150) (HSC quiescence and surface identity markers)
quizzes.explanation: Measuring each node of the Copley 2013 axis directly — Lin28b (upstream RNA-binding protein), let-7 (intermediate miRNA), and Hmga2 (downstream effector) — generates a three-way readout: if Lin28b is intact but Hmga2 is selectively reduced without let-7 elevation, DCAF7 functions as a co-factor downstream of Lin28b (a); if both Lin28b and Hmga2 are reduced with elevated let-7, DCAF7 restrains the switch (b); if all three are unchanged while HSC output is perturbed, DCAF7 operates independently (c). Downstream fetal identity markers (Sox17, Igf2bp2/3) or ISG output (IFIT1/3, Mx1) report consequences of axis activity rather than causal position within it.
quizzes.citation: Bowie 2007 · Blood; Copley 2013 · Science · §4 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: The SCF^βTrCP complex ubiquitinates IFNAR1 after CKII primes a DSGxxS phosphodegron, directing the receptor toward K63-linked ubiquitin-dependent endocytosis and lysosomal degradation. Which feature is architecturally CONSERVED if CRL4^DCAF7 similarly ubiquitinates IFIT3?
choices: A cullin scaffold (CUL4) paired with the RBX1 RING domain recruits the ubiquitin-charged E2, mirroring the CUL1–RBX1 catalytic core of SCF^βTrCP
The substrate receptor DCAF7 recognizes a DSGxxS phosphodegron on IFIT3, analogous to βTrCP recognition of phospho-IFNAR1
SKP1 bridges the cullin to DCAF7, performing the same adaptor role it plays in SCF^βTrCP
K63-linked polyubiquitin chains deposited on IFIT3 serve as an endocytic sorting signal for ESCRT-dependent MVB delivery, as on IFNAR1
quizzes.explanation: SCF and CRL4 are both cullin-RING ligases (CRLs): each pairs a cullin (CUL1 or CUL4) with RBX1 to position the E2, making this catalytic core the conserved element. The adaptors differ (SKP1 in SCF vs. DDB1 in CRL4), the substrate receptors differ (βTrCP F-box vs. DCAF7 DWD domain), βTrCP's phosphodegron requirement is not shared by DCAF family members, and whether IFIT3 polyubiquitination generates K63-endocytic vs. K48-proteasomal chains is an unresolved gap in the thesis context.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In limiting-dilution bone marrow transplantation, approximately what fraction of CD150+CD48− LSK cells achieves long-term multilineage reconstitution, and why is this quantitative benchmark essential for interpreting an LT-HSC phenotype caused by DCAF7 loss?
choices: ~20%; the low purity means reconstituting activity is dominated by contaminating multipotent progenitors, so HSC-intrinsic signals must be normalized to total donor chimerism rather than input cell number
~47%; this enrichment level reveals that true LT-HSC changes — expansion in JAK2V617F or contraction on Dcaf7 loss — are diluted and masked within the more heterogeneous LSK gate
~70%; this threshold is sufficient to detect clonal dominance by JAK2V617F without requiring single-cell limiting-dilution normalization across transplant recipients
~90%; near-complete purity is necessary to eliminate progenitor contamination that would otherwise confound LT-HSC frequency estimates in Dcaf7-null competitive transplants
quizzes.explanation: Kiel et al. (2005) established ~47% long-term multilineage reconstitution purity for CD150+CD48− LSK cells, defining the gold-standard sensitivity of the SLAM gate. Because the broader LSK population is far more heterogeneous, phenotypic LT-HSC expansions (JAK2V617F) or contractions (Dcaf7 loss) are diluted below detection unless the purified SLAM gate — not the LSK gate — serves as the experimental denominator.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In an Mx1-Cre conditional knockout experiment designed to delete *Dcaf7* in adult hematopoietic cells, poly(I:C) injection is required to induce Cre recombinase. What is the primary confound this approach introduces when IFN-α signaling is the experimental outcome measure?
choices: A. poly(I:C) activates TLR3 and MDA5, inducing endogenous type I IFN production that upregulates IFIT3 and directly perturbs the DCAF7–IFIT3–IFN-α axis under study
B. poly(I:C) signals through TLR3 to activate NF-κB, upregulating IFIT3 and other IRF3-target genes independently of type I IFN and JAK–STAT, mimicking the readout
C. poly(I:C) transiently depletes IFN-α–producing plasmacytoid dendritic cells, suppressing baseline IFN-α and generating a pseudo-rescue phenotype in knockout animals
D. poly(I:C) induces mosaic Cre expression across hematopoietic and non-hematopoietic compartments, diluting the cell-autonomous phenotype and reducing statistical power
quizzes.explanation: poly(I:C) is sensed by TLR3 (endosomal) and MDA5 (cytosolic), both of which signal through IRF3/7 to drive type I IFN production; the resulting IFN-α/β then upregulates IFIT3 via JAK–STAT—the same axis being measured—creating a baseline perturbation that is mitigated only by a 3–4 week washout period before phenotyping.
quizzes.citation: Kühn 1995; de Boer 2003 · §5 Deep Gap Analysis

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In the 'somatic interferonopathy' model of JAK2V617F MPN — analogous to TREX1-deficient Aicardi-Goutières syndrome — JAK2V617F drives cytosolic nucleic-acid accumulation through replication stress. Which statement correctly pairs the upstream innate sensor, the mechanistic gap left by ruxolitinib, and the predicted position of DCAF7 inhibition in this causal chain?
choices: cGAS-STING senses JAK2V617F-driven cytosolic DNA; ruxolitinib suppresses downstream JAK1/2-STAT signaling but leaves the sensor intact; DCAF7 inhibition interrupts ISG amplification at the IFIT3 node, a mechanistic position ruxolitinib cannot reach.

RIG-I/MDA5 senses cytosolic RNA from oncogene-driven transcriptional stress; ruxolitinib directly suppresses RIG-I helicase activity; DCAF7 inhibition reduces IFN-α secretion by blocking IRF7 dimerization upstream of gene transcription.

cGAS-STING senses cytosolic DNA generated by replication stress; ruxolitinib inhibits STING directly by competing with cGAMP binding; DCAF7 inhibition acts at the IFN receptor level to prevent STAT1 phosphorylation.
TLR7 senses endosomal self-RNA shed from JAK2V617F-expressing HSCs; ruxolitinib inhibits TLR7-MyD88 signaling; DCAF7 inhibition targets IRF3 phosphorylation upstream of IFN-α transcription.
quizzes.explanation: In JAK2V617F MPN, replication stress generates cytosolic DNA sensed by cGAS-STING, directly paralleling the undigested nucleic-acid accumulation in TREX1-null AGS; ruxolitinib's JAK1/2 inhibition acts downstream of the IFN receptor and therefore cannot silence the sensor or reduce danger-signal load, while DCAF7 inhibition would disrupt ISG induction specifically at the IFIT3 node — between STAT activation and the effector interferon program — a position complementary to JAK inhibition rather than redundant with it.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: Pevonedistat inhibits NAE (NEDD8 E1) and stabilizes IFIT3. What mechanistic feature of this drug specifically implicates a cullin-RING ligase (CRL) in IFIT3 ubiquitylation, rather than implicating a broader proteasomal or E3-ligase pathway?
choices: A. Neddylation is required to shift cullin into the active Rbx1 conformation; because HECT, RBR, and RING-only E3 ligases do not depend on cullin neddylation, pevonedistat abolishes activity exclusively across the ~250-member CRL family.

B. NAE is the shared E1 for both NEDD8 and ubiquitin; pevonedistat therefore depletes ubiquitin conjugates globally, and IFIT3 stabilization implicates the proteasome rather than any specific E3 class.

C. Non-neddylated cullins sequester Cand1, which broadly inhibits 26S proteasome import channels; IFIT3 stabilization is thus a secondary consequence of impaired proteasomal gating rather than CRL-specific inactivation.

D. The Rbx1 RING domain is shared by multiple non-cullin RING-type E3 ligases; pevonedistat therefore inactivates all Rbx1-containing complexes, making IFIT3 stabilization consistent with any Rbx1-dependent pathway.
quizzes.explanation: Neddylation drives a conformational shift in cullin that positions Rbx1 for catalytic activity; without this shift, CRL activity is abolished while HECT, RBR, and RING-only ligases — which are cullin-independent — remain fully active (Brownell 2010; Duda 2008). IFIT3 stabilization under pevonedistat therefore restricts the responsible E3 to a member of the CRL family.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: A researcher plans to validate mouse MPN findings by editing primary human CD34+ HSCs for NSG transplantation studies. Which statement best explains why CRISPR-RNP electroporation is the preferred delivery method for this bridging approach?
choices: Lentiviral Cas9 delivery achieves comparable editing rates and permits selection of stably integrated constructs over 10–14 days, making it preferable when uniform Cas9 expression across CD34+ subpopulations is required.

RNP electroporation yields 40–80% on-target editing without extended ex vivo culture, preserving HSC engraftment capacity and enabling multiplex editing across DCAF7, IFIT3, and JAK2V617F loci with NSG transplant readout at 8–12 weeks.

Plasmid-based Cas9 transfection limits Cas9 expression duration and reduces off-target indels, while permitting co-delivery of HDR templates for precise knock-in of disease alleles in mobilized progenitor cells.

Base editing via lipid nanoparticle delivery enables single-nucleotide correction at the JAK2V617F locus without double-strand breaks, minimizing p53-dependent apoptosis in CD34+ cells during ex vivo manipulation.
quizzes.explanation: RNP electroporation achieves 40–80% editing efficiency while avoiding extended ex vivo culture that degrades HSC long-term repopulating activity, a critical limitation of lentiviral Cas9 approaches. It further supports multiplex targeting of DCAF7, IFIT3, and JAK2V617F in the same cell population, enabling combinatorial genetic dissection with functional NSG engraftment as the readout.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: You plan to map the DCAF7 interactome in JAK2V617F MPN hematopoietic stem cells by transducing HSPCs with a proximity-labeling fusion construct and transplanting them into irradiated recipient mice. Which feature of TurboID makes it preferable to BioID for this in vivo experimental design?
choices: A. TurboID completes proximity labeling within 10–30 minutes of biotin addition, permitting a brief in vivo pulse after MPN is established in transplanted mice, whereas BioID's 18–24 hour labeling window is incompatible with this timeline.
B. TurboID is a smaller protein tag than BioID, allowing the DCAF7 fusion to transit nuclear pore complexes more efficiently and improving recovery of nuclear-resident binding partners.
C. TurboID exhibits higher substrate selectivity than BioID, substantially reducing non-specific labeling of abundant cytoplasmic chaperones that confound interactome identification in HSCs.
D. TurboID requires a lower concentration of exogenous biotin than BioID, preventing competitive inhibition of endogenous biotin-dependent carboxylases critical for HSC metabolic homeostasis.
quizzes.explanation: BioID's 18–24 hour labeling requirement is incompatible with controlled in vivo biotin administration after HSPC engraftment; TurboID's 10–30 minute kinetics allow a brief biotin pulse once JAK2V617F MPN is established in the recipient mouse (Branon 2018). The accelerated labeling window—not fusion-protein size, substrate selectivity, or biotin dosage—is the defining advantage for this transplantation design.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: In a K-εGG diGly ubiquitin-remnant proteomics experiment, NEDD8 conjugation also produces a diGly tag after trypsin digestion. Which experimental strategy most directly distinguishes NEDD8-modified lysines from true ubiquitination sites in the dataset?
choices: Pre-treat cells with MG132 to block proteasomal degradation; NEDD8-modified proteins accumulate at higher steady-state levels than ubiquitinated substrates, enabling separation by relative abundance.
Perform a parallel His-tagged ubiquitin pulldown; diGly sites absent from the His-Ub eluate but present in the K-εGG dataset are classified as NEDD8-modified.
Treat cells with pevonedistat to inhibit the NEDD8-activating enzyme (NAE1); diGly sites that disappear from the dataset under this condition are NEDD8-modified rather than ubiquitinated.
Stimulate cells with IFN-α before harvest; NEDD8-modified sites are identified by their insensitivity to the interferon-driven increase in diGly signal.
quizzes.explanation: Pevonedistat (MLN4924) selectively inhibits NAE1, blocking all neddylation without perturbing ubiquitin or ISG15 conjugation; diGly sites that disappear in pevonedistat-treated cells are therefore NEDD8-derived, not ubiquitinated. ISG15 sites are separately discriminated by their induction upon IFN treatment — a distinct orthogonal control — not by NAE inhibition.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: A colleague proposes replacing the conditional Dcaf7^F/F^ Mx1-Cre model with a dTAG-DCAF7 knock-in system. Which property of dTAG-mediated degradation most directly addresses limitations inherent to the Cre-inducible approach in the context of IFN-pathway studies?
choices: Graded, dose-dependent protein depletion by titrating dTAG-13 concentration, enabling partial loss-of-function studies not achievable with binary Cre recombination
Acute, reversible protein depletion within hours, allowing on/off experiments and eliminating poly(I:C)-induced interferon confounds inherent to Mx1-Cre induction
Cell-intrinsic protein loss confined to the transplanted clone without requiring irradiation-based bone marrow chimerism to generate experimental animals
Enhanced on-target specificity because dTAG-13 acts exclusively on FKBP12^F36V-tagged DCAF7, preventing off-target recombination at cryptic loxP sites elsewhere in the genome
quizzes.explanation: dTAG degrades the tagged protein within hours (versus days-to-weeks for Cre-mediated deletion), enabling immediate versus adaptive DCAF7-loss phenotypes to be distinguished and on/off/on experimental designs via ligand washout; critically, because no poly(I:C) is required, IFN-pathway induction intrinsic to Mx1-Cre systems is eliminated, removing a major confound in a study of DCAF7→IFIT3→IFN-α signaling.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.D.3 · IFIT2 is pro-apoptotic and restrained by IFIT3 — Stawowczyk
quizzes.stem: Stawowczyk et al. 2011 expressed IFIT2 independently of IFN stimulation in HEK293 and HeLa cells and observed caspase-3 cleavage and cell death. Which of the following results most accurately describes the mechanistic conclusions of that study?
choices: A. IFIT2-induced apoptosis required Bax/Bak and was selectively suppressed by IFIT3 co-expression but not by IFIT1 co-expression.
B. IFIT2-induced apoptosis required caspase-8 activation and was selectively suppressed by IFIT3 co-expression but not by IFIT1 co-expression.
C. IFIT2-induced apoptosis required Bax/Bak and was selectively suppressed by IFIT1 co-expression but not by IFIT3 co-expression.
D. IFIT3 expression alone triggered Bax/Bak-dependent apoptosis, and IFIT2 co-expression rescued cell viability by sequestering IFIT3 away from mitochondria.
quizzes.explanation: IFIT2 expression alone activated the intrinsic (mitochondrial) apoptotic pathway, demonstrated by rescue with Bcl-xL and E1B-19K and complete loss of death in Bax−/−/Bak−/− MEFs; critically, co-expression of IFIT3 — but not IFIT1, the other confirmed IFIT2 binding partner — suppressed this cell death, defining IFIT3 as a selective gatekeeper of IFIT2's pro-apoptotic function rather than a passive scaffolding component.
quizzes.citation: Stawowczyk M, Van Scoy S, Kumar P, Reich NC. The interferon stimulated gene 54 promotes apoptosis. J Biol Chem. 2011;286(12):10983–92. PMID: 21190939

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · §5 — Experimental Methodology: Deep Gap Analysis
quizzes.stem: You want to verify efficient Cre-mediated recombination at single-cell resolution in LT-HSCs of your Mx1-Cre × Dcaf7^F/F^ model and distinguish a genuine Dcaf7-null HSC phenotype from expansion of non-recombined escaper cells. Which strategy best accomplishes both goals?
choices: Sort CD150+CD48−LSK cells and perform droplet-based scRNA-seq; identify non-recombined escapers by detecting reads spanning the floxed Dcaf7 exon, then compare transcriptomes of recombined versus escaper populations across timepoints.

Cross in a Rosa26-EYFP (or mT/mG) reporter allele and quantify the YFP+ (or mG+) fraction within CD150+CD48−LSK cells by flow cytometry at serial timepoints; a declining recombined fraction implicates escaper outgrowth, whereas a stable fraction supports a genuine Dcaf7-null phenotype.

Isolate bulk LSK cells at serial timepoints and measure the ratio of floxed-to-deleted Dcaf7 alleles by allele-specific qPCR; a rising floxed/deleted ratio over time is interpreted as evidence of preferential escaper expansion.

Perform single-molecule FISH for Dcaf7 mRNA on bone marrow cryosections at serial timepoints; absence of puncta in CD150+ cells confirms recombination, while persistent signal identifies non-recombined escapers.
quizzes.explanation: A Rosa26-EYFP or mT/mG reporter permanently and heritably marks every cell that has undergone Cre-mediated recombination, enabling flow-cytometric single-cell quantification of the YFP+/mG+ fraction within immunophenotypically defined LT-HSCs (CD150+CD48−LSK); tracking this fraction at serial timepoints directly tests for escaper outgrowth — a declining recombined proportion indicates a fitness cost confounding phenotypic interpretation, while a stable proportion argues the observed HSC phenotype reflects genuine Dcaf7 loss.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.D.2 · IFIT3 knockdown sensitizes dengue-infected lung epithelium t
quizzes.stem: Hsu et al. 2013 knocked down IFIT3 in dengue virus–infected A549 human lung epithelial cells and quantified cell death and viral replication. What was the primary functional conclusion supported by their data?
choices: IFIT3 is anti-apoptotic and antiviral in dengue-infected lung epithelium: its depletion increased caspase-3/8/9 cleavage, BAX upregulation, and annexin V positivity while simultaneously elevating viral titers by plaque assay.

IFIT3 is pro-apoptotic in dengue-infected lung epithelium: its depletion reduced TUNEL signal and caspase activation, indicating that IFIT3 drives extrinsic apoptosis downstream of dengue pattern recognition.

IFIT3 is functionally redundant with IFIT1 in antiviral defense: knockdown of IFIT3 alone had no effect on apoptosis or viral titers because IFIT1 maintained cap-0 mRNA translational suppression in its absence.

IFIT3 restrains pyroptosis rather than apoptosis in dengue-infected epithelium: its depletion shifted cell death from NLRP3-dependent IL-1β release toward caspase-3–mediated apoptosis without altering viral titers.
quizzes.explanation: Two independent IFIT3 siRNAs increased TUNEL signal, annexin V/PI staining, and cleavage of caspases-3, -8, and -9 (with concordant BAX upregulation) in DV-infected A549 cells, while plaque assays simultaneously revealed higher viral titers — demonstrating that IFIT3 both restrains caspase cascades and suppresses viral replication in dengue-infected lung epithelium. This anti-apoptotic, pro-survival role is the context-dependent function a committee will ask you to reconcile with IFIT3's antiproliferative output in JAK2V617F MPN cells.
quizzes.citation: Hsu et al. (2013) PLoS One 8(11):e79518. PMID 24223959.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.D.4 · Vaccinia C9 ankyrin-repeat/F-box protein targets IFIT1/2/3 f
quizzes.stem: Liu et al. 2019 established that vaccinia virus C9 counteracts the type I IFN response by directing proteasomal degradation of IFIT1/2/3 in human cells. Which domain architecture allows C9 to physically bridge IFIT substrates to the ubiquitin–proteasome system?
choices: N-terminal ankyrin repeats bind IFIT1/2/3 directly; a C-terminal F-box recruits SKP1–CUL1–RBX1 to ubiquitinate the complex.
N-terminal PRYSPRY domain recognizes ISG15-conjugated IFITs; a C-terminal RING domain catalyzes direct ubiquitin transfer to IFIT substrate lysines.
N-terminal ankyrin repeats bind IFIT1/2/3; a C-terminal BTB domain recruits CUL3–RBX1, mirroring SPOP-mediated degradation of interferon-stimulated proteins.
N-terminal WD40 β-propeller engages IFIT1 as the primary degron; a C-terminal F-box bridges SKP1–CUL1, with IFIT2/3 cleared as orphaned subunits after IFIT1 removal.
quizzes.explanation: C9 contains two separable functional modules: N-terminal ankyrin repeats that directly contact IFIT1, IFIT2, and IFIT3, and a C-terminal F-box that engages SKP1 to assemble an SCF (SKP1–CUL1–RBX1) E3 ligase; proteasome inhibition with MG132 fully stabilized all three IFITs in C9-expressing cells, and genetic rescue of an IFIT-KO line confirmed IFITs are the epistatic targets. The BTB–CUL3 (choice C) and WD40-based CRL4 (choice D) platforms represent real cullin-RING architectures used by cellular substrate receptors but were not recovered as C9-associated complexes in the AP-MS data reported in this study.
quizzes.citation: Liu et al. 2019, Cell Reports; PMID 31644906; doi:10.1016/j.celrep.2019.09.039

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S1.D.5 · IFIT1/IFIT3 drive GSDME-dependent pyroptosis in myeloma and
quizzes.stem: He et al. (2024) identified clioquinol (CLQ) as a pyroptosis inducer in multiple myeloma and leukemia cells and demonstrated that IFIT1 and IFIT3 are required for this response. Co-immunoprecipitation experiments revealed which mechanistic basis for IFIT1/IFIT3-driven GSDME cleavage?
choices: A. IFIT1 and IFIT3 form a complex with BAX and N-terminal GSDME, directing N-GSDME to mitochondria and increasing mitochondrial membrane permeabilization
B. IFIT1 and IFIT3 scaffold MAVS on the outer mitochondrial membrane, recruiting IRF3 to sustain a caspase-independent IFN-β feedforward loop that amplifies pro-death signaling
C. IFIT1 and IFIT3 sequester eIF3 subunits to suppress cap-dependent translation of anti-apoptotic Bcl-2 family proteins, lowering the apoptotic threshold
D. IFIT1 and IFIT3 promote NLRP3 inflammasome assembly upstream of caspase-1, generating GSDMD-mediated pyroptotic pores independent of the mitochondrial pathway
quizzes.explanation: He et al. showed by co-IP that IFIT1 and IFIT3 physically associate with BAX and the N-terminal GSDME fragment (N-GSDME), directing N-GSDME to mitochondria where it increases membrane permeabilization — a caspase-3/GSDME axis mechanistically distinct from canonical NLRP3/caspase-1/GSDMD pyroptosis. This mitochondrial scaffolding function accounts for why venetoclax (a BAX activator) synergized strongly with CLQ through the same IFIT1/IFIT3–GSDME axis.
quizzes.citation: He et al., Cancer Letters 2024 (PMID 38462032; DOI: 10.1016/j.canlet.2024.216797)

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.A.3 · Wu et al. 2025 — constitutive WDR68/DCAF7 knockout causes in
quizzes.stem: Wu et al. 2025 performed bulk whole-embryo RNA-seq comparing WDR68^−/− with wild-type littermates. Which signaling pathway was most notably activated in the knockouts, suggesting that DCAF7 loss engages a metabolic stress program during mammalian development?
choices: A. HIF-1 (hypoxia-inducible factor) pathway, linking DCAF7 loss to hypoxic/metabolic stress
B. Type I interferon / JAK-STAT pathway, reflecting innate immune derepression in null embryos
C. Wnt/β-catenin pathway, consistent with AUTS2's established role in Wnt-regulated transcription
D. p53-mediated DNA-damage response pathway, as observed in other CRL4-substrate-receptor knockouts
quizzes.explanation: Transcriptomic profiling of WDR68^−/− embryos identified upregulation of HIF-1 target genes, implicating hypoxic/metabolic stress as the primary downstream consequence of DCAF7 loss and providing a mechanistic basis for the observed intrauterine growth retardation and embryonic lethality. The remaining distractors draw on adjacent biology — JAK-STAT from the DCAF7→IFIT3→IFN-α axis in JAK2 MPN, Wnt/β-catenin from AUTS2's known chromatin functions, and p53 from CRL4-DDB2 and related damage-repair complexes — but none were identified as the primary deregulated pathway in this dataset.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.B.2 · Trilling et al. 2011 — DDB1–CUL4 is recruited by cytomegalov
quizzes.stem: In Trilling et al. 2011, which set of findings most directly demonstrated that MCMV pM27 suppresses type I interferon signaling by hijacking host CRL4 machinery to degrade STAT2?
