choices: A. pM27 truncation mutants that lost DDB1 binding also failed to degrade STAT2, and DDB1 siRNA knockdown both rescued STAT2 protein levels and severely impaired MCMV replication in IFN-conditioned cells — phenocopying M27-deficient virus.
B. pM27 co-immunoprecipitated with CUL4B and reduced STAT1 phosphorylation at Y701, impairing IRF9–STAT1 heterodimerization in IFN-β–treated NIH-3T3 cells.
C. siRNA depletion of CUL4A phenocopied M27-deficient viral fitness, while pM27 truncation mutants that retained DDB1 binding degraded both STAT1 and STAT2 with equal efficiency.

D. Pharmacologic TYK2 inhibition rescued STAT2 protein half-life in pM27-expressing cells, indicating that pM27 promotes phosphorylation-dependent STAT2 turnover rather than direct ubiquitin-ligase recruitment.
quizzes.explanation: The paper's key evidence was a strict structure–function correlation: pM27 truncation mutants unable to bind DDB1 lost the ability to degrade STAT2, establishing DDB1 recruitment as mechanistically required. DDB1 siRNA knockdown then provided independent confirmation — rescuing STAT2 levels and collapsing viral fitness in IFN-conditioned cells, phenocopying M27-null MCMV — while STAT1 remained unaffected, showing substrate selectivity.
quizzes.citation: Trilling M, et al. Mouse cytomegalovirus pM27 antagonizes IFN-γ signalling by degrading STAT2. PLoS Pathog. 2011;7(6):e1002069. PMID: 21698215.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.A.1 · Peng et al. 2016 — DCAF7 targets DNA ligase I for CRL4-media
quizzes.stem: Peng et al. (2016) elevated DCAF7 from a catalogued 'putative DCAF' to a functionally validated CRL4 substrate receptor for DNA ligase I. Which single experimental result most directly established that designation?
choices: Purified recombinant CUL4A–DDB1–RBX1 reconstituted LIG1 polyubiquitylation only when DCAF7 was included in the reaction, and mutation of the three diGly-mapped lysines (K7, K17, K124) substantially reduced ubiquitin incorporation relative to wild-type LIG1.

Recombinant CRL4^DCAF2 (CUL4A–DDB1–DCAF2/CDT2) ubiquitylated LIG1 at K7/K17/K124 in a PCNA-PIP-box–dependent manner, with DCAF7 serving as an accessory co-activator rather than the primary substrate-docking subunit.

FLAG-DCAF7 pulldowns co-immunoprecipitated DDB1 and CUL4A in proliferating HEK293T cells, but LIG1 association required prior CK2-mediated phosphorylation of the LIG1 N-terminal regulatory domain, establishing a phospho-degron analogous to CRL4^CDT2 targeting of CDT1.

DCAF7 siRNA knockdown in serum-starved HCT116 cells stabilized both LIG1 and FEN-1 to equal extents, indicating that CRL4^DCAF7 degrades the Okazaki fragment maturation complex as a unit rather than targeting a single client selectively.
quizzes.explanation: The in vitro reconstitution is the biochemical cornerstone of the paper: DCAF7 alone, within the minimal CUL4A–DDB1–RBX1 scaffold, is both necessary and sufficient to recruit LIG1 and direct its polyubiquitylation, satisfying the defining criterion of a bona fide substrate receptor. The parallel finding that FEN-1 turnover was DCAF7-independent under identical conditions rules out promiscuous scaffold activity and establishes client selectivity.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.A.2 · Lear et al. 2025 — small-molecule DCAF7 inhibitors (BC18630/
quizzes.stem: In Lear et al. 2025, which experimental result most directly established DCAF7 as a bona fide CRL4 substrate receptor for TFEB, rather than a coincidental co-precipitating interactor?
choices: A. Cell-free reconstitution with recombinant CUL4A, DDB1, DCAF7, E1, UbcH5 E2, and ubiquitin produced DCAF7-dependent polyubiquitylation of in vitro-translated TFEB under defined conditions.

B. A siRNA kinome screen (~600 kinases) with high-content nuclear-TFEB imaging identified PAK2 as the CRL4 adaptor subunit that directly recognizes phosphorylated TFEB, with DCAF7 functioning as the upstream priming kinase.

C. Cycloheximide chase in PAK2-knockout BEAS-2B cells produced TFEB stabilization equivalent to that observed in DCAF7-null cells, ordering PAK2 and DCAF7 as redundant substrate-recognition subunits in a shared CRL4 complex.

D. AlphaScreen competition assays demonstrated that BC18630 disrupts the DDB1–CUL4A scaffold interface rather than the DCAF7–TFEB protein–protein interaction, stabilizing TFEB by preventing CRL4 complex assembly.
quizzes.explanation: The in vitro ubiquitylation reconstitution — using only defined recombinant components (CUL4A/NEDD8/RBX1, DDB1, DCAF7, E1, UbcH5, ubiquitin) with in vitro-translated TFEB as substrate — is the definitive biochemical proof of enzymatic sufficiency: DCAF7 provides substrate specificity within the minimal CRL4 machinery, ruling out all other cellular cofactors. This was corroborated by CHX-chase stabilization of TFEB specifically in DCAF7-knockout (not PAK2-KO) BEAS-2B cells, confirming the substrate-receptor relationship in intact cells.
quizzes.citation: Lear et al. 2025, Sci. Adv. DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.adv4033 (PMID: 40465712)

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.B.1 · Zemke & Berk 2017 — a DCAF7–FOXK–CtBP complex assembled by a
quizzes.stem: In Zemke & Berk 2017 (Cell Host & Microbe), adenovirus E1A suppresses a delayed wave of ISG induction (~12 h post-infection) in primary human airway epithelial cells by co-opting DCAF7, FOXK, and CtBP through its C-terminus. E1A point mutants unable to engage all three partners de-repressed ISGs including IFIT2 and ISG15, with IRF3 identified as the required transcription factor. What was the primary mechanism by which disruption of the DCAF7–FOXK–CtBP complex de-repressed ISG expression?
choices: A. IRF3 protein accumulated ~10-fold without a corresponding change in IRF3 mRNA, indicating the complex normally targets IRF3 for post-translational destabilization rather than transcriptional silencing.
B. TBK1-mediated phosphorylation of IRF3 at Ser396 increased upon complex disruption, driving IRF3 dimerization and nuclear translocation without a detectable change in total IRF3 protein abundance.
C. Autocrine IFN-β secretion rose upon complex disruption, amplifying JAK–STAT signaling and enabling ISGF3 (STAT1–STAT2–IRF9) assembly at ISG promoters in a feedforward loop.
D. IRF3 mRNA was transcriptionally upregulated through a STING-dependent feedforward mechanism, with IRF3 protein accumulation as a secondary consequence of increased translation.
quizzes.explanation: Zemke & Berk demonstrated by quantitative western blot that IRF3 protein rose ~10-fold in E1A triple-binding-mutant cells while IRF3 mRNA was unchanged, establishing post-translational stabilization—not transcriptional induction—as the mechanism. siRNA knockdown of IRF3 (but not IRF9) fully abrogated ISG activation, confirming IRF3 protein stability as the rate-limiting node through which the DCAF7–FOXK–CtBP complex restrains the IFN response.
quizzes.citation: Zemke & Berk, Cell Host & Microbe 22(6):782–792, 2017. PMID 29241042. DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2017.11.008

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.C.1 · Miyata & Nishida 2011 — DYRK1A binds WDR68 and drives its nu
quizzes.stem: Miyata & Nishida (2011) showed that co-expression of DYRK1A induces robust nuclear accumulation of WDR68 (DCAF7) in mammalian cells. Which of the following best describes the mechanism responsible for this relocalization?
choices: A. Physical binding to the N-terminal domain of DYRK1A is sufficient; a catalytically inactive DYRK1A mutant drives nuclear accumulation as effectively as wild-type
B. DYRK1A phosphorylates residues within the WD40 propeller core of WDR68, unmasking a cryptic nuclear localization signal
C. DDB1 competes with DYRK1A for the WD40 propeller surface of WDR68, releasing a cytoplasmic retention signal and permitting nuclear entry
D. DYRK1B, which also binds WDR68, is the principal driver of nuclear translocation; DYRK1A binding is necessary but not sufficient without DYRK1B co-expression
quizzes.explanation: Truncation mapping localized the WDR68–DYRK1A interaction to DYRK1A's N-terminal domain, with the catalytic and C-terminal regions dispensable for binding. Critically, a kinase-dead DYRK1A mutant induced WDR68 nuclear accumulation as robustly as wild-type, demonstrating that physical association — not phosphorylation — is the mechanistic driver of relocalization.
quizzes.citation: Miyata & Nishida 2011, Biochim Biophys Acta Mol Cell Res, PMID 21777625; Figs. 2–4 (co-IP truncation mapping, kinase-dead immunofluorescence)

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.D.2 · Jin et al. 2006 — the DCAF family and CDT2 as the CRL4 recep
quizzes.stem: Jin et al. (2006) established that CRL4^CDT2 destroys Cdt1 specifically during S phase rather than constitutively. Which mechanistic feature best explains this cell-cycle restriction?
choices: A. CDT2 is recruited to active replication forks through a PCNA–Cdt1 ternary interaction, confining Cdt1 ubiquitylation to sites of ongoing DNA synthesis
B. CDT2 protein levels are transcriptionally induced by E2F at the G1/S boundary, making it available only after cells commit to replication
C. Cyclin E–CDK2 phosphorylates a degron on Cdt1 after origin firing, converting it into a neo-substrate recognized by the CDT2 WD40 surface
D. DDB2 competes with CDT2 for DDB1 binding in G1, sequestering DDB1 in an NER-dedicated complex until S-phase entry displaces it
quizzes.explanation: Jin et al. showed that CDT2 localization to replication forks depends on a PCNA–Cdt1 interaction, making Cdt1 ubiquitylation strictly coupled to active DNA synthesis rather than to changes in CDT2 abundance or phosphorylation state. This fork-dependent recruitment established the proof-of-principle that a DCAF can act as a conditional substrate receptor, recognizing its client only within a defined cellular compartment.
quizzes.citation: Jin J, Arias EE, Chen J, Harper JW, Walter JC. A family of diverse Cul4-Ddb1-interacting proteins includes Cdt2, which is required for S phase destruction of the replication factor Cdt1. Mol Cell. 2006;23(6):859-869. PMID 16949367. DOI: 10.1016/j.molcel.2006.08.010

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.D.3 · Soucy et al. 2009 — MLN4924 (pevonedistat) inhibits NEDD8-ac
quizzes.stem: Soucy et al. (2009) established MLN4924 (pevonedistat) as a pharmacologic tool for collapsing CRL-dependent substrate turnover. Which statement best describes the molecular mechanism by which MLN4924 inactivates CRL complexes?
choices: A. MLN4924 forms a covalent NEDD8-adenylate adduct within the active site of NAE1, blocking NEDD8 transfer onto cullins and thereby inactivating the entire CRL superfamily.
B. MLN4924 inhibits the COP9 signalosome (CSN), trapping cullins in a hyper-neddylated state that prevents substrate-receptor exchange and renders CRL complexes catalytically incompetent.
C. MLN4924 acts as a competitive ATP mimetic that directly inhibits the RING-domain E2-docking surface of RBX1, selectively blocking ubiquitin transfer within assembled CRL4 complexes.
D. MLN4924 suppresses UBA1-mediated ubiquitin activation, globally reducing ubiquitin flux and secondarily depleting free ubiquitin available to CRL-associated E2 enzymes.
quizzes.explanation: MLN4924 is a mechanism-based inhibitor that reacts with NEDD8 inside the NAE1 active site to form a tight-binding NEDD8-MLN4924 adduct, preventing NEDD8 from being charged onto UBC12 and subsequently conjugated to the cullin scaffold; without cullin neddylation, all CRL complexes lose the conformational activation required for substrate ubiquitination. This global CRL collapse is evidenced by concomitant stabilization of multiple unrelated CRL substrates (p27, Cdt1, ORC1) across distinct CRL complexes, re-replication, and apoptosis.
quizzes.citation: Soucy TA et al. An inhibitor of NEDD8-activating enzyme as a new approach to treat cancer. Nature. 2009;458(7239):732-6. PMID: 19360080.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.D.1 · Angers et al. 2006 — molecular architecture of DDB1–CUL4A an
quizzes.stem: Angers et al. 2006 solved the crystal structure of a CUL4A–DDB1–ROC1 complex and defined the DCAF/DWD protein family by tandem-affinity purification. Which structural feature of DDB1 was identified as the docking platform for interchangeable DCAF substrate-specificity factors?
choices: A. A double-β-propeller platform of DDB1 that is structurally distinct from the single β-propeller that anchors DDB1 to CUL4A
B. The single β-propeller of DDB1 shared with the CUL4A-anchoring interface, allowing competitive displacement of the scaffold by incoming substrate receptors
C. The ROC1/RBX1 RING domain, which directly contacts WD40-repeat proteins to position substrates near the E2 ubiquitin-conjugating enzyme
D. A conserved cullin-homology domain on CUL4A that recruits WD40-repeat proteins through a cullin-box–dependent mechanism analogous to F-box recruitment in CRL1
quizzes.explanation: Angers et al. showed that DDB1 is structurally bipartite: one β-propeller clamps CUL4A while a separate double-β-propeller platform serves as the variable module to which DCAF/DWD proteins dock via conserved WD40 sequence features. This spatial separation means the substrate receptor can be swapped without disrupting the cullin-adaptor interface, establishing the exchangeable-DCAF grammar that governs all CRL4 substrate recognition.
quizzes.citation: Angers S, Li T, Yi X, MacCoss MJ, Moon RT, Zheng N. Molecular architecture and assembly of the DDB1–CUL4A ubiquitin ligase machinery. Nature. 2006;443(7111):590–593. PMID: 16964240

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.C.2 · Ritterhoff et al. 2010 — WDR68/Han11 is a scaffold coupling
quizzes.stem: Ritterhoff et al. 2010 showed that both RNAi-mediated depletion and overexpression of Han11 (DCAF7/WDR68) impaired HIPK2- and MEKK1-driven transcriptional responses in human cells. Which mechanistic model best accounts for this bidirectional loss-of-function phenotype?
choices: A. Han11 acts as a stoichiometric scaffold that physically bridges MEKK1 to DYRK1 and HIPK2; limiting Han11 prevents productive ternary complex assembly, while excess Han11 sequesters client kinases in unproductive binary sub-complexes.

B. Han11 competes with CRL4–DDB1 for the substrate-recognition surface on HIPK2; depletion accelerates HIPK2 ubiquitylation and turnover, while excess Han11 locks HIPK2 in a DDB1-excluded, transcriptionally inactive complex.

C. Han11 serves as a co-chaperone that stabilizes DYRK1A and HIPK2 in signaling-competent conformations; depletion leads to kinase misfolding and aggregation, while excess Han11 drives premature dimerization that occludes substrate-binding clefts.

D. Han11 couples MEKK1 to the MKK3/MKK6–p38 MAPK axis; depletion disconnects MEKK1 from p38 induction, while overexpression saturates the MKK3/MKK6 intermediaries and attenuates downstream transcription factor activation.
quizzes.explanation: A stoichiometric scaffold must be present at defined molar ratios relative to its clients: too little scaffold prevents ternary complex assembly, while excess free scaffold titrates client kinases away from productive complexes — a mass-action hallmark that distinguishes scaffolds from simple adaptors or co-activators. Ritterhoff et al. explicitly invoked this stoichiometric model to explain why Han11 overexpression phenocopies knockdown for both HIPK2-driven DNA-damage responses and MEKK1-driven stress responses.
quizzes.citation: Ritterhoff S, Schmitz ML. (2010) Han11/DCAF7 scaffolds HIPK2 and MEKK1 for stress-responsive signaling. EMBO J 29:4112–4124. PMID: 20940704.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.F.1 · Słabicki et al. 2020 — CR8 converts a kinase inhibitor into
quizzes.stem: Słabicki et al. 2020 identified CR8 as a molecular-glue degrader of cyclin K through an integrated pharmacogenomic and structural approach. What mechanistic feature distinguishes how CR8 engages the CRL4 machinery from the canonical molecular-glue paradigm established by IMiDs?
choices: A. CR8's solvent-exposed pyridyl moiety contacts DDB1 directly, bypassing any DCAF substrate receptor, and presents the CDK12–cyclin K complex to CUL4 for ubiquitylation.
B. CR8 recruits a previously uncharacterized DCAF substrate receptor that bridges the CDK12–cyclin K complex to the CUL4B–DDB1 scaffold for degradation.
C. CR8 co-opts DCAF15 — analogous to aryl-sulfonamide degraders of RBM39 — to ubiquitylate cyclin K within an assembled CDK12 complex.
D. CR8 repurposes the CRBN thalidomide-binding pocket, inducing a neosubstrate interaction with cyclin K in a manner analogous to lenalidomide-driven IKZF1 degradation.
quizzes.explanation: Structural analysis showed that CDK-bound CR8 presents a solvent-exposed pyridyl group that contacts DDB1 directly, routing the CDK12–cyclin K complex to CUL4 for ubiquitylation without any intervening DCAF. This receptor-bypass mechanism expanded the molecular-glue concept by demonstrating that CRL4's DDB1 surface can accept induced substrates independent of a canonical substrate receptor.
quizzes.citation: Słabicki M et al. 'The CDK inhibitor CR8 acts as a molecular glue degrader that depletes cyclin K.' Nature. 2020;585(7824):293–297. PMID: 32494016.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.E.2 · Petzold, Fischer & Thomä 2016 — lenalidomide reshapes the CR
quizzes.stem: The 2.45 Å crystal structure of the DDB1–CRBN–lenalidomide–CK1α quaternary complex revealed the mechanistic basis for lenalidomide's molecular glue activity. Which statement best describes what the structure demonstrated about how lenalidomide recruits CK1α to CRBN?
choices: A. Lenalidomide creates a composite DDB1–CRBN surface that engages a β-hairpin loop in the CK1α N-lobe, enabling CK1α recruitment despite no measurable intrinsic CRBN–CK1α affinity in the absence of drug.

B. Lenalidomide inserts between a zinc-finger degron motif in CK1α and the CRBN β-propeller, stabilizing an otherwise weak but pre-existing CRBN–CK1α interaction in a manner analogous to IKZF1 recruitment.

C. Lenalidomide binds the cullin-associating domain of CRBN and allosterically repositions the substrate-binding β-propeller to increase catalytic throughput for ubiquitin transfer onto pre-bound neosubstrates.
D. Lenalidomide exploits a CK1α-specific hydrophobic groove on CRBN that cannot accommodate structurally unrelated proteins, restricting neosubstrate recruitment to the kinase superfamily.
quizzes.explanation: Petzold et al. demonstrated that CRBN and CK1α have no measurable affinity for one another in the absence of drug; lenalidomide creates a composite surface that directly contacts a β-hairpin loop in CK1α's N-lobe, making the interaction entirely drug-dependent. Critically, IKZF1 engages the same composite CRBN surface through a structurally related (yet sequence-unrelated) motif, establishing that a single molecular glue can recruit multiple substrates by presenting a shared structural handle rather than a substrate-unique binding site.
quizzes.citation: Petzold CJ, Fischer ES, Thomä NH. Structure of a CRBN–DDB1–lenalidomide–CK1α complex reveals basis for rational design of pharmacological glues. Nature. 2016;532(7597):29–34. PMID 26909574.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.E.1 · Fischer et al. 2014 — crystal structure of DDB1–CRBN in comp
quizzes.stem: Fischer et al. 2014 solved crystal structures of human DDB1–CRBN bound to each clinical IMiD and performed an unbiased endogenous substrate screen. Which statement best describes the paper's central mechanistic conclusion about how IMiDs act on CRL4^CRBN?
choices: A. IMiDs occupy a defined glutarimide-binding pocket on CRBN and act as molecular glues that simultaneously displace the endogenous substrate MEIS2 and recruit IKZF1/IKZF3, providing a dual-activity model that accounts for both on-target and neomorphic degradation.

B. IMiDs competitively occupy the CRBN thalidomide-binding pocket to displace the endogenous substrate AMPK-β1, redirecting CRL4^CRBN toward IKZF1/IKZF3 in a manner that is strictly dependent on the pomalidomide amino group.

C. The glutarimide moiety of IMiDs structurally mimics the β-hairpin degron of GSPT1, enabling CRBN to recruit neosubstrates through peptide mimicry rather than creation of a composite protein–protein glue interface.

D. IMiDs allosterically destabilize the DDB1–CRBN interface, releasing CRBN from the CRL4 scaffold so it functions as a monomeric E3 ligase that ubiquitinates IKZF1 and IKZF3 without displacing any endogenous CRL4^CRBN substrate.
quizzes.explanation: The Fischer structures revealed that IMiDs bind a pre-formed hydrophobic pocket on CRBN via the phthaloyl-glutarimide pharmacophore and create a composite surface that neomorphically recruits IKZF1/IKZF3; the unbiased screen further identified MEIS2 as the natural CRL4^CRBN substrate, and showed IMiDs evict it while installing the neosubstrates — a dual-activity model that unified the therapeutic mechanism in myeloma with the del(5q) MDS activity.
quizzes.citation: Fischer ES et al. Structure of the DDB1–CRBN E3 ubiquitin ligase in complex with thalidomide. Nature. 2014;512(7512):49-53. PMID: 25043012.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.E.3 · Matyskiela et al. 2018 — SALL4 is the CRBN neosubstrate that
quizzes.stem: Matyskiela et al. (2018) used humanized-CRBN transgenic mice, rabbit embryo assays, and degradation proteomics to explain why thalidomide causes limb defects in humans and rabbits but not in mice. Which finding best explains the species specificity of thalidomide teratogenicity?
choices: A. Mouse SALL4 carries sequence variation in its CRBN-binding degron that prevents thalidomide-induced CRL4^CRBN recruitment, whereas human and rabbit SALL4 remain susceptible to degradation.
B. Mouse CRBN contains amino acid substitutions near the thalidomide-binding pocket that reduce drug binding affinity, shielding mouse embryos regardless of SALL4 sequence.
C. IKZF1 and IKZF3, the canonical CRL4^CRBN IMiD neosubstrates, are absent from mouse limb-bud mesenchyme during the critical embryonic window, preventing their thalidomide-induced degradation.
D. Thalidomide is rapidly converted to non-teratogenic hydrolysis products by mouse embryonic CYP enzymes before it can recruit SALL4 to CRL4^CRBN in limb-bud progenitors.
quizzes.explanation: The paper mapped species specificity to sequence variation within SALL4 itself — mouse SALL4 is intrinsically resistant to thalidomide-induced CRBN recruitment, whereas human and rabbit SALL4 are susceptible. This locates the teratogenic gate in the neosubstrate degron rather than in the E3 ligase or pharmacokinetics, establishing that C2H2 zinc finger sequence divergence can gate CRL4 reprogramming by a small molecule.
quizzes.citation: Matyskiela ME et al. (2018) SALL4 mediates teratogenicity as a thalidomide-dependent cereblon substrate. Nat Chem Biol 14(10):981–987. PMID 30190590.

quizzes.sourceLabel: Supplement · S2.F.2 · Mayor-Ruiz et al. 2020 — rational discovery of molecular-glu
quizzes.stem: Mayor-Ruiz et al. 2020 developed a molecular-glue discovery platform centered on a hyponeddylation-rescue screen. Which statement correctly describes both the mechanistic logic of the MLN4924 selectivity filter AND the key finding regarding the identified hit compounds?
choices: A. MLN4924 globally inactivates CRL-family ligases by blocking cullin neddylation; compounds that lost cytotoxicity under hyponeddylated conditions were identified as CRL-dependent glues, with primary hits shown to degrade cyclin K by recruiting CDK12–cyclin K directly to CRL4B without a dedicated substrate receptor.

B. MLN4924 selectively blocks CRBN neddylation while sparing other CRL4 adaptors; compounds rescued under hyponeddylation were classified as CRBN-independent glues that degrade cyclin K by bridging CDK12 to a dedicated DCAF-family substrate receptor on CRL4B.

C. MLN4924 prevents proteasome loading by globally blocking CRL-mediated substrate ubiquitylation; compounds that maintained cytotoxicity under hyponeddylated conditions were confirmed as CRL-independent glues degrading CDK9 via direct proteasome recruitment.

D. MLN4924 specifically inactivates CRL2–VHL complexes; compounds rescued under hyponeddylated conditions were identified as CRL2-dependent glues that degrade BRD4 by stabilizing a VHL–BRD4 neo-substrate interaction independently of HIF-1α.
quizzes.explanation: MLN4924 inhibits the NEDD8-activating enzyme NAE1, preventing cullin neddylation and thereby inactivating the entire CRL superfamily; differential loss of cytotoxicity under these hyponeddylated conditions is therefore a selective readout for CRL-dependent mechanisms. The defining result was that hit compounds degraded cyclin K by inducing a direct CDK12–cyclin K interaction with CRL4B without any dedicated substrate receptor — establishing a receptor-free neo-substrate mechanism and validating the hyponeddylation-rescue screen as a general paradigm for molecular-glue discovery.
quizzes.citation: Mayor-Ruiz C et al. (2020) Rational discovery of molecular glue degraders via scalable chemical profiling. Nat Chem Biol 16:1073–1081. PMID 32747809.
